title: Bug Journal 2026-03-22

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-21 Daily Overview Completed Work: Synchronized repository status via git and rclone, fixed a sub-process vulnerability in the summary tool, integrated Codex usage tracking into the monthly report, upgraded the ECC plugin to use Opus 4.6 on all proxies, and created detailed documentation. Implementation Methods: Used a git conflict resolution mechanism with remote priority, fixed Windows paths using Path.as_posix(), added the –yes flag for npx sub-process calls, modified monthly_summary.py to summarize codex_token_usage separately, used sed to batch edit YAML files for 28 proxies, created tutorials and contributor documentation. Impact: Established a reliable data synchronization workflow between devices and cloud storage, eliminated suspended sub-process calls that hindered daily summary generation, enabling the monthly report to fully track Codex usage, maximized AI reasoning quality through Opus 4.6 and effortLevel max, and provided clear introductory materials for the ECC plugin system. Configured the development environment, fixed key vulnerabilities in the summary process, integrated Codex support, and upgraded all AI proxies to Opus 4.6
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ ECC Plugin Exploration and Configuration — Explored Everything Claude Code plugin (28 proxies, over 47 skills), upgraded all proxies from sonnet/haiku to opus, changed effortLevel from ‘high’ to ‘max’, created detailed ECC_TUTORIAL.md. ✅ Fixed npx Sub-process Suspension Issues — Discovered that without the –yes flag, npx would cause interactive prompts during sub.process.run(capture_output=True), which blocked the collection of token usage. Added the –yes flag to all three npx calls in daily_summary.py. ✅ Integrated Codex into Monthly Summary Process — Modified monthly_summary.py to separate codex_token_usage from token_usage, created combined_token_usage_summaries for charts, updated format_monthly_markdown to include Codex section, and updated all documentation (README.md, summarize/README.md, AGENTS.md) to mention Codex support. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Rclone Data Synchronization Settings — Created ~/.config/gadget/sync.json configuration file, extracted personal data from gdrive:gadget() (12 directories), fixed Windows path issues in sync.py using Path.as_posix(). ✅ Git Repository Synchronization and Merge Conflict Resolution — Pulled latest changes from origin/main, resolved 13 merge conflicts by accepting the remote version, discarded local changes based on user preferences. ✅ Document Updates (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) — Added missing CLI parameters (–paper, –author_id, –homepage, –deploy) to research/CLAUDE.md; created a root AGENTS.md guide with 395 words containing project structure, commands, and conventions. ✅ PowerShell Execution Policy Diagnosis — Discovered that PowerShell execution policies prevented npx/codex.ps1 wrapper, provided solutions using cmd wrapper and Set-ExecutionPolicy. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Infinite suspension when calling npx @ccusage/codex@latest from Python sub-process Solution: Added the –yes flag to npx calls to skip the ‘Ok to proceed? (y)’ interactive prompt, as subprocess.run(capture_output=True) could not answer this question without stdin.
**Key Insight:**subprocess.run with capture_output=True had no stdin, so any interactive prompts would cause an infinite suspension; always use –yes or -y flags for package management in automated scripts.
2. Daily exports collected Codex token usage, but monthly summaries did not aggregate or display this usage Solution: Added a separate summary path for codex_token_usage in aggregate_token_usage(), created combine_usage_summaries for combined outlines, updated format_monthly_markdown() to display the Codex section, and updated all documentation (README.md, summarize/README.md, AGENTS.md) to mention Codex support.
Key Insight: Adding new data sources to the aggregation process requires updating not only input parsing but also aggregation logic, LLM input purification (removing mechanical fields), and output formatting.
General Issues 3. Windows Path().parent generates backslashes, causing rclone remote path issues (e.g., ‘gdrive:gadget/website\personal’) Solution: Use Path.as_posix() to convert Windows paths to forward slashes for building rclone remote paths.
Key Insight: Even on Windows, rclone remote paths must use forward slashes; path operations require explicit .as_posix() conversion when constructing remote URIs.
4. PowerShell Execution Policy blocks npx and codex command.ps1 wrappers Solution: Recommended immediate use of cmd wrapper (npx.cmd, codex.cmd) as a temporary solution, with Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned for permanent fix.
Key Insight: On Windows, npm installations create .cmd and .ps1 wrappers; when PowerShell execution policies are restricted, .cmd is generally usable while .ps1 requires policy changes.
5. Git merge conflicts occur when pulling from remote after local changes Solution: Use git stash, git pull, then accept the upstream version (–theirs) and discard the stash to resolve remaining conflicts.
Key Insight: After local cleanup, using –theirs to fully accept the remote version is more concise than manually resolving conflicts, especially if local changes are intended to be discarded.
Human vs AI Methods Strategic Level Method of Learning ECC Plugin Role Method Human Users prefer hands-on tutorials with step-by-step documentation explaining expected outcomes AI AI initially attempted to automate exploration and report generation, then shifted to planning mode for structured methods Difference Analysis: Humans prefer self-directed learning with clear checkpoints; AI defaults to automation and delegation to sub-agents. User methods are better suited for muscle memory building and understanding.
ECC Proxy Model Selection Strategy Role Method Human Users want all proxies upgraded to Opus 4.6 for optimal quality, willing to accept higher costs AI AI suggests a hierarchical approach (Opus for core proxies, Sonnet for routine tasks, Haiku for lightweight tasks) to balance cost and quality Difference Analysis: Humans prioritize consistency and maximum capability; AI prioritizes cost optimization. User choices reflect emphasis on quality over cost in this personal project.
Git Conflict Resolution Method Role Method Human Users know they should discard local changes and fully match remote ones, as they have already cleaned up personal data on GitHub AI Initially attempted to merge two versions by popping a stash Difference Analysis: Humans have context regarding recent repository cleanup; AI lacks this context, retaining all work by default. Context is important for destructive operations.
AI Limitations Key Limitation - AI initially did not understand that users wanted a self-guided tutorial. When asked “Help me get familiar with this plugin through my current project,” the AI instead initiated an automated sub-agent program. General Limitations AI suggested a proxy model tier optimized for cost, while users clearly desired high-quality services (using Opus in all cases), but AI did not correctly interpret user preferences. When creating documentation, AI’s initial AGENTS.md file had 402 words (over the target of 400), requiring iterative trimming of content, although this limitation was explicitly stated. Learning Outcomes Key Takeaways Using the subprocess.run method with capture_output=True results in no stdin input—any interactive prompts cause an infinite suspension; in automated processes, always use –yes/–non-interactive flags for package management and CLI tools. When adding new data sources to the aggregation pipeline, updates are required: (1) input collection, (2) aggregation logic, (3) LLM input cleansing (removing new fields from prompts), (4) output rendering—missing any of these steps leads to data loss. Windows path objects generate backslashes; when constructing URIs or remote paths that require forward slashes (e.g., rclone, URLs, Unix paths), Path.as_posix() must be used to avoid cross-platform compatibility issues. Claude Code (ECC) plugin architecture consists of 5 layers forming a closed loop—skills (passive knowledge) → proxies (professional reasoning) → commands (user-facing) → hooks (automated triggers) → continuous learning (feedback capture) → skills. Practical Takeaways On Windows systems, due to PowerShell execution policy restrictions, npm installations have both .ps1 (blocked) and .cmd (runnable) wrappers; .cmd is a general alternative. ECC’s effort levels have 4 tiers: low/medium/high/maximum; ‘maximum’ level includes the latest added features, allowing use of the most tokens (39,199) and deepest reasoning. Dialogue Summary GitHub Parent Directory ✅ Plugin Installation Command 19:19:43.111 | claude_code Users executed local commands to add and install the ’everything-claude-code’ plugin market and plugin, then reloaded the plugin. Aside from command confirmation, there was no AI interaction.
Gadget ✅ Git Synchronization and Rclone Data Pull 02:35:05.863 | claude_code Triggered pull of latest changes from remote, encountered 13 merge conflicts during the process. AI initially tried to retain local changes, but users explicitly stated they wanted full match with remote (remote had already cleaned up personal data). Resolved this issue by using git reset –hard origin/main. Then set up rclone sync.py, fixed Windows path errors (backslashes replaced with forward slashes), and pulled all personal data from gdrive:gadget/: committed path fixes.
✅ Fixed npx codex suspension issues 19:15:43.784 | claude_code Users reported that ’npm exec @ccusage/codex@latest –json –breakdown’ hung indefinitely. AI diagnosed that without the –yes flag, npx would trigger an interactive prompt like “Continue?”, but subprocess.run(capture_output=True) had no stdin input, causing a hang. This issue was resolved by adding –yes to all 3 npx calls (fetch_codex_usage_full and fetch_ccusage_full) in dailySummary.py.
✅ Added Codex support to Monthly Summary 19:09:16.955 | codex Users wanted to include Codex output, and ccusage to track Codex usage. AI found that daily exports already supported Codex, but monthly aggregation did not. Updated monthlySummary.py: removed codex_token_usage from LLM input (just like token_usage), added aggregate_token_usage() to separately summarize Claude and Codex data, created combine_usage_summaries() for combined outlines/charts, updated format_monthly_markdown() to render the Codex section. Also updated documentation (README.md, summarize/README.md, AGENTS.md) to mention Codex support. Verified through syntax checks and smoke tests.
✅ Created AGENTS.md contributor guide 18:50:04.708 | codex Users requested a repository contributor guide. AI scanned the project structure, read the existing summarize/AGENTS.md and test/AGENTS.md, checked git history for commit conventions. Created a root AGENTS.md with 397 words, covering single-unit repository structure, tool commands (summarize/research/test/website), Python style (PEP 8, Black, Ruff), testing methods (no centralized test suite, specific to tools), commit conventions (regular commits, no Co-Authored-By field), and security notes.
✅ Diagnosed npx/codex PowerShell blocking issues 19:09:16.955 | codex Users reported that ’npx codex’ did not work properly in the current environment. AI checked the paths and found npx.ps1 and codex.ps1, but PowerShell execution policies blocked the .ps1 scripts. Explained that cmd wrappers (npx.cmd, codex.cmd) were used as an immediate solution, with Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned as a permanent fix.
🔍 Plugin list status check 19:24:26.931 | claude_code Users ran local commands/plugin list, /cost, /status—no work required from AI.
Gadget Research ✅ ECC Plugin Exploration and Configuration 21:18:16.758 | claude_code Users ran/init to view CLAUDE.md, which was already very comprehensive. AI added 2 missing configuration file parameters (–paper, –author_id). Then users wanted to learn how to use the ECC plugin. AI explored the plugin structure (28 proxies, over 47 skills, 59 commands), developed a detailed tutorial plan, read internal proxy definitions and skill descriptions. Users explicitly stated they wanted a hands-on tutorial. AI created an ECC_TUTORIAL.md file with 6-step guided exercises. Then users requested all proxies use Opus 4.6 + effortLevel max level. AI updated settings.json to set effortLevel to maximum, batch edited 28 proxy files, and set their models to opus (per user request, one document updater remained as sonnet).
✅ CLAUDE.md Documentation Audit 20:37:15.819 | claude_code Users ran/init. AI found that CLAUDE.md was already very accurate and up-to-date, only adding 2 missing CLI parameters (configuration file commands’–paper, –author_id’).

title: Bug Journal 2026-03-21

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-20 Daily Overview Completed Work: On the MacBook, systematically maintained the infrastructure and data management of the gadget project: generated daily reports for 2026-03-19 using AI dialogue summaries, updated 6 README/tutorial files to reflect the output results/consistent directory structure, verified line by line the consistency of all 10 document files with the actual code, identified and fixed errors in rclone sync-all (only downloading logs without reports), designed and executed operations to remove personal data from the git history (452 files, 94MB), and established a centralized rclone synchronization infrastructure. Completion Method: Used recursive daily_summary.py for export/merge pipelines to generate reports; used batch editing tools to update documentation; started 3 parallel exploration agents to thoroughly verify the consistency of code and documentation; implemented the_rclone_download_reports() function to fix sync-all logic issues; used git rm –cached + git filter-repo to rewrite history records; unified rclone configuration with the gdrive:gadget remote path. Impact: All preparations for the 2026-03-19 report were completed; documentation and code repository were completely consistent (7 inconsistencies fixed); eliminated the reason for repeated calls of LLM in sync-all (final check now runs correctly); personal data was completely removed from the git history and forced pushed to GitHub (repository size reduced from 22MB to 516KB); established clear data privacy boundaries (452 files are now synchronized by rclone instead of git). DCC Completed Work: No activities today Completion Method: Not applicable Impact: Not applicable MacBook Completed Work: Completed documentation maintenance for the gadget repository, fixed rclone synchronization architecture issues, removed personal files from git, and processed multi-device reports for 2026-03-19. Completion Method: Updated 6 README/tutorial files using batch editing tools (approximately 220 insertions, 113 deletions); started 3 parallel exploration agents for documentation verification; implemented the_rclone_download_reports() function to fix sync-all issues; used AskUserQuestion to confirm rclone path (gdrive:gadget), handled risks of git history rewriting, and indicated sources in Search.md; executed a 7-step git cleanup plan: updated.gitignore files, used git rm –cached to remove 435 files, created sync.py wrapper, used git filter-repo to clear history records, migrated rclone remote paths, forced push to GitHub, and first fully uploaded rclone data; restored.gitkeep files after filtering. Impact: Fixed the issue where repeated calls of LLM caused final check failures in sync-all; documentation and code repository were consistent (7 numerical/path inconsistencies fixed); designed a complete data privacy boundary solution (removed 452 files from git, rewritten 24 commit history records, unified rclone management); repository size reduced from 22MB to 516KB; provided a clear implementation path for future maintenance. TzJsDesktop Completed Work: No activities today Completion Method: Not applicable Impact: Not applicable tianhe Completed Work: No activities today Completion Method: Not applicable Impact: Not applicable On the MacBook, generated daily reports for 2026-03-19, fully updated and verified the consistency of all documentation and code repository (7 inconsistencies fixed), identified errors in rclone sync-all (missing reports caused repeated calls of LLM), and executed a complete git history cleanup operation, removing 452 personal files (94MB), while establishing a centralized rclone synchronization infrastructure (repository size reduced from 22MB to 516KB).
Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Design and execute removal of personal data from git + establish rclone synchronization infrastructure — Executed an 8-step plan: (1) Confirmed use of gdrive:gadget for unified remote paths, retained Search.md in git, accepted risks of git history rewriting; (2) Updated.gitignore files with 17 rules regarding personal data; (3) Used git rm –cached to remove 435 files (logs/reports, website content, research data, test results), while retaining local copies; (4) Created sync.py script supporting push/pull/status/config commands, and allowed use of –dry-run and –category flags to specify 4 types of data (summary, website, research, test); (5) Used git filter-repo to remove personal data from all 24 commits (repository size reduced from 22MB to 516KB); (6) Migrated existing rclone data from gdrive:summarize to gdrive:gadget; (7) Forced push of the rewritten history to GitHub; (8) Firstfully uploaded all personal data. Restored.gitkeep files after filtering. Established clear boundaries: git manages public code/config, rclone manages personal data/runtime output. ✅ Fixed rclone sync-all error: only downloading logs without reports — Discovered that CMD_merge_sync_all only called_rclone_download_logs() and did not call reports, resulting in final check failures on local machines, while other devices had generated final reports. This caused repeated calls of LLM. Implemented the_rclone_download_reports() function to download remote reports before checking the final state, ensuring that distributed collaboration status synchronization worked correctly. ✅ Comprehensive line-by-line verification of consistency between documentation and code repository — Started 3 parallel exploration agents to verify the truthfulness of each statement in 10 document files (root directory, summary, research, test, website CLAUDE.md/README.md/tutorial files) with the actual code repository. Identified 7 inconsistencies: CPU/GPU matrix size values in test/CLAUDE.md were reversed (single-core should be 2048 instead of 8192, GPU should be 8192 instead of 2048), cache path in summarize/CLAUDE.md was outdated, “two-stage” and “three-stage” in research/CLAUDE.md were inconsistent, output directory default values were incorrect, comments “3-6” in student_discovery.py did not match the code’s “3-7”. All issues were resolved through batch editing tools and grep zero-residue verification. Also scanned the list of personal files (452 files: summary logs 133/86MB + reports 80/1.7MB, website content 186/4.9MB, research cache/project/reports 60/560KB, test results/data 40/852KB, total approximately 94MB). ✅ Updated all README.md and tutorial.md files to reflect output/migration status — Updated 6 files (root directory README/CLAUDE.md, summary/README+tutorial, test/README+benchmark/README, research/TUTORIAL), moved output paths to unified output/{logs, reports, cache, data}//tree, added CLI flags (–export-past, –force, –affiliation, –conference, –source, etc.), dependencies between tools are now implemented through the common/package, sync.py as the new top-level tool. All files underwent approximately 220 insertions and 113 deletions. After verifying all code uses Path(file).resolve().parent dynamic parsing, outdated warnings about hard-coded user paths were removed. Implementation and Fixes - ✅ Batch generated daily reports for 2026-03-19 based on multi-device AI dialogue logs — Recursively ran the daily_summary.py script to complete the following tasks: (1) Parsed dialogue logs from DCC, MacBook, TzJsDesktop, tianhe, covering 5 projects (MIHD, CalendarPro/Life Copilot, Error Recovery Benchmark, robobrain_pi, gadget); (2) Generated JSON summaries for each project containing tasks/problems and human/AI analysis; (3) Merged summaries of the 4 projects into a comprehensive daily report, including global and per-device overview. Also processed tianhe’s historical reports (Error Recovery Benchmark: SpaceMouse pipeline, MimicGen deformation errors, Sanchi-style architecture) as well as cross-device reports (CalendarPro query routing, robobrain.pi BCE weight calculation, gadget final processing failures) Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. Personal data files (dialogue logs, AI summaries, blog content, research cache, benchmark test results) are tracked and pushed to GitHub through git, making the boundary between code repository and data storage unclear and posing a privacy risk Solution: Designed a three-layer cleanup mechanism: (1) Current working directory: Used git rm –cached to remove tracking records; (2) Rewrote all 24 commit history records using git filter-repo; (3) After forced push, used rclone to uniformly manage personal files (remote path gdrive:gadget). Reduced repository size from 22MB to 516KB
Key Insight: The responsibility boundaries of Git and rclone must be clearly defined: Git manages public code and copyable configuration settings, while rclone is responsible for personal data and runtime output. Mixing the two can lead to repository expansion (94MB of personal data), privacy leakage, and collaboration difficulties. A clear separation can establish data privacy boundaries
2. When users perform merge operations on other devices to generate final reports, local merge–sync-all still considers all dates as non-final states, leading to repeated calls of LLM Solution: AI discovered that CMD_merge_sync_all only called_rclone_download_logs() and did not call reports, resulting in local final check failures. Implemented the_rclone_download_reports() function to pull existing reports from remote before synchronizing all final state checks
Key Insight: In a distributed collaboration scenario, state synchronization must synchronize inputs (logs) and outputs (reports) simultaneously; otherwise, repeated processing logic will lead to resource waste (repeated calls of LLM)
3. The CPU BLAS matrix_size recorded in test/CLAUDE.md was incorrectly marked as 8192 (single-core) and 4096 (all cores), while GPU was set to 2048 Solution: Confirmed the actual code: CpuSingleCoreBLASBenchmark.MATRIX_SIZE=2048, CpuAllCoresBenchmark.MATRIX_SIZE=4096, GpuBenchmark.MATRIX_SIZE=8192. Updated documentation to match the actual situation
Key Insight: Differences between documentation and code will accumulate over time; systematic line-by-line verification using parallel agents can detect numerical errors that may be overlooked when reading casually. Systematic and comprehensive verification is necessary to ensure correctness
General Problems 4. git filter-repo removed.gitkeep files and the directory after filtering, destroying the preservation of the directory structure Solution: Recreated.gitkeep files (research/cache, research/project, research/reports), used touch and git add -f operations, and submitted them as a separate “directory structure restoration” submission after filter-repo
Key Insight: The –invert-paths option of git filter-repo will delete the entire directory tree including structural files; restoration of.gitkeep must be submitted after filter-repo, not before filtering
5. In research/CLAUDE.md, line 74 mentions a “two-stage evaluation process,” but the title in line 79 is “three-stage evaluation process” — there is an internal contradiction Solution: Confirmed that the code implemented 3 stages (filtering → in-depth analysis → citing impact); updated line 74 to “three-stage,” and batch replaced all 7 occurrences of “two-stage” in research_scout.py with “three-stage”
Key Insight: Contradictions in documentation indicate that historical evolution has not been fully coordinated; batch corrections using grep can ensure no residual inconsistencies
Comparison of Human and AI Methods Strategic Level Data Management Architecture: Long-term planning vs Short-term fixes Role Method Human After AI fixed the error in syncing all files, users immediately raised higher requirements: “All files related to me should be synchronized via rclone, GitHub should not retain copies of these files” — fundamentally redefined the data boundaries Artificial Intelligence AI initially only repaired the problem by adding the_rclone_download_reports function, without actively examining the rationality of the overall architecture. Only after users raised questions did it conduct comprehensive exploration and design Difference Analysis: Humans have stronger insights in architectural planning and data privacy awareness; AI tends to solve problems within the current framework, rather than questioning the entire framework itself. This indicates that humans have advantages in strategic planning
Git Filter-Repo Strategy: Incremental cleanup vs Complete rewriting Role Method Human Users directly specified “use git filter-repo + forced push,” without considering incremental git rm for new commits only Artificial Intelligence AI proposed the second step (git rm –cached for new commits) and the fourth step (filter-repo for historical data), adopting a two-step approach, while also noting the irreversibility of filter-repo Difference Analysis: Humans prioritize thorough cleanup (removing personal data from all history records, reducing repository size from 22MB to 516KB); AI adds a safety layer (first using git rm to create checkpoints, then performing destructive operations). Combining these two methods allows for taking advantage of both: clean history records + security guarantee with.git backups
Integrity of rclone synchronization strategy: Input syncing only vs Full state synchronization Role Method Human Users discovered that when synchronizing all files, only logs were downloaded, immediately pointing out “Reports also need to be downloaded,” thus directly finding the root cause of the problem Artificial Intelligence AI initially only implemented_rclone_download_logs; after receiving user feedback, realized that final checks relied on report files, so added the_rclone_download_reports function Difference Analysis: Humans have a more comprehensive understanding of the need for distributed collaboration state synchronization; AI’s initial implementation only covered input syncing (logs), but ignored output syncing (reports), requiring user correction. This indicates that humans have advantages in understanding the needs for full state synchronization
Decision-making on Document Verification Scope: Targeted vs Comprehensive Role Method Human Users demanded “Check all document files and confirm that every sentence is correct” — no pre-screening open-ended comprehensive audit Artificial Intelligence AI initially proposed only verifying specific areas or using sampling; under user insistence, started 3 parallel Explore agents to thoroughly cover all 10 files AI Limitations Key Limitations In designing rclone synchronization logic, AI failed to anticipate the dependence of final checks on reports in sync-all scenarios, resulting in users needing to explicitly state “Reports also need to be downloaded.” Lack of foresight in maintaining integrity of distributed collaboration state synchronization. AI initially did not suggest line-by-line document verification until users explicitly requested it; default mode was targeted/sample-based verification, which might overlook numerical errors (such as matrix_size, doctoral degree duration). General Limitations AI initiated 3 parallel exploration agents to scan the code repository (list of personal files, rclone logic, git history), although this approach is comprehensive, efficiency may not be ideal—this information can be quickly obtained through several grep/ls/git commands, and the overhead of agents might be excessive. AI failed to predict that.gitkeep files would be removed by git filter-repo –invert-paths (directory structure preservation issues); users needed to manually restore them after post-filter-repo. Learning Outcomes Key Takeaways### Practice Learning Outcomes When building a centralized synchronization infrastructure (sync.py), supporting the –dry-run and –category flags from the beginning allows for incremental testing and selective synchronization without uploading all data. Rclone remote path migration (gdrive:summarize/ → gdrive:gadget/) is less risky, with atomic moves. When modifying remote paths, it’s necessary to check the integrity of configuration migrations: updating (1) summarize/daily_summary.py config.json reading, (2) research/research_scout.py configuration, (3) test/sync logic, (4) website/Hugo integration, (5) sync.py global configuration, and (6) existing remote data migration scripts. Dialogue Summary 🔄 rclone synchronization architecture fixes and removal of personal files from git 00:57:00.216 | claude_code Users found that merge –sync-all only downloads logs but not reports, leading to failed final checks and repeated LLM calls. AI implemented a fix for _rclone_download_reports(). Users further proposed removing all personal files (452 files, 94MB) from git entirely and switching to rclone for management. AI explored the code library to identify a list of personal files, using AskUserQuestion to confirm rclone paths (gdrive:gadget), Search.md attribution information, and risks of git history rewriting. An implementation plan was generated in 8 steps, and planning mode was entered.
✅ Batch generation of daily reports for 2026-03-19 + comprehensive documentation maintenance + git history cleanup 01:02:50.662 | claude_code Multi-stage process: (1) recursively generate daily reports for 2026-03-19 from AI dialogue logs across multiple devices, covering 5 projects (MIHD, CalendarPro, Error Recovery, robobrain_pi, gadget), merging summaries of each project into a comprehensive report with device overview. (2) Update 6 README/ tutorial files to reflect output/directory migration, new CLI flags, common/package dependencies, and changes in sync.py tools (about 220 insertions and 113 deletions). (3) After verifying all code uses dynamic path resolution, outdated hard-coded path warnings were removed. (4) Start 3 parallel exploration agents for thorough documentation verification, identifying and fixing 7 issues (CPU/GPU matrix_size values, cache paths, number of stages, doctoral degree duration, output_dir default value). (5) Implement complete git history cleanup: update.gitignore files, use git rm –cached to remove 435 personal files, create sync.py for rclone wrapper, use git filter-repo to clean up history (22MB → 516KB), migrate rclone remote paths, force push to GitHub, and upload all data via rclone for the first time. Restore.gitkeep files after post-filter-repo.
✅ Handling daily reports for Tianhe devices on 2026-03-19 01:18:37.282 | claude_code Users provided a pre-generated JSON format daily report (Tianhe’s work for 2026-03-19: Error Recovery Benchmark SpaceMouse collection pipeline fixes, MimicGen deformation errors, Sanchi data architecture design, training error scenario generation scripts). AI directly returned the JSON content. This could be used for report format verification or archiving.
✅ Handling daily reports across devices on 2026-03-19 01:13:53.752 | claude_code Users provided a pre-generated JSON format daily report (TzJsDesktop’s work for 2026-03-19: CalendarPro CLAUDE.md refinement and calendar query error fixes, robobrain_pi positive sample weighted BCE training and inference optimization, gadget final processing issues, GPU zombie process identification). AI directly returned the JSON content. This could be used for report system verification or multi-device work summary.

title: Bug Journal 2026-03-20

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-19 Daily Overview Completed Work: Four parallel projects were carried out, including spatial transcriptomics experiments using the Harmony algorithm to reveal trade-offs in semantic batch mixing. A robot error recovery process was implemented, optimizing training through portable deployment, which passed 139 tests. The CalendarPro/Life Copilot architecture was transformed to eliminate single-intent bottlenecks by using multi-intent classifiers. Over 2400 lines of code were thoroughly audited and archived, fixing calendar query issues and renaming the project. Additionally, knowledge management infrastructure was expanded: the gadget research tool’s 5-mode system was upgraded to support deep analysis on any scale. A comprehensive analysis of Yuke Zhu’s academic trajectory from 2012 to 2026 was conducted, covering 18 to 100 papers. This involved multiple analysis iterations and integrated historical daily reports from February to March 30, 2026.
Completion Method: Controlled experiments were performed (Harmony fusion with alignment, weighted BCE loss for task completion). Core infrastructure was implemented (multi-intent LLM analyzer, coordinate transformation fixes,分割长文本分析和LLM合并, dynamic timeout formula of 300 seconds + 120 seconds/50K characters). Systematic refactoring was carried out (thoroughly auditing over 2400 lines of code, removing dead code, second-phase cleanup, redesigning the 5-mode research tool, modifying 7 source files). Intensive batch processing operations were also performed (data from 15-20 researchers, including 6 rounds of in-depth analysis on Yuke Zhu, resulting in full-text analysis of 100 papers using 47 block segmentation methods. Historical reports over 30 days were integrated, and 12 JSON fixes were made to finalize the mechanism fault diagnosis method).
Impact: The fundamental limitations of linear batch correction when embedding dimensions have different semantics were identified. Through MimicGen deformation repairs, the problem of demonstrating enhancements from 10 to 1000 was solved. The single-intent architecture limitation of CalendarPro was eliminated, and critical vulnerabilities were fixed. The portable recovery deployment passed tests with scores of 139/139. A complete academic trajectory of Yuke Zhu was established (h-index of 56, citations of 24,611, from data infrastructure builder to foundational model pioneer and world model architect). The research tool’s capabilities shifted from “quick overview” to supporting full-text analysis on any scale. Historical work over February to March was integrated, establishing a unified cross-device work record. Deadlock issues caused by infinite LLM API cost cycles were identified (3 device dates marked as finalized=False).
DCC
Completed Work: MIHD spatial transcriptomics experiments across samples were conducted, and strategic plans were developed for ARI improvement and batch effect reduction. Claude Code demonstrations were prepared for laboratory meetings. Historical sessions served as data sources to generate retrospective reports covering milestones from February to March 2026 (Visium HD adaptation, RM-IDEAL evaluation, scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiment, dead code cleanup of approximately 250K lines of code).
Completion Method: Fusion-aligned Harmony was implemented and evaluated in sections 151508-151673. Multi-agent exploration was initiated to diagnose cluster ARI gaps (0.475 compared to SOTA’s 0.66%). A 30-minute demonstration was designed, covering planned patterns, permissions, and AI overconfidence examples using DLPFC data. Historical work was retrospectively analyzed on TzJsDesktop using the gadget summary tool.
Impact: Key findings: Harmony improved batch mixing metrics (ASW-batch increased by 36%), but semantic alignment declined due to inconsistent dimension semantics during each training session. Batch correction and semantic alignment are two different attributes that need to be trained together. Complete demonstration materials were provided. A dual-track solution for ARI and batch effect problems was determined. Historical milestones from February to March were fully archived.
MacBook
Completed Work: An error recovery collection environment with SpaceMouse integration was configured. Remote file transfer was debugged, and checkpoints were uploaded to the athena server. Brief environmental setup attempts were interrupted. Historical sessions served as data sources to generate retrospective reports covering gadget tool development and Desktop Video 4.1 features from February to March 2026.
Completion Method: A recovery_collect conda environment was created. The 3Dconnexion driver was uninstalled to solve device locking issues. The robosuite macros_private.py file was modified, and the EnvWrapper.env property error was fixed. Portable packages were downloaded via scp, and 14GB of checkpoints were transferred. setup_env.sh was run to create the recovery_collect conda environment. Dependencies for mujoco/robosuite/mimicgen were installed (sessions were interrupted during environment creation). Historical work was retrospectively analyzed on TzJsDesktop using the gadget summary tool.
Impact: SpaceMouse ran successfully. Only 11 types of errors produced scenarios. Large model checkpoints were successfully transferred for subsequent work. Environmental setup was incomplete. Historical work on MacBook (gadget summary models switched to Sonnet, Desktop Video historical features, 4.1 screen saver clock transparent frosting repair) was recorded in a structured report.
TzJsDesktop
Completed Work: Comprehensive knowledge management and system refactoring of major active devices were carried out: CalendarPro/Life Copilot completed a thorough transformation of the system (multi-intent classifiers, calendar query fixes, code library health checks, archiving over 2400 lines of code). The research tool was significantly upgraded (5-mode system design for block-based long text analysis). In-depth analyses of Yuke Zhu were conducted, resulting in full-text analysis of 100 papers under deep wide mode. 47 block segments and 5 retries optimizations were used. Historical daily reports over 30 days were integrated, fixing JSON format errors (including unescaped quotes, truncated fields, incomplete parentheses). Two-level finalization mechanism issues were identified through code review. Tutorial documents were updated with actual device names.
Implementation Method: A multi-intent analyzer based on Claude CLI was designed and implemented, replacing the dual-validation + 7-layer keyword processing scheme. Three-level calendar query errors were fixed. Thorough code library audits and phased refactoring were carried out. Seven source files were modified (analysis.py added_MODE_CONFIG and block logic, prompts.py added TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT, cli.py/research_scout.py/config.py/models.py updated options). The Chunk_papers_by_year greedy algorithm was implemented (target of 150K characters per session), dynamic timeout formula (300 seconds + 120 seconds/50K characters), and TRAJECTORY_MERGE_PROMPT for semantic integration. In-depth analyses of Yuke Zhu were conducted multiple times, using different paper subsets (from 1 paper to 100 full-text papers, divided into 47 blocks through LLM merging). The daily_summary.py export→merge workflow was batch-调用的, handling over 30 historical dates. JSON format errors were manually fixed (including unescaped quotes, truncated fields, incomplete parentheses). Two-level finalization mechanism issues were identified through code review. Tutorial.md document was updated with actual device names.
Impact: The basic single-intent bottleneck of CalendarPro was eliminated, allowing handling of multi-action messages. Calendar query functionality was restored. The complexity of classification code was reduced by approximately 200 lines. Over 2400 lines of dead code were removed, while complete test coverage was maintained (373-424 test passes). The research tool’s capabilities improved from “quick overview” to “deep excavation” — deep wide mode supported full-text analysis on any scale, breaking through the capacity limit of a single LLM through the block merging mechanism. Comprehensive academic analysis of Yuke Zhu was completed, revealing his development trajectory: from visual genome vision understanding to AI2-THOR embodied intelligence, then to robosuite/RoboTurk ecosystem construction, to MineDojo/Voyager LLM-driven embodied agents, and finally to geometric intermediate representation + GPU-native simulation + VLM alignment (2012-2025, 86-100 papers, 7 breakthrough achievements). Over 30 key historical works were integrated (three major upgrades of CalendarPro, ErrorRecoveryBenchmark M5-M14 milestones, MIHD benchmark Visium HD adaptation and code cleanup, Desktop Video 4.1 release). Three device dates caused permanent finalized=False deadlock (March 03 MacBook, March 07 MacBook/TzJsDesktop, March 15 tianhe), leading to infinite LLM API cost cycles. Format errors in the LLM JSON generation system were recorded (special character escape, long text truncation, incomplete nested objects)
tianhe Completed Work: Model training optimization, error recovery collection, and portable deployment preparation were carried out. Training scenarios with controlled amplitude distributions were generated, and MimicGen deformation errors were fixed, making it possible to enhance by 10 to 1000 times. A human demonstration collection architecture was designed, and a git proxy was configured. Historical sessions served as data sources for retrospective reports, covering milestones from February to March 2026 (Pi0.5 Phoenix training, BC-RNN baseline, ErrorRecoveryBenchmark M5-M14, robobrain_pi training monitoring improvements, LLaVA model conversion, LIBERO evaluation extension).
Implementation Method: Positive sample weighting was applied to the BCE loss balance (pos_weight=10), and sampleactions_with_completion was created to reuse VLM prefix features, avoiding repeated forward transfers. A comprehensive recovery collection refactoring was carried out, fixing SpaceMouse initialization and rendering errors. The generate_training_scenes.py file was created, using ControlledRng for deterministic amplitude sampling (ratio of 0/9 to 9/9), and _replay_with_warping was fixed to truly apply pos_delta transformation. A three-tier data pool was designed (training 10/subtypes → enhancement 200/subtypes → evaluation 100+/subtypes). The git protocol was changed from SSH to HTTPS, and a proxy (127.0.0.1:10087) was used. Historical work was retrospectively analyzed on TzJsDesktop using the gadget summarize tool.
Impact: Model training reached a plateau of balanced gradient contributions. Portable deployment packages were created (compressed size 252MB), containing 139 unit tests that passed unit testing. It was possible to collect 1320 training scenarios with uniform amplitude distribution. Through pos_delta repair, MimicGen enhancement paths became possible for generating demonstrations with a factor of 10 to 1000 (10 seeds × 200 targets × 50% success rate). 352 files were cleanly pulled through the proxy. Core work on tianhe from February to March 2026 was fully archived.
Cross-device multi-project development was carried out: spatial transcriptomics Harmony alignment experiments revealed trade-offs in semantic batch mixing. A robot error recovery process for training optimization and portable deployment was implemented (139 tests passed). CalendarPro/Life Copilot completed architectural transformations, eliminating single-intent bottlenecks by using multi-intent classifiers. Knowledge management infrastructure was expanded, including an upgrade of the 5-mode system of research tools to support block-based long text analysis. Yuke Zhu completed comprehensive academic trajectory analysis (2012-2026, over 100 papers, more than 10 analysis iterations), as well as batch integration from February to March 30, 2026.
