content:
Yiran Chen — Research Analysis Report Analysis Mode: fast | Analysis Date: 2026-03-16T00:35:12
Rating: Rising Star (73.7/100) Basic Metrics Metric Value Institution Unknown h-index 65 Total Citations 17,649 Recent 5-year Citations 1,099 Number of Papers 369 Papers in Top Conferences 23 Publication Years 2003 - 2023 Semantic Scholar 5442167 Research Trajectory Yiran Chen’s academic trajectory shows three clear transitions. The first phase (2003-2007) focused on optimizing the power consumption of traditional computer architectures, with Deterministic Clock Gating (DCG) being a representative example. This was a solid foundation in classical VLSI design. The second phase (2008-2015) marked his transition into the emerging storage field: as technologies like Spin-Transfer Torque RAM (STT-RAM), Phase Change Memory (PCM), and Memristor moved from laboratory research to engineering, Chen quickly took charge of NVM device modeling, reliability analysis, cache architecture integration, and neuromorphic computing. He published extensively in top conferences such as HPCA, DAC, and ICCD, laying the foundation for this field’s methodology. The third phase (2016-present) saw a breakthrough transformation: his 2016 paper on Structured Sparsity Learning received 2,478 citations, marking his entry into deep learning compression with a hardware-aware perspective. Since then, he has continued to produce influential work in distributed training communication compression, ReRAM-based accelerators (PipeLayer/GraphR), and AI security (anti-attacks, federated learning defense). Chen’s ability to bridge the gap between hardware and AI lies in his focus on “how to run AI efficiently on limited hardware.” He integrates device physics, circuit design, architecture, and algorithm optimization into a unified research framework. This rare all-around perspective allowed him to lead the way in hardware-algorithm co-design when this became mainstream.
Breakthrough Works 1. A novel architecture of the 3D stacked MRAM L2 cache for CMPs (2009) Description: The first systematic design of three-dimensional stacked MRAM as an L2 cache for multi-core processors. It evaluated the entire process from circuit modeling, thermal analysis to performance/power trade-offs at the architectural level, proving the engineering feasibility of NVM replacing traditional SRAM caches.
Why it couldn’t be done before: Key conditions were not mature by 2009: first, there was no proven compact circuit model for STT-RAM magnetic tunnel junctions; second, three-dimensional chip stacking technology was just in the academic prototype stage; third, previous NVM research mainly focused on device physics, lacking system-level methodologies to integrate them into existing microprocessor storage hierarchies. Chen’s team had both magnetic device modeling and computer architecture skills, filling this interdisciplinary gap.
Impact: It received 453 citations, pioneering the research paradigm of using NVM as a cache alternative. This led to numerous follow-up studies on STT-RAM/PCM/RRAM cache architectures in the following decade, establishing a methodological framework for evaluating devices-circuit-architecture interactions.
2. Memristor Crossbar-Based Neuromorphic Computing System: A Case Study (2014) Description: A systematic implementation and analysis of neuromorphic computing systems based on memristor cross-arrays. This work covered complete engineering issues such as analog matrix-vector multiplication, non-linear activation implementations, and device variation tolerance training. It was a landmark work in the development of memristor-based neuromorphic computing from concept to engineering system.
Why it couldn’t be done before: Memristors were first fabricated and verified by HP Labs in 2008. It took several years for sufficient experimental data on device characteristics (switching ratio, variation distribution, durability) to be available for modeling. More importantly, mapping memristor analog conductance to neural network weights required a combined background in circuit design and neural network training theory—teams with this cross-disciplinary perspective were rare before 2014.
Impact: It received 338 citations, becoming a foundational case study in memristor-based neuromorphic computing. Many subsequent “Computing-in-Memory” accelerator works built upon this work.
3. Learning Structured Sparsity in Deep Neural Networks (2016) Description: Proposed a structured sparsity learning method that automatically learns structured sparse patterns for hardware acceleration during training by applying group Lasso regularization to filter, channel, convolution kernel shapes, and network layer depths. This allowed DNNs to learn structures directly applicable to hardware acceleration without specialized sparse computing hardware.
Why it couldn’t be done before: Previous network compression methods (such as Han Song’s 2015 unstructured pruning) produced random sparsity that could not be efficiently utilized by general-purpose hardware. Specialized sparse computing engines were required. The key insight was the lack of realization that the granularity of sparsity constraints could be elevated from weight level to structure level, with group regularization naturally emerging during training. Chen’s team shifted hardware feasibility requirements back into algorithm design, making this hardware-aware ML methodology highly advanced at the time.
Impact: It received 2478 citations, making it one of his most cited papers. It changed the research paradigm in DNN compression, leading to structured pruning becoming the mainstream technology for model deployment in industry, and inspiring many subsequent channel pruning and layer pruning methods.
4. TernGrad: Ternary Gradients to Reduce Communication in Distributed Deep Learning (2017) Description: Proposed using ternary (-1, 0, +1) gradients instead of full-precision floating-point gradients for gradient synchronization during distributed training. Theoretical convergence was proven, and experiments showed significant reduction in communication bandwidth requirements while maintaining almost no loss of accuracy.
Why it couldn’t be done before: Large-scale distributed deep learning training had not become common before 2017. Communication bottlenecks became prominent with the rise of large models like AlexNet/ResNet. Previous quantitative research focused on weight compression for inference, and using gradient quantization for training compression required a deep understanding of SGD convergence dynamics. The insight that the direction of gradients rather than exact values was more important emerged in 2017 when the need for distributed training became urgent.
Impact: It received 1053 citations, pioneering the subfield of gradient compression for distributed learning and communication optimization. It directly led to developments like 1-bit SGD and SignSGD, having a lasting impact on large-scale AI training infrastructure.
5. PipeLayer: A Pipelined ReRAM-Based Accelerator for Deep Learning (2017) Description: Proposed a pipelined ReRAM-based accelerator for deep learning inference. By mapping neural network layers to ReRAM cross-arrays and designing cross-layer pipeline data streams, it achieved a combination of in-memory computing and high throughput inference, outperforming GPU solutions in terms of energy efficiency and speed.
Why it couldn’t be done before: Three conditions needed to be met: (1) The ReRAM device model had to be mature enough to support system-level simulation; (2) The computational mode of deep learning inference (mainly dense matrix multiplication) had to be well analyzed, making its compatibility with analog cross-arrays obvious; (3) ADC/DAC design technology and peripheral circuit capabilities had to be sufficient to support mixed-signal system design. Before 2017, there was insufficient experimental data on ReRAM chip prototypes, and the modeling of DNN tolerance for accuracy loss was not mature.
Impact: It received 750 citations, becoming a landmark work in ReRAM-based in-memory computing accelerators. It established the principle of “eliminating ReRAM write latency bottlenecks with pipelines,” leading to research on CIM accelerators like ReTransformer and Lattice.
Research Areas Non-volatile memory architecture (STT-RAM/RRAM/PCM) device modeling and system integration In-memory computing based on memristors/ReRAMs and neuromorphic accelerators Deep neural network compression and hardware-aware efficient inference (structured pruning, low-rank decomposition, quantization) AI security and robustness (anti-attack generation and defense, data poisoning, federated learning security) Distributed machine learning and federated learning (communication compression, client selection, heterogeneity handling) Neural architecture search (NAS) and AutoML Methodological Evolution Chen’s methodological evolution shows a clear “spiral rise from bottom to top, then from top to bottom.” In the early stages (2003-2010), SPICE simulation and VLSI analysis tools were central. The methodology was rooted in circuit design, focusing on precise modeling at the device and circuit levels. From 2010 to 2015, the methodology expanded to the architecture level—introducing statistical variation analysis, Monte Carlo probability modeling, and cross-layer (device-circuit-architecture) collaborative evaluation frameworks. This “from physics to system” vertical integration became its core competitiveness. Since 2016, the methodology has changed direction: while retaining hardware implementation feasibility constraints, it shifted towards algorithm optimization. SSL and TernGrad exemplify the reverse thinking of “hardware constraints driving algorithm design.” After 2019, the methodology expanded into system security, using game theory (attack-defense confrontation) and privacy computing frameworks to analyze federated learning security. In recent years, it has extended to LLM inference optimization. The overall trajectory is: single-layer precise modeling → cross-layer collaborative optimization → hardware-algorithm joint design → system security analysis. Each expansion was based on the deep accumulation of the previous stage.
Domain Impact Yiran Chen’s impact on the field is reflected in three dimensions: First, in the emerging memory field, his work on STT-RAM/RRAM (2008-2015) established the foundational methodology for NVM computer architecture research, turning “using NVM to replace/supplement SRAM” from physical concept into an engineering-oriented research agenda. Second, in deep learning systems, his papers on SSL (2478 citations) and TernGrad (1053 citations) are textbook works. The former defined the technical direction of structured pruning, while the latter pioneered the subfield of gradient compression. Together, they have received over 3500 citations, profoundly influencing the technology roadmap for AI chip deployment and large-scale training infrastructure. Third, in AI security and trustworthy AI, his team has made foundational contributions to anti-attack samples, poisoning attacks, and federated learning defense, promoting a systematic understanding of AI system vulnerabilities in academia. His unique value lies in being one of the few scholars who have published highly cited works in both top computer architecture conferences (HPCA/MICRO/ISCA) and machine learning conferences (NeurIPS/ICML/CVPR/ICLR). He continues to act as a “translator” and “bridge builder” between the hardware and AI communities. His h-index of 65 and total citations of nearly 18,000 are top-tier in this composite field.
Cited Students/Leadership Roles Name Source Status Co-authored Papers Relationship Score Research Areas Hsin-Pai Cheng Co-authored - 20 1.00 Hardware-aware neural network compression (quantization, pruning), neural architecture search, efficient inference systems Fan Chen Co-authored - 16 1.00 Memristor/ReRAM in-memory computing accelerators, DNN hardware mapping and optimization Nathan Inkawhich Co-authored - 14 1.00 AI anti-robustness, anti-attack sample generation and defense, deep learning security evaluation Linghao Song Co-authored - 24 1.00 In-memory computing architecture, DNN computation graph mapping, non-volatile memory system integration Xiaoxiao Liu Co-authored - 14 1.00 Federated learning, distributed machine learning, privacy-preserving AI Jiachen Mao Co-authored - 10 1.00 Neural network accelerator design, DNN compilation and optimization Jie Guo Co-authored - 11 0.94 Non-volatile memory (STT-RAM/PCM) device modeling and circuit design Kent W. Nixon Co-authored - 16 0.94 Non-volatile memory systems, low-power embedded AI Enes Eken Co-authored - 10 0.94 STT-RAM/RRAM memory architecture and reliability Ismail Bayram Co-authored - 9 0.93 Non-volatile memory modeling and simulation, memory reliability analysis Top 20 Cited Papers # Year Citations Title 1 2016 2,478 Learning Structured Sparsity in Deep Neural Networks 2 2017 1,053 TernGrad: Ternary Gradients to Reduce Communication in Distributed Deep Learning 3 2017 750 PipeLayer: A Pipelined ReRAM-Based Accelerator for Deep Learning 4 2009 453 A novel architecture of the 3D stacked MRAM L2 cache for CMPs 5 2008 346 Circuit and microarchitecture evaluation of 3D stacking magnetic RAM (MRAM) as a universal memory replacement 6 2018 342 DPATCH: An Adversarial Patch Attack on Object Detectors 7 2014 338 Memristor Crossbar-Based Neuromorphic Computing System: A Case Study 8 2020 329 A Survey of Accelerator Architectures for Deep Neural Networks 9 2017 289 MoDNN: Local distributed mobile computing system for Deep Neural Network 10 2017 278 GraphR: Accelerating Graph Processing Using ReRAM 11 2017 229 Generative Poisoning Attack Method Against Neural Networks 12 2019 228 Neural Predictor for Neural Architecture Search 13 2017 205 Accelerator-friendly neural-network training: Learning variations and defects in RRAM crossbar 14 2019 199 Feature Space Perturbations Yield More Transferable Adversarial Examples 15 2011 198 Emerging non-volatile memories: Opportunities and challenges 16 2016 187 Faster CNNs with Direct Sparse Convolutions and Guided Pruning 17 2023 186 OpenOOD v1.5: Enhanced Benchmark for Out-of-Distribution Detection 18 2015 181 Vortex: Variation-aware training for memristor X-bar 19 2014 156 eButton: A wearable computer for health monitoring and personal assistance 20 2015 156 RENO: A high-efficient reconfigurable neuromorphic computing accelerator design
summary: Yiran Chen: Yiran Chen’s academic journey can be divided into three distinct stages. The first stage (2003-2007) focused on optimizing the power consumption of traditional computer architectures, with deterministic clock gating being a key technique used in VLSI design. This period marked his solid foundation in this field. The second stage (2008-2015) saw him turning to emerging storage technologies as his research focus.

content:
Yongfeng Ding — Researcher Analysis Report Analysis Mode: fast | Analysis Time: 2026-03-16T00:12:19
Rating: Early Researcher (18.6/100) Basic Metrics Metric Value Institution Unknown h-index 2 Total Citations 24 Recent Citations in Last 5 Years 24 Number of Papers Published 4 Papers Published in Top Journals 0 Publication Period 2023 - 2026 Semantic Scholar 2241760086 Research Trajectory Yongfeng Ding is an emerging researcher in the early stages of their academic career. They began publishing papers in 2023, with only 4 works to date. While they don’t possess the characteristics of a ‘field expert’, they have shown some originality in the quantitative analysis of microstructures in composite materials. Their key contribution was the development of the ‘degree of randomness’ metric in 2023, which is used to assess the uniformity of fiber distributions in composite materials. This methodological work has received 16 citations, indicating recognition within the field.
It’s worth noting that in 2026, there was a paper on EEG emotion recognition that had nothing to do with the previous three papers (in the field of traffic injury prevention). This significant shift across disciplines is either indicative of the researcher exploring interdisciplinary collaboration or possibly a case of people with the same name but different identities. Overall, this researcher is still in the early stages of academic accumulation, with too little research output to determine their long-term direction.
Breakthrough Works 1. A new quantitative method to evaluate the spatial distribution of fibres in composites: the degree of randomness (2023) Description: Proposed a new quantitative metric called ‘degree of randomness’, which is used to assess the randomness of fiber distributions in composite materials, providing a measurable mathematical description of fiber distribution uniformity.
Why it couldn’t be done before: There was a lack of a unified quantitative framework for evaluating the randomness of fiber distributions. Researchers relied on visual judgment or indirect metrics such as nearest-neighbor distances, lacking a dimensionless metric that could be compared across different material systems. The key insight was to transfer the concept of randomness from statistical physics to the analysis of microstructures in composite materials.
Impact: Provided a standardized evaluation scale for evaluating algorithms that generate virtual microstructures. This directly supported an algorithm comparison work published in the same year, which received 6 citations.
2. Comparison of three algorithms generating virtual microstructures in terms of the degree of randomness (2023) Description: Based on the ‘degree of randomness’ metric, this paper systematically compares the performance of three algorithms that generate virtual microstructures in composite materials, evaluating their ability to reproduce realistic fiber distributions.
Why it couldn’t be done before: There was a lack of a unified evaluation criterion for comparing different algorithms for generating virtual microstructures. Each algorithm was developed independently and could not be compared across disciplines. The introduction of the ‘degree of randomness’ method made this systematic comparison possible.
Impact: Received 16 citations. This is the most influential work of the researcher so far, providing an algorithm selection reference for the community of composite material digital twins and multi-scale modeling.
3. Micromechanical modelling of unidirectional continuous fibre-reinforced composites: A review (2025) Description: A systematic review of micromechanical modeling methods for unidirectional continuous fiber-reinforced composites, outlining the evolution and current state of research in this field.
Why it couldn’t be done before: This is a review article written after the researcher established a quantitative method for microstructures in 2023. They had now acquired an overview of the entire micromechanical modeling field from a more macroscopic perspective.
Impact: Received 2 citations (published in 2025, with citation count still accumulating). It helps to integrate scattered literature on micromechanical modeling and provides a systematic reference for researchers new to this field.
Research Directions Quantitative analysis of microstructures in composite materials and randomness analysis Micromechanical modeling of fiber-reinforced composites (Possibly cross-disciplinary) EEG feature selection and emotion recognition Methodological Evolution The researcher started by proposing a new quantitative metric (degree of randomness method, 2023), which was then applied to empirical research on algorithm evaluation in the same year. This shows a compact two-step approach: ‘proposal of method → verification of method’. In 2025, they turned to writing a review article, indicating that they began to attempt to establish a field perspective rather than focusing solely on individual technical points. If the EEG paper in 2026 belongs to the same researcher, it indicates a break from methodology—from statistical quantification in materials science to physiological signal processing and machine learning feature selection. There is no clear path of knowledge transfer between these areas, indicating an unclear cross-disciplinary motivation.
Overall, the methodological evolution so far is relatively shallow, with too few papers published to determine whether the direction will continue to focus on this area.
Field Impact Yongfeng Ding’s impact in the field is currently limited to the specific direction of microstructural analysis in composite materials. Their key contribution was the proposal of the ‘degree of randomness’ metric and its application in algorithm evaluation. With only 3 years of publication, 4 papers, and 24 citations (mainly in material-related journals), their influence is still localized and early-stage. They have not yet achieved a level of systematic impact on the entire field of composite materials or related areas. However, if they continue to focus on quantitative analysis of microstructures and expand into multi-scale modeling applications, there is potential for them to develop significant academic influence.
Top 20 Papers with High Citations # Year Citations Title 1 2023 16 Comparison of three algorithms generating virtual microstructures in terms of the degree of randomness 2 2023 6 A new quantitative method to evaluate the spatial distribution of fibres in composites: the degree of randomness 3 2025 2 Micromechanical modelling of unidirectional continuous fibre-reinforced composites: A review 4 2026 0 Optimization and validation of multiscale feature selection for EEG-based recognition of drivers’ negative emotions.
summary: Yongfeng Ding is an emerging researcher in the early stages of their academic career. They began publishing papers in 2023, and currently have only four publications to their name. Therefore, they do not yet possess the characteristics of a ’leading expert in the field’. However, they have demonstrated certain levels of originality in the area of quantitative analysis of composite materials’ microstructure. Their key contributions focus on the concept of ‘randomness’, which was introduced by them in 2023.
title: Yongfeng Ding — Researcher Profile

content:
AI Limitations ✅ Linguistic Understanding: AI may struggle with complex or ambiguous language, leading to inaccurate predictions or incomplete responses.### Conference Award Knowledge is Severely Outdated and Incomplete It’s possible to recall important awards from 2-3 years ago, such as the Best Paper at NeurIPS/CVPR, but it lacks coverage of medium-level conference and workshop awards or honors from recent years (2024 and beyond). It’s correct to refuse to guess, but this limits its usefulness. The recall rate for award IDs is approximately 0% (12/13 queries resulted in nothing). Failure to Consider Zero-Sample Constraints When Proposing Joint PCA and Procrustes Alignment These methods require access to both parts of the data simultaneously, violating deployment requirements. This was corrected only with explicit user guidance.
Unable to Eliminate Ambiguity for Researchers with the Same Name Without External Signals There is a lack of an institutional knowledge graph or consistency check mechanism. Unless the discrepancies are very serious, merged materials from upstream databases are accepted without question. This affects approximately 30% of analytical researchers.
Four Rounds of Time to Fix Condora/Environment/Path Issues Before Users Specified Basic Requirements (Using Claude Cli Instead of Anthropic API) Repeating similar failure modes should have triggered an investigation into the root cause earlier.
Chinese Nested JSON Generation is Unstable There is a 20% failure rate (6 out of 30 outputs are incorrect), requiring fixes. Errors include unclosed strings, escaped quotes in Chinese text, and truncated structures. English JSON is more reliable.
When Users Say “Implement the Following Plan”, It Attempts to Enter the Planning Mode, Misinterpreting Detailed Plans as Triggers for Planning Workflows Instead of Direct Execution Specifications Users had to explicitly reject and clarify their intentions.
Lessons Learned Key Lessons - The Critical Advantage of Basemodels in Cross-Sample Tasks Lies in Their Ability to Provide Shared Embedding Spaces Without Joint Training; Pre-trained Weights Ensure Comparability for Any Input. Zero-shot Cross-Sampling Retrieval Essentially Depends on This Feature—Any Optimization for Each Sample Cannot Replace It. Name ambiguity is the primary data quality issue in automated character profiling—affecting approximately 30% of analysis subjects. Multiple signal approaches are needed: h-index and domain consistency, citation time distribution, institutional metadata, collaborator network analysis. A single data source is not enough. Three-Layered Strategy for Restoring Data Augmentation: Scene configuration (using different error scenarios as initial states), cross-degree (rotating perturbations from D0 to D1/D2), cross-type (cross-enhancement within RBG). Combined with MimicGen trajectory segmentation technology, data requirements can be reduced by 81% (from 1,740 samples to 329 samples). Coordinate transformation errors in composite model systems can be masked through a state replay mechanism—this mechanism covers the positions defined in XML, only becoming apparent at new initializations. This explains long-standing errors that are difficult to detect through visual inspection during demonstrations. Why Not Before: The “Before” counterfactual reasoning framework reveals the time dependence between technical prerequisites (GPU/pre-trained models), data availability (large-scale datasets), and theoretical foundations (new concepts). Academic achievements highly depend on matching “technical stack maturity” and “time window”—not just individual talent. Users prefer using the Claude Cli backend locally (claude –print) rather than the cloud API with keys. This preference must be remembered when making future gadget/research invocations. Repeated authentication failures should trigger questions about backend assumptions. Award recognition based on LLMs has basic recall limits without real-time API integration—training data cannot keep up with conference papers. A hybrid approach is needed: (1) LLM screening candidates; (2) verification using DBLP/OpenReview APIs; (3) manual resolution of conflicts. Paradigm Shifts in the Robotics Field Between 2016-2023: Deep Reinforcement Learning (2016-2019) → Large-Scale Datasets for Cross-Perspective Performance (2020-2022) → Vision Language Models-Based Models (since 2023). Each period lasted only 2-3 years; researchers working during these transitions had disproportionately significant impacts. Golden Rule of Hot Path Optimization: Read paths assume resources exist, write paths create them. DiskCache mkdir has been moved from read operations (frequently called path_for) to write operations (rarely called put()), resulting in a 10-fold efficiency improvement. This rule applies to all persistent layers such as file systems, databases, and caches. Background Bash tasks in Claude Code do not inherit from conda init; ‘conda run -n ENV’ or absolute paths (${CONDA_PREFIX}/bin/python) must be used. In non-interactive sub shells, directly calling conda activate causes a CondaError. Structured output verification should be separated from the generation process—the two-stage “generate then fix” pattern is more suitable for complex nested patterns than single perfect outputs; this makes it possible to have an unknowable verification layer. Restoring Behavioral Group Abstraction: Classify 29 types of errors based on required motion primitives (re-grasping, retrieval, retraction, redirection, rearrangement), rather than classifying them by type. This allows sharing data within the same group—this is more practical than collecting data separately by error type. Dialogue Summary MIHD Space Transcriptomics ✅ Cross-Sample Embedding Diagnosis: Basemodels are Crucial 2026-03-16 | claude_code A control experiment was conducted comparing 5 embedding methods used for cross-sample retrieval. The results showed that the embedding spaces generated by PCA/STAIG for each sample were incomparable (with a hit rate of 14-29%). User insights into zero-sample constraints suggest that basemodel joint training methods are no longer applicable. scGPT Gene FM achieved a 100% hit rate (average SL@50=0.416), while UNI2 Vision FM performed poorly due to H&E batch effects. A comprehensive visual process was generated (40 PDF files: 44MB per method, 35 files per layer, totaling 6.3MB). The documents were formatted with letter-sized pages and a five-panel layout. Bilingual diagnostic documentation was provided, with PNG images of embeddings proving the incomparability of embedding spaces using control variable methods.
Error Recovery Benchmark Test ✅ Complete Process: Data Collection Plan, Error Fixing, Scene Generation, Recovery Implementation 2026-03-16 | claude_code Multi-stage project: (1) A behavioral group classification method was designed to reduce 29 subtypes to 5 RBGs. Using MimicGen enhancement technology, the number of manual demonstrations was reduced from 1,740 to 329 (an 81% reduction). (2) Git archaeology was used to fix Coordinate Error in CompositeBodyObject, restoring _relative_to_corner logic—this error was hidden for 3 months under HDF5 replay mechanism. (3) The v5 scene generation task was started on Slurm (job number 49363, GPU node an46): more than 14,500 scenes were generated across 5 tasks, with progress tracked using tqpm. The process included 4 levels. (4) A complete recovery process was implemented: 8 modules (recovery_types, segmenter, collection/augmentation/conversion), 3,544 lines of new code, and 34 tests—all 139 tests passed. This process was designed for SpaceMouse remote operations and suitable for Phoenix MCM + Diffusion Policy training formats.
VLA-RoboTwin ✅ Evaluation of Manual Operations in Videos 2026-03-16 | claude_code The 4-layer reasoning chain was modified to overlay model predictions on evaluation videos: pi0.py returned sample actions (actions, {“manip_progress”: progress}), policy.py’s infer() function unpacked the tuple, pi_model.py stored last_infer_result, and deploy_policy.py used cv2 to draw white text and black outlines in the upper left corner. Support for 1-dimensional (“prog: 0.420”) and 2-dimensional (“prog_L: 0.420 prog_R: 0.350”) display formats was provided. Users confirmed that the last layer of MLP did not have a sigmoid function, so they decided to test the current design before adding constraints.
CALVIN Dataset Converter ✅ rlds→lerobot Data Format Conversion 2026-03-16 | claude_code The existing calvin→rlds and calvin→lerobot scripts were integrated into rlds_to_lerobot.py. tfds.builder was used to load the RLDS dataset, and field mapping was applied to traverse steps (observation.image→observation.images.top, etc.), adding frames to LeRobotDataset. The task scope was clearly limited to code integration, not environment verification.
Gadget Research Analyzer ✅ Academic Performance Analysis System Development: Over 30 Researchers Participated in Analysis, Verification, and Optimization 2026-03-16 | claude_code The complete development cycle included: (1) Batch execution of trajectory analysis by over 30 researchers (Chelsea Finn, Sergey Levine, Yuke Zhu, Ruoshi Liu, Yiran Chen, Benjamin Eysenbach, P. Tokmakov, Yunzhu Li, etc.), generating structured JSON files containing trajectory summaries, breakthrough points (combined with why_not_before analysis), research topics, and methodological evolution. (2) Identified systematic issues: name ambiguity accounted for about 30% of errors (Yan Yang published 140 papers in medical/computer vision/physics fields, Yiran Chen had collisions in oncology/chip architecture, Fan Chen had conflicts in chemistry/computer science). The recurrence rate of award IDs was approximately 0% (12/13 queries resulted in nothing), and the instability rate of Chinese JSON format was 20% (6 out of 30 outputs needed fixing). (3) Five rounds of environment debugging: activating conda tools → using conda run commands → installing common software packages → injecting PYTHONPATH into programs → switching to –api backend mode (based on user preference from anthropic to Claude Cli). (4) Code quality review using the /simplify function revealed 6 issues (missing StudentCandidate import, duplicate SHA-256 hash values, DiskCache mkdir operations in hot read paths, wasted LLM homepage calls, redundant Phase 2 co-author analysis, mathematical module scope issues). (5) The performance analysis method confirmed that “technical stack maturity + time window” was a key success predictor; trends in the robotics field were identified (deep imitation mode from 2016-2019 → cross-modal dataset mode from 2020-2022 → VLA base model era after 2023).

summary: The work on the embedding of essential models for space transcriptomics has been completed. A error recovery process was designed and implemented, resulting in a data reduction of 81%. Key flaws in the robosuite coordinate system were also fixed. Large-scale scenario generation tasks have been initiated. In collaboration with over 30 researchers, academic analysis tools have been systematically developed.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-16

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W11 (2026-03-09 ~ 2026-03-15) This week, six main tasks were advanced simultaneously on three devices: DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop. ① MIHD spatial transcriptomics discovered fundamental flaws in the sample-crossing embedding methodology (independent processing per section leads to non-comparable feature spaces), and repairs were initiated. ② ErrorRecoveryBenchmark improved from bug fixes to a full-scale implementation with 13 skills/29 subtypes, overcoming the problem of objects not falling during Drop skills. After identifying limitations in the online quota architecture, a new offline injection approach was established. ③ VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 completed the entire process from environment setup and training performance optimization (JAX version alignment + 33% speed improvement) to data collection for new variables and auxiliary task experiments. ④ The gadget toolchain upgraded its architecture to MCP Server+common/shared packages, with research profilers achieving priority student discoveries on scholar homepages. ⑤ CalendarPro completed a comprehensive optimization in 7 stages, passing all 230 tests with a 40-60% reduction in token consumption. ⑥ The gadget research toolchain integrated citation graph analysis and completed depth profiles of 7 embodied AI scholars.
Weekly Overview Indicator Value Date Range 2026-03-09 ~ 2026-03-15 Active Days 6 / 7 Total Conversations 29 Projects Involved 19 Completed Tasks 36 Ongoing Tasks 10 Total Tokens 309,110,118 Total Cost $227.47 Average Daily Cost $32.50 Project Progress VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 (Active for 6 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Successfully converted 50 episodes of RoboTwin to LeRobot format (11459 frames) Diagnosed a 33% difference in training time between pi05 and openpi, upgraded 6 key dependencies such as JAX 0.5.0→0.5.3, reducing training time from 20h to 15h Completed full-chain repairs for the eval.sh environment: updated torchvision to 0.22.1, set CUDA_HOME from source code to recompile curobo to solve ABI incompatibility Implemented 5 new data variables for Place Dual Shoes (manip_progress_time/distance_left/right, target_endpose, target_joint), using a post-processing architecture with move() and backward patch pickle to handle future state dependencies Designed and implemented four sets of auxiliary experiments to predict operation progress across six files (last_token vs special_token × time vs distance), adding a stop_gradient isolation strategy and ProgressConfig configuration switches Fixed missing regex configurable issues in CheckpointWeightLoader, pi0.py LeRobot shape squeeze problems, and normalized training step 100 action_loss/aux_loss curves Blocked Items:
⚠️ Four auxiliary experiments were blocked due to the LeRobot dataset not containing new fields. The dataset needs to be re-converted ⚠️ The eval.sh default checkpoint_id=5000 path does not exist and needs to be corrected to a valid value (15000/25000/29999) ErrorRecoveryBenchmark (Active for 4 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Fixed two critical bugs: loss of return values from monitor.update() and taxonomy tag mapping, re-labeling 1029 historical scenarios Overcome the problem of objects not falling during Drop skills: ran 15 physical steps using mujoco.mj_step() to bypass OSC controller interference Fixed 5 systematic failed skills (drop three skills + grasp_misalignment+trajectory_regression+wrong_object), with all 105 unit tests passing E2 Drop was split into 3 independent skills according to the recovery strategy (drop_in_transit/drop_at_wrong_place/drop_with_interaction), with the benchmark expanded to 13 skills/29 subtypes Fixed a silent failure bug in Stack name parsing, generated MP4 demonstrations for 11 demo skills, and completed v4 code archiving Completed v5.1 architecture planning (InjectionEngine refactoring + speed limit + human example collection pipeline), determining a recovery training milestone starting before April 1st v5 full runs produced 231 scenarios and MP4s; the first round of D0 generated 207 scenarios Blocked Items:
⚠️ There is still a gap of 600 scenarios in the D0 generation, with 5 root causes needing re-validation ⚠️ The kinematic tree structure for coffee machine components (lid suspension, base deviation) issues are not yet diagnosed ⚠️ Work on implementing the offline injection architecture for v5.1 has not begun MIHD (Spatial Transcriptomics) (Active for 3 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Completed the RM-IDEAL benchmark across 151673↔151508 samples, showing a positive correlation between Layer_1/5 (r≤0.66), and a negative correlation between Layer_3 revealing specificity of fusion embedding layers Implemented the CrossModalEnhancer module (building spatial neighbor KV sequences + symmetric InfoNCE), passing 3-mode tests on CPU side Bypassed the big tensor bug in RTX 2080 Ti cuBLAS by projecting to hidden_dim and then aggregating neighbors + mini-batch contrast loss Review of scGPT literature confirmed that zero-shot is inferior to PCA/scVI, providing strategic evidence for gene encoder selection Completed a major restructuring of MIHD output directories (all 14+ file path references updated) Discovered fundamental flaws in the sample-crossing embedding methodology and initiated repairs using the raw_shared intersection of HVG intersections (1137 genes) as a baseline Blocked Items:
⚠️ The MIHD STAIG embedding module was completely zeroed out (model collapse), GPU retraining failed due to PyTorch 2.9.0+PyG CUDA conflicts, and visualization across slices was blocked ⚠️ The diagnosis of raw_shared embeddings is still ongoing, and the complete GPU pipeline evaluation of CrossModalEnhancer has not been completed gadget Toolchain (Active for 2 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Encapsulated 9 MCP tools using FastMCP+capture_stdout+asyncio.to_thread, refactored to a content return mode (save parameters control writing) Enhanced the research_scout logging system (RotatingFileHandler with dual output), adding zero new dependencies to bioRxiv/PubMed Created 6 common/modules eliminating about 500 lines of duplicate code, unified 6 path constants in paths.py, and simplified .gitignore to a single line outputs/ Implemented Homepage-Based student discovery (4-stage strategy: homepage priority + co-authorship supplement), completing depth profiles of 7 embodied AI scholars Integrated research_scout.py into a unified CLI entry point (profile/citations subcommands), integrated Semantic Scholar citation graph API, and added Hugo research section Blocked Items:
⚠️ The deployment of the profiles for 7 scholars in Hugo is not yet complete ⚠️ The problem of LLM generating long Chinese format JSON quotes has not been fully resolved CalendarPro (Active for 2 days) — ✅ Completed Completed:
Implemented the gadget integration layer (ResearchScoutTool+DailySummaryTool+conda run across environments), automatically triggered at 8AM/11PM daily, with all 13 unit tests passing Completed a comprehensive optimization in 7 stages (confidence threshold, mixed routing, prompt simplification + Chinese token correction, exponential backoff, configurable scheduling weights, automatic threshold tuning, ThoughtStore memory caching), fixed 4 real misclassification scenarios, reducing token consumption by 40-60%, and all 230 tests passed UniVLA/CALVIN Evaluation (Active for 2 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Completed the analysis of CALVIN dependency chain (identified 4 issues), found that the evaluation was purely online simulation, and extracted eval-only files (1.3GB→600KB) Added –single_gpu mode to bypass torchrun/DDP, fixed multiple hard-coded paths, and installed braceexpand dependencies Blocked Items:
⚠️ The full evaluation script has not been verified, and is still in the iterative debugging stage Key Tasks ✅ CalendarPro 7-stage comprehensive optimization (2026-03-15) — Implement semantic routing confidence thresholds, mixed routing (Dense 70%+Keyword 30%), prompt simplification (530 lines→base+11 fragments)+Chinese token correction (×1.5/word), exponential backoff retries, configurable scheduling weights, automatic threshold tuning, ThoughtStore memory caching, fixed 4 real misclassification scenarios, reducing token consumption by 40-60%, and all 230 tests passed ✅ gadget research toolchain CLI integration+citation graph+depth profiles of 7 scholars (2026-03-15) — Integrated paper scout and researcher profiler into a unified CLI with research_scout.py, added Semantic Scholar citation graph API (automatic analysis of top 5 papers using citation analysis), completed depth profiles of Mingyu Ding/Ruoshi Liu/Xiaolong Wang/Shuran Song/Yunzhu Li/Yuke Zhu/Chelsea Finn for 7 scholars, identifying the complete mentor relationship network 🔄 ErrorRecoveryBenchmark v5 full repairs and scale to 13 skills/29 subtypes (2026-03-15) — Fixed 5 systematic failed skills, E2 split into 3 semantically independent skills, completed v4 archiving, v5 full runs produced 231 scenarios, the first round of D0 generated 207 scenarios (target 600), and completed v5.1 architecture planning (InjectionEngine+speed limit+human example collection, recovery training starting before April 1st) ✅ gadget common/shared package extraction+outputs/ unified directory restructuring (2026-03-15) — Created 6 common/modules (io/cache/json_utils/llm/hugo), eliminated about 500 lines of duplicate LLM calls and JSON parsing code, unified 6 path constants in paths.py, simplified .gitignore to a single line outputs/, updated 4 CLAUDE.md files ✅ gadget MCP Server design implementation and tool content return refactoring (2026-03-09) — Encapsulated 9 MCP tools using FastMCP+capture_stdout+asyncio.to_thread, refactored from ‘write file return path’ to ‘return complete content+save parameters optional writing’, confirmed the pip install -e.+console entry point distribution scheme, all tools passed verification 🔄 MIHD sample-crossing embedding methodology diagnosis and repair (2026-03-15) — Discovered that per-section independent HVG selection + independent PCA fitting led to non-comparable feature spaces, negating the misconception that ‘PCA is better than STAIG=input features weak’, initiated the raw_shared intersection of 1137 genes as a baseline solution, and discovered layer-specific phenomena in STAIG at Layer_1/5 (SL@50=0.94-1.0) vs intermediate layers with complete failures ✅ pi05 training performance optimization: JAX version alignment+dependency conflict repair (2026-03-11) — By comparing parallel sub-proxies and locating the cause of 33% slower training time through pyproject.toml/uv.lock/wandb logs, identified JAX version differences (0.5.0 vs 0.5.3) as the main reason, optimized with uv override-dependencies to resolve lerobot torch<2.7 conflicts, successfully completed uv lock (305 packages) 🔄 pi05 four sets of operation progress prediction auxiliary experiments design and implementation (2026-03-14) — Implemented manip_progress auxiliary predictions for time vs distance across six files under the JAX/Flax NNX framework, added a stop_gradient isolation strategy and ProgressConfig configuration switches, fixed CheckpointWeightLoader and LeRobot shape issues, and normalized training step 100 loss curves ✅ ErrorRecoveryBenchmark v5.1 architecture planning (2026-03-15) — Refactored ContextReplayEngine into InjectionEngine (directly restoring injection frames sim state, bypassing the VLA assumption without a context window), added motion speed limits, designed a keyboard teleoperation human example collection pipeline, limited data sources to MimicGen demo, and clarified the phased implementation plan from Mar 16-31 ✅ RoboTwin new data variable post-processing collection architecture implementation (2026-03-13) — Used move() followed by backward patch pickle to implement 5 new variables, solved target_endpose/target_joint dependencies on future states, fixed manip_progress_distance negative values (np.clip to [0,1]), pkl2hdf5.py general recursive design requires no modification 🔄 VLA eval.sh environment full-chain repairs (2026-03-12) — Upgraded torchvision 0.22.1+cu126 to fix NMS operator mismatch, set CUDA_HOME to conda targets directory from source code to recompile curobo to solve ABI incompatibility, remaining checkpoint_id=5000 path issue needs correction ✅ gadget Homepage-Based student discovery strategy implementation (2026-03-15) — Implemented homepage_discovery.py module (~200 lines), 4-stage discovery strategy (homepage priority + co-authorship supplement), multi-strategy URL discovery (S2 homepage field+LLM suggestion+-homepage parameter), HTMLParser text extraction, 2MB limit+7-day cache TTL, solving the fundamental problem of S2 co-author analysis being completely ineffective for top scholars Problems and Solutions 1. Drop Skill: The OSC controller actively maintains the EEF position (impedance control) in env.step(), and after the claw is opened, the object is held by a finger and cannot fall freely [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-15) Solution: Bypass the controller and directly set MuJoCo qpos/qvel, then call mujoco.mj_step() to run 15 physical steps for initial separation, followed by standard control cycles
2. MIHD sample-crossing embedding comparison ineffective: per-section independent HVG selection + independent PCA fitting leads to non-comparable feature spaces, the conclusion that ‘PCA is better than STAIG’ indicates a methodological error [MIHD] (2026-03-15) Solution: Use the raw_shared intersection of shared HVGs (1137 genes) and unified processing as the correct baseline. Load directly from the original HDF5 instead of relying on per-section caches (there is an integerization bug in var_names)
3. Stack name parsing silent failure: stack.yaml uses cubeA/cubeB, MuJoCo’s actual name is cubeA_main, _sim_body_name2id returns -1, Python negative index causes all task phase detections to be misjudged as pre_reach [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-15) Solution: Fix the body name segment, add a main/_body0 suffix backtracking logic in sim_body_name2id, and handle failure output by checking WARNING instead of silently returning -1
4. pi05 training is 33% slower than openpi (20h vs 15h), intuition suggests hardware differences, but the root cause is unknown [VLA-RoboTwin/pi05] (2026-03-11) Solution: By comparing parallel sub-proxies at the software level (pyproject.toml/uv.lock/wandb logs), identify the main reason as JAX version differences (0.5.0 vs 0.5.3). XLA compiler optimization has accumulated over time; use uv override-dependencies to resolve lerobot torch version upper limit conflicts
5. curobo pre-compiled.so and torch 2.7.1 ABI incompatibility (undefined symbol), JIT recompilation failed due to non-standard CUDA header path in conda [VLA-RoboTwin] (2026-03-12)Solution: Set CUDA_HOME to the root directory of the conda environment, set CPATH to targets/x86_64-linux/include/, and use pip install -e to recompile from source. 6. Severe imbalance in online quota generation: premature_release naturally captures 7233 items, with 7 types completely at zero. The distribution of policy behavior is uneven and uncontrollable [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-09) Solution: Establish an offline injection architecture: first collect trajectories during a full rollout, index injectable points offline, then selectively inject according to quotas; skip over已满 types.
7. CalendarPro intention misclassification: no confidence threshold (0.52 is also considered a valid classification), time expressions are misjudged by keyword routing, short confirmation words have no context understanding, Chinese tokens are estimated with a 3-fold deviation [CalendarPro] (2026-03-15) Solution: Add per-routing confidence thresholds (0.40-0.60), introduce a keyword scorer combined with an embedding routing method of 70/30 ratio. Split the system prompt into base and 11 fragments for injection as needed, and estimate Chinese tokens at ×1.5.
8. S2 co-author analysis is completely ineffective for top scholars (Levine/Abbeel/Finn, etc.) (all depth-2 fields are empty). There is a serious name ambiguity issue with Xiaolong Wang/Shuran Song [gadget] (2026-03-15) Solution: Reconstruct the strategy to prioritize homepage-first: preferentially capture student lists from professors’ personal pages, and co-authorship should only serve as a supplement. Use multiple strategies for URL discovery. Mark warnings for name ambiguities and recommend using S2 authorId for precise queries.
9. Incorrect assumption in VLA context replay architecture: a complete N-1 frame replay mechanism was designed, but most VLAs do not have a context window. This overhead is an ineffective design [ErrorRecoveryBenchmark] (2026-03-15) Solution: Reconstruct ContextReplayEngine into InjectionEngine, directly restoring the injected frame sim state. Limit data sources to MimicGen demo data, which is more controllable.
10. RTX 2080 Ti + PyTorch 2.9.0 triggers cuBLAS CUBLAS_STATUS_EXECUTION_FAILED for high-dimensional tensors with N>3500 [MIHD] (2026-03-09) Solution: First project to hidden_dim(128) using Linear projection, then index neighbors (to avoid high-dimensional large tensors entering cuBLAS). Change InfoNCE to a mini-batch comparison loss (batch_size=512).
11. MCP Server tool returns file paths, AI cannot consume the content directly [gadget] (2026-03-09) Solution: Reconstruct the tool to call underlying functions directly without using cmd_* wrappers, returning complete content (markdown/JSON). Control file writing through save parameters.
12. Incorrect assumption in pi0.py regarding LeRobot’s internal behavior: inferred shape=(1,) features are kept in b,1 format and code is modified. In reality, LeRobot DataLoader will squeeze to b, resulting in a mismatch in shape during training [VLA-RoboTwin/pi05] (2026-03-15) Solution: Verify the actual shape by running training and observing logs (‘aux_targets[…]: (32,)@float32’), restore the original[:, None] and jnp.stack operations.
Learning Outcomes Architecture The necessary prerequisite for comparing embeddings across samples is shared feature space: independent HVG selection per section + independent PCA fitting equals dual irrelevance. An effective baseline must use a shared HVG intersection + joint processing, or a foundation model with fixed pre-trained weights. There is a fundamental conflict between direct state operations in MuJoCo and the feedback controller (OSC): sim.forward() only updates kinematics, while mujoco.mj_step() advances dynamics and bypasses the controller. The simulation injection design must clearly choose one path. The semantic split of error types should be based on ‘whether the recovery strategy is the same’ rather than ‘whether the injection mechanism is the same’: the detection conditions and recovery logic for drop_in_transit/drop_at_wrong_place/drop_with_interaction are completely different. Even if the injection actions are the same, they must be modeled separately. The semantic router architecture has a flaw: embedding nearest neighbors always produce results that cannot express ‘uncertainty’. Combining confidence thresholds + alternative LLMs + keyword scorers is the most practical repair model, which can be applied to all vector retrieval-based classification systems (RAG routing, tool selection, etc.). MCP tools should aim to consume AI as their primary goal: return complete content, with file writing being an optional side effect. General benchmarks should not assume that the model has a context window. InjectionEngine directly restoring the sim state is more versatile than context replay. For top scholars (500+ papers), analysis of co-author frequencies cannot identify students - the first-author signal is diluted by many collaborators. Professors’ homepages explicitly list students, which is a factor of reliability. The citation graph (forward + backward) is a core feature of research toolchains. ‘Relevance’ and ’number of citations/popularity’ should be handled independently. Offline injection architecture is better than online quota systems for building balanced error scenario datasets: decouple ’exploring injectability’ from ’executing injections’, allowing precise control over the number of each type of error; online natural capture is greatly affected by policy behavior distribution and cannot control type balance. Debugging Upgrading to a new JAX version (0.5.0→0.5.3) can improve training speed by about 33%. The cumulative effect of XLA compiler optimization cannot be ignored. In the conda environment, compile CUDA extensions: set CUDA_HOME to the root directory of the conda environment, and set CPATH to envs//targets/x86_64-linux/include/ (not /usr/local/cuda/include/); after upgrading torch to a new version, all .so files that depend on torch C++ ABI need to be recompiled. Assumptions about the internal behavior of third-party frameworks must be verified through actual running: LeRobot automatically squeezes single-scalar feature into batch_size for DataLoader when shape=(1,) is inferred. This inference is unreliable. The actual effective values in training configuration need to be verified from wandb logs. Code default values may be overridden by CLI parameters. Monitoring GPUs within K8s containers: scan/proc//fd/ for devices linked to/dev/nvidia*, and prioritize reading CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. Processes that open all GPU devices but do not occupy memory are usually monitoring tools, which can be filtered based on this information. Silent failures are the most dangerous bug mode: body_xpos[-1] negative index always returns the same position in two cubes; integerizing cached var_names results in zero intersection of gene names. Any parsing failure should immediately trigger a WARNING instead of returning a sentinel value. Perform integrity checks before using cached data. Domain Knowledge scGPT independent benchmark (Genome Biology 2025) confirms that zero-shot is inferior to PCA/scVI; scGPT-spatial only compares with weak baselines (ARI≈0.30-0.40). SOTA (GraphST, ARI≈0.55-0.63) was not included, and there is no independent third-party verification. When evaluating new methods, it is necessary to verify whether their baseline is the current SOTA. CALVIN evaluation is a purely online simulation that does not read episode data at all. Only validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml is needed. The 1.3GB dataset can be compressed into a 600KB eval-only version. A list of humanoid AI scholars and their mentors: Mingyu Ding←Jitendra Malik, Ruoshi Liu←Carl Vondrick, Xiaolong Wang←Abhinav Gupta, Shuran Song←Thomas Funkhouser, Yunzhu Li←Antonio Torralba, Yuke Zhu←Li Fei-Fei - presenting a systematic group of top-level perception/robotics mentors to humanoid AI. Stream matching is becoming the mainstream action decoding architecture for VLA. Pi0 time convention: t=1 for pure noise → t=0 for target action. Using adaRMS to inject time conditions in Pi0.5 is better than simple splicing. In VLA-assisted tasks, isolating the main task gradient with stop_gradient is a safe starting point. Tools Prompt engineering injection strategy: Split the system prompt into base (~50 lines) + intent-specific fragments (dynamically injected by category), which can reduce token consumption by 40-60%. The token density for Chinese characters is about 6 times that of English characters (1.5 tokens/character vs 0.25 tokens/character). Without correction, the context length will be systematically underestimated. For projects with multiple tools coexisting, the output directory should be organized according to ‘file type priority’ (outputs/reports/summarize/, not summarize/reports/). The.gitignore can be simplified to a single line outputs/. The Python re-export shim mode (only containing from x import y; all=[…]) is an elegant migration method that remains backward compatible. The PubMed esearch→efetch two-step E-utilities API can index metadata of subscription journals such as Nature/Cell/Science for free. The bioRxiv API is also open, and neither requires new dependencies (urllib.request). Verifying the feasibility of a pipeline with a small batch is better than using a full load. Notes on AI Usage Effective Patterns:
✓ Parallel sub-proxies accelerate multi-dimensional code analysis: rely on version differences for diagnosis. Start 3+ sub-proxies simultaneously during code library exploration to cover different file sets, significantly reducing analysis time. ✓ Goal-driven delegation + iterative debugging loop: Users provide clear termination conditions (‘fix until no errors’). AI runs iteratively independently → reports an error → makes the smallest modification possible. There is an built-in error-correction mechanism. ✓ Deep code library exploration identifies architectural challenges: CrossModalEnhancer actively detects degradation problems in single spots KV (each spot has only one vector), and proposes a solution to build KV sequences from spatial neighbors. ✓ Hack sys.path → progressive refactoring of common/packages: The re-export shim mode remains backward compatible while eliminating duplicate code. ✓ Verifying the feasibility of a small batch pipeline is better than using a full load (207 scenarios reveal 5 systematic defects). End-to-end integration testing reveals implicit dependencies at the pipeline level more effectively than unit testing. Limitations:
✗ Insufficient ability to reflect on experimental conclusions: Directly attribute numerical results without actively questioning the validity of the experimental design. The methodological flaws in MIHD embedding need to be corrected by users through external triggers. ✗ Silent failure modes were not actively detected: Names of Stack bodies returned -1, negative indices in Python, integerizing cached var_names. All these require user discovery due to lack of integrity checks. ✗ Over-engineering and incorrect architectural assumptions: VLA context replay is based on the wrong assumption that ‘all VLAs need a context window’. Incorrect inferences about LeRobot’s shape behavior were made and code was modified, which requires user correction or verification. ✗ Insufficient ability to actively question the applicability of methodologies: S2 students continue to debug the code logic when they discover that it is ineffective. They do not actively question the boundaries of the methodology itself and need guidance from users before turning to the homepage solution. ✗ Weak ability to handle name ambiguities in Semantic Scholar: There is a lack of active entity disambiguation for common Chinese-English translations. LLM analysis cannot automatically identify ambiguous researchers. ✗ The signature was not verified before using APIs: The FastMCP version parameter and conda –no-banner were discovered to be incompatible only after running the program. Next Week’s Plans Next week (2026-W12) focus: ① Implement ErrorRecoveryBenchmark v5.1 - Complete generation of D0 scenarios for 5 fixed skills (targeting 600+ items), progress with the reconstruction of InjectionEngine, speed limit on motion, and collection pipeline for humanoid examples using keyboard teleoperation. Milestone: Start training recovery strategies by April 1st; ② VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 - Re-convert LeRobot dataset (including 5 new fields like manip_progress), start four groups of auxiliary experiments for training and comparative analysis, and conduct formal strategy evaluation after correcting checkpoint_id in eval.sh; ③ MIHD - Complete the diagnosis of the raw_shared baseline and draw conclusions on methodological fixes, solve the problem of 151676 GPUs being retrained (fixed PyTorch version), and evaluate the full GPU pipeline of CrossModalEnhancer; ④ gadget/research - Deploy portraits of 7 scholars to the Hugo research section, and explicitly require English quotation marks in prompts to completely eliminate JSON pollution generated by LLMs; ⑤ UniVLA - Complete the verification process for CALVIN evaluation (in the –single_gpu mode).

summary: This week, six projects were carried out concurrently on three devices: DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop. The projects include the following key tasks: ① MIHD spatial transcriptomics identified fundamental flaws in the cross-sample embedding methodology (independent processing per section leads to non-comparable feature spaces), and efforts are underway to fix these issues. ② ErrorRecoveryBenchmark improved from bug fixes to a comprehensive scale-up, covering 13 skills/29 subtypes. The project also addressed the issue of objects not falling when dropping skills, and after identifying limitations in online quota architectures, a new approach for offline injection was established. ③ VLA-RoboTwin/pi05 completed the entire process from environment setup, training performance optimization (JAX version alignment, 33% speed improvement) to data collection, and auxiliary task experiments. ④ The gadget toolchain upgraded its architecture to include MCP Server, common/shared packages, and unified output directories. Research profilers enabled researchers to prioritize student discoveries on scholar pages. ⑤ CalendarPro underwent comprehensive optimization in 7 stages, with all 230 tests passing. Token consumption was reduced by 40-60%. ⑥ The gadget research toolchain integrated citation graph analysis and completed profiles of 7 renowned AI scholars.
