title: Bug Journal 2026-03-05

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-04 Daily Overview Completed Work: Cross-device multi-project work, including space transcriptomics literature research, document improvements, large-scale VLA training infrastructure setup, and comprehensive error recovery benchmark documentation synchronization. Completion Methods: DCC: Web-based Arxiv paper analysis to extract evaluation methods; MacBook: Codebase analysis using .init commands for document review; Tianhe: Parallel proxy exploration to align codebases with documents, checkpoint inventory through file system traversal, and multi-GPU training coordination planning. Impact: Comprehensive understanding of the cross-sample ecosystem evaluation framework; improved documentation for Desktop Video; laid the foundation for a 6-phase VLA training activity over several weeks; synchronized the error_recovery_benchmark project overview with actual conditions (6,474 episodes, 53 framework files, 22 scripts, 122 tests). DCC Completed Work: Research on QueST paper evaluation methods for space transcriptomics. Completion Method: Accessed and analyzed Arxiv HTML version (v1) and abstract pages (v3) to obtain detailed information about the evaluation framework. Impact: Used WWL Graph Kernel + Wasserstein distance as a benchmark for ecosystem similarity, clarified the evaluation framework, and provided metrics for top-match accuracy and overall similarity accuracy. MacBook Completed Work: Audit and improvement of Desktop Video CLAUDE.md documentation using .init commands. Completion Method: Analyzed codebase structure (Swift/SwiftUI/AVFoundation), identified vulnerabilities in existing documents, and made three targeted edits: fixing capitalization issues in agents.md, adding localization hints for Localizable.xcstrings, and improving the inline documentation of ChangeLog format. Impact: Improved document clarity and completeness, providing accurate file references and bilingual development workflow guidance for future Claude code examples. Tianhe Completed Work: Comprehensive documentation updates for zhaoganlong framework training setup + error_recovery_benchmark, BC-RNN performance survey. Completion Method: Created a 7-task implementation plan for the 6-phase training process, involving 9 MimicGen tasks; deployed 3 parallel exploration proxies for codebase analysis; checked points through file system traversal; systematically edited documentation based on actual implementations; started BC-RNN training and evaluation comparisons. Impact: Laid the foundation for an 8× A800 GPU training activity over several weeks, with checkpoint locations confirmed; documentation aligned with reality, including accurate statistics, detailed API descriptions, and a three-source pipeline architecture; identified anomalies with BC-RNN having 0% success rate that need debugging. Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ zhaoganlong Self-reflection Framework Training Setup and Documentation — Created a comprehensive 7-task implementation plan for all 6 phases (Phase 0-5: data preparation, diffusion strategy, LLaVA MPM, MCM feedback collection, LLaVA MCM, lifelong learning), involving 9 MimicGen tasks, using 49 8× A800 GPUs for training. Recorded complete training methods, including checkpoint inventory: Pi0.5 Phoenix (180GB, 21 steps, last modified on 2026-02-24), base OpenPI model (about 12GB each), diffusion strategy snapshots (4, 253MB each). Started modifying 4 data preparation scripts to enable all tasks. Created with inference pipeline patterns and complete file paths. ✅ Update error_recovery_benchmark Project Overview.md with Actual Implementation — Aligned project overview documentation with actual codebases by deploying parallel proxies: added detailed implementations of 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, 20 error classifiers, along with API descriptions; updated code statistics (scripts increased from 18 to 22, tests from 109 to 122, evaluation runs from 7 to 11); corrected M14 evaluation data (726 episodes → 6,474 episodes, completion rate of about 83%, performance per strategy); corrected VLA model types (Pi0-FAST → Phoenix/Flare); recorded plugin architecture with registration patterns. ✅ Redesign error_recovery_benchmark Core Pipeline Architecture — Updated Section 3.1 pipeline to show three parallel error scenario sources: (1) demonstration dataset replay + injection, (2) VLA/strategy deployment + injection, (3) natural failure capture—all input into ErrorSceneDatabase. Fixed the issue where the original chart only showed paths based on demonstrations. Added explanations of each path’s purpose and complementary relationships. ✅ QueST Cross-sample Query Evaluation Method Research — Accessed QueST paper (arxiv 2410.10652), extracted the complete evaluation framework: used Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman Graph Kernel to build benchmarks from cell type labels, evaluated based on top-1 accuracy of ecosystem matching and overall similarity accuracy (Pearson correlation with WWL benchmark). Synthetic query experiments used 9 queries, varying numbers of cells (1-3) and sizes (50-200 cells). Users corrected to use v3 instead of v1; confirmed v3 exists but no HTML version. 🔄 Investigate BC-RNN 0% Success Rate Anomalies — Users reported that BC-RNN showed 0% SR on all normal tasks, despite expected success rates of about 60-70% during training deployment. Started exploring differences between training and evaluation code (observation keys, environment settings, checkpoint loading), but interrupted the work. Needed to compare deployment of training scripts with benchmark evaluation settings. Users mentioned previous fixes for ‘object’ observation key issues—possibly related. Implementation and Fixes ❌ Review ErrorSceneDatabase Design — Users raised questions about the current database design. Explored actual implementations (meta.json index, scenario JSON + NPZ pair, deduplication, automatic classification, 4 segmentation strategies). Users felt some parts were ‘strange’, but abandoned the discussion before identifying specific issues. ✅ Desktop Video CLAUDE.md Improvement — Analyzed Desktop Video codebase using .init commands, identified three areas for improvement in existing CLAUDE.md, and made fixes: corrected capitalization issues in agents.md, added localization workflow hints (Localizable.xcstrings), improved ChangeLog format documentation, added bilingual entry structures and date format instructions. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Error_recovery_benchmark Documentation does not match code: Project Overview.md describes outdated pipelines, lacks implementation details, and statistical information is inaccurate (726 episodes vs. actual 6,474 episodes) Solution: Deployed parallel exploration proxies to thoroughly check error_framework/, scripts/, configs/, outputs/ directories, then systematically updated documentation with verified data: implementations of detector/injector/validator/classifier, actual M14 evaluation numbers, real code statistics. Key Insight: In active projects, document drift is inevitable—a systematic alignment process between code and documentation is needed. Parallel proxy exploration mode works well for large codebases: one proxy per main directory, then integrating findings.
2. BC-RNN has only a 0% success rate on all regular tasks (such as coffee, stack, thread), despite training results showing performance of about 60-70% Solution: Started exploration but not completed: needed to compare (1) observation dictionary keys between training and evaluation, (2) environment initialization differences, (3) correctness of checkpoint loading, (4) consistency of action space. Users mentioned previous fixes for ‘object’ observation key issues—possibly related.
Key Insight: Mismatches between training and evaluation are common failure modes. BC-RNN had a 0.28% success rate on error recovery tasks (5/1800 successes), but only a 0% success rate on regular tasks (0/250), indicating environmental or observation data mismatches, not strategy defects. A systematic comparison list is needed: observation keys, environment configuration, action boundaries, camera settings, standardization.
3. zhaoganlong’s data preparation scripts only enabled the coffee_d1 task (other 8 tasks were commented out), which hinders multi-task training Solution: Systematically uncomment the 9 tasks in the TASK_MAPPING dictionary, remove pdb.settrace() debugging breaks in 4 scripts to avoid interrupting batch execution.
Key Insight: The existing codebase was designed for single-task prototypes; to scale to multi-task production environments, task lists need to be enabled in data pipelines, checkpoint paths, and orchestration scripts.
4. Flowcharts do not reflect users’ multi-source vision: Only shows error injection based on demonstration, lacks VLA deployment injection and natural failure capture paths Solution: Rewrote Section 3.1 flowcharts into three parallel paths (demonstration injection, strategy deployment injection, natural failure capture), all converging to ErrorSceneDatabase. Added explanations of each path’s purpose and how they complement each other.
Key Insight: Visual charts must align with users’ mental models, not just code structure. Users’ concerns about ‘VLA deployment injection during execution’ indicate that the code supports this feature, but the documentation omits it—missing documentation is as bad as incorrect documentation.
5. arXiv paper PDF version returned binary-encoded content, making it impossible to extract method details directly Solution: Switch to the HTML version provided by arxiv.org/html/2410.10652v1, which provides complete technical details.
Key Insight: For arXiv papers, the HTML version is easier for machines to read than the PDF version; always check the availability of the HTML version first.
6. zhaoganlong’s framework checkpoints are scattered across multiple locations (Pi0.5 Phoenix alone occupies about 180GB), making it unclear which ones are usable Solution: Conducted a comprehensive file system search: found Pi0.5 Phoenix checkpoints (21 steps, total capacity 180GB, last modified on 2026-02-24), base OpenPI checkpoints (pi0_base, pi05_base, pi0_fast_base, pi0_libero, each about 12GB), and diffusion strategy checkpoints (4 snapshots, 253MB each, dated 2025-08-22). Confirmed all paths and sizes, and marked the availability status.
Key Insight: A checklist of checkpoints is crucial for reproducibility. Large checkpoint directories (180GB) indicate that many training steps are saved—this is beneficial for analysis, but clear documentation is needed to specify which steps are used for evaluation.
General Issues 7. Users initially asked “How does this paper handle cross-sample queries,” which was vague—possibly referring to methods or evaluations Solution: Users clarified that they were referring to evaluation methods, not implementations. Re-curated papers focusing on evaluation metrics, real value construction, and experimental design.
Key Insight: Questions about the “how” of papers may imply (1) technical implementation or (2) verification/valuation methods—always clear which aspect the user wants.
8. QueST v3’s HTML version returns a 404 error when accessed directly Solution: v3 is only available in PDF format (no HTML rendering). Use v1 HTML for detailed content extraction, then check the v3 abstract page for version differences (no substantive differences mentioned).
Key Insight: Not all arXiv paper versions have HTML rendering; if subsequent versions only have minor formatting changes, then v1 HTML may be sufficient.
9. Desktop Video CLAUDE.md contains lowercase ‘agents.md’ references, while the actual file name is ‘AGENTS.md,’ which can lead to file not found errors in case-insensitive file systems Solution: Fixed the reference, using the correct uppercase ‘AGENTS.md’ as the file name.
Key Insight: References to document files must be strictly case-sensitive to ensure reliability across different file system configurations (macOS is case-insensitive by default, but can be set to be case-sensitive).
Human-AI Methodology Strategic Level Multi-source Design Pipeline Architecture Role Method Human The user immediately realized that the flowchart for a single source (demonstration dataset) missed their core vision: they wanted to clearly show VLA deployment injection and natural failure capture as the same error scenario sources. They saw that the code supported this (), but the documentation omitted this AI The AI initially focused on recording the content explicitly stated in the README/notes, treating demonstration-based injection as the main path. It did not infer from code capabilities what the user wanted to emphasize about multi-source architecture Difference Analysis: Humans perceive system-level problems as “how errors can be generated?” while AI sees them as file-level issues: “What does this script do?” Humans use domain knowledge to detect anomalies and infer possible root causes (natural error scenario diversity requires multiple sources). AI’s systematic exploration, although comprehensive, is slower—it will eventually find the problem, but does not prioritize high-priority failure modes. This shows that domain expertise is valuable during debugging.
Distinguishing Implementation from Evaluation in Paper Analysis Role Method Human When the AI first provided implementation details, the user immediately clarified “No, I mean how is the evaluation conducted?” AI The AI initially treated “How does this paper handle cross-sample queries” as a question about technical methods (graph autoencoders, contrast learning, adversarial training) Difference Analysis: Humans clearly know which aspects are crucial for their research (evaluation framework), while AI provides a comprehensive technical overview. Humans’ clarifications are important in guiding attention to relevant evaluation metrics.
Granularity of Training Coordination Role Method Human The user provided a complete implementation plan, including a dependency diagram for the 6 phases, clear GPU assignments (Phase 1: GPU 0, Phase 2: GPU 1-4, etc.), parallel execution strategies, and tmux session management requirements AI The AI created a TodoList matching structure for 7 tasks and began systematically modifying files one by one, reading all files in parallel before editing them Difference Analysis: Humans pre-designed a complete coordination architecture, considering hardware limitations (6-7 GPUs already occupied), estimating each phase’s timeline (Phase 1: 2-4 days, Phase 2: 4-8 hours), and specifying failure recovery plans (tmux sessions remain running after disconnection). AI followed instructions but did not actively propose monitoring strategies, checkpoint verification, or resource contention handling in multi-day training activities.
Method of Documenting and Aligning Code Role Method Human The user explicitly asked for “Based on the content in the folder.” They wanted detailed implementation descriptions “just like what you just did” (referring to the previous ErrorSceneDatabase API summary), focusing more on the actual code than document statements AI The AI deployed parallel exploration proxies to read all code and then systematically updated documentation. Pattern: Exploration → Verification → Documentation. Used file system commands to obtain actual values (wc -l, du -sh, stat), not trusting document statements Difference Analysis: Both agreed on the importance of using real data, but humans specified an output style (detailed API documentation embedded in overview documents). AI defaults to a shorter update frequency—humans want comprehensive processing to prevent future deviations. This shows that treating documentation as an “executable specification” is effective.
Implementation Level Versatility of Research Paper Versions Role Method Human The user explicitly corrected the statement “Don’t use v1, there’s a newer version with updates” showing awareness of paper evolution AI The AI defaulted to using the first available complete text version (v1 HTML), without checking subsequent versions Scope of Document Improvement Role Method Human Users initiate/init commands to trigger comprehensive code analysis and CLAUDE.md auditing.期望AI can autonomously identify vulnerabilities and propose improvements AI AI analyzed the existing CLAUDE.md and found it “already excellent and comprehensive,” with only three simple targeted fixes, without proposing broader structural improvements Difference Analysis: Humans use.init as a mechanism to discover potential document issues; AI defaults to a conservative “good enough” evaluation, making only obvious corrections (case sensitivity, missing parts), potentially overlooking deeper organizational or integrity issues.
AI Limitations Key Limitations Unable to infer multi-source pipeline architecture from code: Although explicitly supports for policy deployment and natural capture, AI only recorded paths based on demonstrations until the user corrected them. It failed to synthesize system-level architectures from individual script capabilities Proxy timeouts when exploring large codebases: When exploring the zhaoganlong framework, all three parallel proxies timed out (each exceeding 1100 seconds), returning zero tokens and requiring restart. Large directory trees with many files can consume proxy token budgets or trigger tool call limits Not prioritizing high-p probability failure modes in BC-RNN debugging: Started extensive code exploration without immediately checking the most likely culprits (observation key mismatches, issues mentioned by users that needed fixing). Systematic exploration is comprehensive but slow—lacking debugging heuristics AI’s processing of Tianhe data was interrupted. While modifying create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py, the multi-file data pipeline was partially modified. AI did not actively check progress or suggest atomic batch operations (modifying all 4 files and then testing them as a unit) to avoid inconsistent intermediate states Unable to directly parse PDF binary content returned by WebFetch tool. For arxiv papers, it heavily relies on HTML versions or preprocessed text General Limitations Ill-defined queries: “How does this paper do X” may imply technical implementation or evaluation methods. Clarifications should be provided when ambiguity exists Unable to identify design issues in ErrorSceneDatabase without specific user instructions: Users indicated the design felt “strange,” but AI couldn’t infer exactly what was wrong (classification logic, source tracking, query API, architectural design). Clear problem statements are needed—the ability to detect design issues from code is limited Failed to check for updates to paper versions before using v1 HTML. When accessing arxiv papers, the latest version should always be verified AI showed a confirmation bias towards the quality of existing documentation during.init-triggered CLAUDE.md auditing, concluding that the content was well-structured instead of exploring potential vulnerabilities (e.g., lack of troubleshooting sections, unclear dependency versions requirements, incomplete architecture diagrams) Learning Points Key Learnings - QueST Evaluation Framework: Using Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman Graphs to verify synthetic base truth in cell type annotations, then using Pearson correlation coefficients to validate model similarity rankings Multi-task machine learning processes built through incremental prototype design (starting with one task) maintain a dispersed single-task configuration (annotated task lists, hardcoded paths). To scale to production environments, systematic reviews of data preparation scripts, dataset mapping JSON files, checkpoint directories, and evaluation configurations are required Document synchronization pattern: Use parallel exploration proxies (one for each main directory), verify all numbers from actual files (summary.json, file system statistics), then systematically update document content section by section. Don’t trust claims in documents—always check the reality of code and data Debugging checklist for training vs evaluation mismatch: When a strategy performs well in training but fails in evaluation, systematically check: (1) observation dictionary keys, (2) environment initialization parameters, (3) action space boundaries, (4) data normalization, (5) camera rendering settings. BC-RNN had 0% recall on normal tasks and 0.28% recall on error recovery tasks, indicating environmental mismatch rather than strategy failure Niche query evaluation across samples requires: (1) obtaining batch-invariant embeddings through adversarial training; (2) using graph kernels to build base truth data without labels; (3) using dual metrics to evaluate Top-1 accuracy and overall ranking correlation For spatial transcriptomics, Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman graph kernels can convert discrete cell type labels into continuous niche similarity scores by calculating graph-level Wasserstein distances between subgraph representations Large-scale HPC training orchestration requires clear dependency mappings (Stage 0→Stage 1+2 parallel→Stage 3→Stage 4→Stage 5), GPU allocation planning for each stage, and persistent session management (tmux) to ensure continuous operation during multi-day training activities and to handle SSH disconnections Multi-source data collection architecture should be clearly stated in documentation: Generation of error scenarios benefits from diverse sources (for reproducibility demonstrations, for realistic policy deployment, for real-world natural fault capture). Flowcharts must show all sources as first-class citizens, not hidden in script options M14 evaluation results showed baseline performance: 6,474 episodes involving 4 strategies against 649 error scenarios. Random method had recall of 0%, accuracy of 1.9%; BC-RNN had recall of 0.28%, accuracy of 0.3%; Pi0 had recall of 0%, accuracy of 0.2%; Pi0.5 had recall of 0%, accuracy of 0%. Near-zero error recovery rate validated the difficulty of the benchmark and laid the foundation for future improvements (future M15 human restoration data and LoRA fine-tuning are expected to show positive effects) Best practices for checkpoint inventory: Record complete paths, sizes, last modification dates, and availability status of all model checkpoints. A large checkpoint directory (180GB) indicates many saved steps—steps used for evaluation should be noted in documentation. Date-based naming helps track the training timeline, but a clear mapping from steps to dates is required Extensibility of error frameworks: 53 Python files (about 14.7K lines of code), including 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, and 20 error classifiers, covering 3 domains (operation, planning, progress). Through a plugin architecture, scalability was achieved using DETECTOR_REGISTRY, INJECTOR_REGISTRY, VALIDATOR_REGISTRY, CLASSIFIER_REGISTRY. A cooling time of 20 steps for each detector prevents the detection of junk information Practical Experience /init command triggered comprehensive codebase analysis + generation/improvement workflow for CLAUDE.md, aimed at preparing for future Claude Code sessions. Even when CLAUDE.md already exists, this kind of analysis is very effective for document review Conversation Summary MIHD (Spatial Transcriptomics) ✅ Research on Cross-Sample Query Evaluation Methods for QueST Papers 22:21:12.826 | claude_code User requested an analysis of the evaluation methods for QueST papers (excluding implementation details). Accessed arxiv paper v1 HTML and v3 abstracts, extracting the complete evaluation framework: Wasserstein Weisfeiler-Lehman graph kernels built base truth data through cell type labels, evaluated by Top-1 accuracy and overall similarity accuracy (Pearson r value). Synthetic queries differed in number of cells (1-3) and size (50-200). User corrected to use v3 version, confirmed that v3 has no HTML version but is quite comprehensive.
Desktop Video ✅ CLAUDE.md Document Review and Improvement via .init Command 20:56:25.688 | claude_code User invoked.init command to trigger codebase analysis and generation/improvement of CLAUDE.md. AI analyzed the Swift/SwiftUI project structure, read the existing CLAUDE.md files, and deemed it “already excellent,” making three targeted fixes: correcting the capitalization of references in agents.md, adding localization prompts for checking Localizable.xcstrings when modifying code, and improving the ChangeLog format documentation. AI confirmed no Cursor/.github copilot rules and verified the.periphery.yml dead code detection configuration.
zhaoganlong Motion-Based Self-Reflection Framework 🔄 Training Infrastructure Setup and Complete Documentation 22:23:48.001 | claude_code User requested a complete 6-stage training plan for the zhaoganlong motion-based self-reflection framework, which was executed on an an49 (8× A800) with 9 MimicGen tasks. AI created a breakdown diagram of 7 tasks, mapping dependencies between stages (data preparation→Diffusion + LLaVA MPM parallel→MCM feedback→LaVA MCM→lifelong learning), and started modifying 4 data preparation scripts by uncommenting TASK_MAPPING entries and removing pdb breakpoints to complete all 9 tasks. AI also studied the framework directory, documenting the entire training method covering HDF5→5Hz→Speed→LLaVA JSON data preparation, using ResNet18+Transformer+UNet for diffusion strategy training, LaVA MPM/MCM LoRA fine-tuning, and lifelong learning. AI listed all checkpoints: Pi0.5 Phoenix (180GB, 21 steps, 2026-02-24), base model (about 12GB per file), diffusion strategy (4 snapshots, 253MB per batch). Additionally, was created. When modifying create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py, the workflow was interrupted. Initially, three parallel exploration proxies timed out (each exceeding 1100 seconds), requiring a restart.
Error Recovery Benchmark Test 🔄 Comprehensive Document Synchronization and Architecture Redesign 03:04:05.254 | claude_code Multi-session all-day meeting regarding updates to error_recovery_benchmark document. (1) By deploying parallel proxies, the system aligned project panorama_summary.md with actual implementations. Added detailed sections for 6 detectors, 4 injectors, 4 validators, and 20 classifiers, along with API descriptions. Updated code statistics (22 scripts, 122 tests, 11 evaluation runs), M14 evaluation data (6,474 cases compared to 726 recorded), and database statistics (649 scenarios, categorized by error type/phase/severity). Fixed issues with VLA model types and version dates. (2) User requested modification of the pipeline architecture to showcase three-source design: demonstration replay + injection, VLA/policy deployment + injection, natural fault capture. Updated section 3.1 of the pipeline diagram. (3) Users raised questions about the design of ErrorSceneDatabase, discussing actual implementations (meta.json index, scenario JSON+NPZ pairs, automatic classification, deduplication, 4 segmentation strategies), but the conversation was interrupted before identifying specific issues. (4) Investigation into BC-RNN’s 0% success rate began—users reported all normal tasks failing completely, while expected training deployment success rates should be around 60-70%. Started studying differences between training and evaluation code, but stopped midway. Issues such as observation key mismatches, environment settings, and checkpoint loading needed to be checked. (5) Analyzed Pi0.5 batch evaluation results: overall SR was 58.9%. Compared with BC-RNN baseline (0%) and M14 error recovery results (both close to 0%), Pi0.5’s visualization videos showed empty directories—users requested re-generation. (6) Provided methods using scp/sftp/VS Code/WinSCP to download the documentation to local machines.

summary: Key Insight: In active projects, document drift is inevitable—a systematic alignment process between code and documentation is needed. Parallel proxy exploration mode works well for large codebases: one proxy per main directory, then integrating findings.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-04

content:
Issues and Solutions Key Issues SwiftUI .ultraThinMaterial displays a fixed color instead of transparent glass when showing videos.
Solution: Replace SwiftUI materials with NSViewRepresentable to wrap the NSVisualEffectView, and specify .inWindow mode along with clear backgroundColor in CATransaction.setDisableActions(true). Key Insight: SwiftUI’s abstraction layer hides the details of rendering processes. For complex visual effects like window-inner transparency, control at the NS level is necessary. In 151676, all STAIG fusion embeddings result in zero values (embedding similarity is 0, Spearman r is NaN), indicating a complete model failure.
Solution: Add a check for np.allclose(embeddings, 0) in load_fused_embeddings. If this condition is met, print a warning and automatically trigger the --recompute_fusion path to re-load the gene/visual embedding data from the cache and retrain STAIG (300 cycles, taking about 50 seconds on RTX 2080 Ti). After fixing, the embedding results return to normal (64 dimensions with a standard deviation of 1.0). Key Insight: Due to unstable training, the STAIG fusion embeddings saved in the second phase may become zero. Active detection and automatic re-computation are required in the benchmark script to avoid manual intervention. When switching videos after selecting one in NSOpenPanel, the old video is still played.
Solution: Add a mechanism to detect mismatches between bookmark URLs, returning the original URL if there is no match during bookmark parsing, and adding a protection in ensureFileAccess.Solutions: Ensure that FileAccess parses the old video URL as per the bookmark on the screen, rather than requesting a new URL. A protection mechanism has been added to compare the parsed path with the requested path; if they differ, the original URL is returned (this URL has temporary sandbox permissions provided by NSOpenPanel). Key insights: There is a conflict between the bookmarks stored on each screen and the multi-video history; a protection mechanism is needed to detect that the bookmarks point to expired files.
The success rate of the Coffee task BC-RNN was 0%, suspected to be an evaluation error. Solutions: The training logs were checked, confirming that the Coffee BC-RNN training itself achieved an SR of 0% across all cycles (600 cycles). This was not an evaluation error; the training for this task indeed failed.
Key insights: It is always necessary to verify the training metrics before debugging the evaluation process. A zero value downstream may reflect a failure in upstream training.
The an53 node crashed at 08:30, resulting in the tmux session being lost, and task 46798 was executed. Solutions: An investigation was conducted through the sacct log, determining that the crash was caused by resource overload (8 GPUs were used up to 100%, with high CPU usage). The AI operation was ruled out as the cause (the last completion was at 05:07, 3 hours before the crash). The SSH host key mismatch issue was resolved using ssh-keygen -R an53.
Key insights: Slurm reported minimal details of the crash, but there was no root access to the system logs. The “Request node busy” error in the srun command indicated that the node was overloaded, not a command failure. When using Slurm, even without root access, minimal crash information can be obtained.
The CUDA kernel for P100 GPU (sm_60) is incompatible with PyTorch (sm_70+ version required); torch.cuda.is_available() returns True, but asynchronous operations fail. Solutions: Initially, an attempt was made to use try/except to catch the exception of torchzeros(1).cuda(), but since the asynchronous operations on P100 could not capture errors. The user successfully ran it after switching to RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75). Finally, the CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" environment variable was added to force the use of CPU in the script.
Key insights: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks whether the driver and library exist, without checking whether the GPU’s computing power is compatible with the current PyTorch. CUDA operations may fail asynchronously and cannot be caught by Python’s try/except. The most reliable way is to switch GPUs or use environment variables to force the use of CPU.
The JSON generated by the LLM was truncated (…truncated marker), resulting in daily_summary.py failing to parse on 2026-02-14 and other dates. Solutions: The truncated original text was sent as a separate prompt to AI, requesting only the repair/extraction of valid JSON sections; AI successfully restored the complete JSON structure.
Key insights: There is a risk of truncation when the LLM generates very long JSON (especially in scenarios where multiple segments are merged); a truncation detection and automatic JSON repair retry logic should be added during the merging stage, rather than relying solely on manual discovery and intervention.
After the screen saver clock text was updated, there was a 0.2-second afterimage left behind. Solutions: CALayer has an implicit animation of approximately 0.25 seconds for changes in content properties. When updating wrapped blurView.layer?.mask in CATransaction, set setDisableActions(true) to force immediate replacement.
Key insights: By default, CALayer has animations for almost all property changes; explicit transaction control is needed to achieve immediate visual updates.
The BC-RNN strategy evaluation crashed due to “Unable to process data type <class ’numpy.float64>’”. Solutions: Robomimic’s TensorUtils.to_tensor() was unable to handle numpy scalar types. The_to_robosuite_obs was modified, only passing keys that contained array/image values to the robomimic strategy.
Key insights: Robomimic expects a structured obs dictionary containing array values; the numpy scalars returned by robosuite (such as gripper_width_float64) must be filtered before being passed to TensorUtils.
The visualization script failed multiple times: Bash heredoc did not conform to != syntax error, file name prefix did not match (151673_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 and filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5), spatial/data path errors (located in Dataset/DLPFC, not directly in the section directory), load_dlpfc_data() returned a value error (returned 1 instead of 2), read_10x_h5 lacked uns[‘spatial’], resulting in sc.pl.spatial() failure. Solutions: Use an independent script/_viz_rm_ideal.py file instead of heredoc; use glob “*filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5” to find the correct file name; directly load sc.read_visium("/path/to/Dataset/DLPFC/151673") (automatically handling the spatial subdirectory); fixed the issue where the unpacked result of load_dlpfc_data was a single return value; abandoned using load_dlpfc_data and used sc.read_visium to ensure the presence of uns[‘spatial’].
Key insights: Bash heredoc is very sensitive to special character escapes; complex scripts should be written as files. The DLPFC data file names have section prefixes; spatial data is located in Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial instead of {section}/spatial; load_dlpfc_data used read_10x_h5 instead of read_visium, resulting in a lack of spatial metadata; sc.pl.spatial heavily relies on uns[‘spatial’], and must be loaded using read_visium.
Common Issues The screen saver clock text displayed gray ghosting effects on the edges. Solutions: The root cause was that the font weights between ScreensaverClockHighlight (.light) and renderTextMask (.regular) did not match, causing the mask to thicken and exposing the frosted glass edge of hudWindow. This issue was resolved by unifying the font weight to .regular.
Key insights: The visual effect layer must maintain pixel-level alignment; even a slight difference in weights can expose underlying content.
Processing historical data over 13 days required frequent switching of date parameters and manual confirmation of export/merge status, which was both tedious and error-prone. Solutions: Manually execute the daily_summary.py’s export –date YYYY-MM-DD and merge commands every day, handling each date individually, and confirming the integrity of the output files through the file system.
Key insights: The summarize tool lacks a batch processing mode (e.g., –date-range 2026-02-07..2026-02-19 automatically iterates); in the future, support for batch processing and progress recovery mechanisms should be added.
Using relative paths with nohup failed when starting from the home directory instead of the project directory via SSH. Solutions: Change the command from ‘ssh an53 “nohup bash scripts/…”’ to ‘ssh an53 “cd $PROJ && nohup bash scripts/…”’, ensuring that the working directory is correctly entered before the script execution.
Human and Artificial Intelligence Methodology Strategic Level Discovery of Configuration Errors in Pi0.5 Base Model Setup Role Method Human The user noticed that LoRA fine-tuning used pi0.5_libero instead of pi0.5_base as the base model, which was considered a key architectural decision that required verification and possible retraining. Artificial Intelligence During multiple evaluations, artificial intelligence did not actively detect this configuration issue. It focused on monitoring progress and success rates, without questioning the choice of the base model. Difference analysis: Humans used their domain knowledge about model architecture to discover basic training configuration errors. Artificial intelligence ignored this strategic issue while focusing on operational metrics.
Diagnosis of Zero Embedding Issues Role Method Human After discovering that the embedding similarity was 0, immediately asked “Can you show me the visualization results?” shifting from numerical inspection to visual diagnosis; after finding the root cause, requested methods for cross-sample patch queries. Artificial Intelligence Initially attempted a full benchmark test for zero embeddings, only starting troubleshooting when NaN values appeared, without actively requesting visualizations; it was not until the user asked for visualization that a need for spatial heat maps was realized; documentation requires explicit user requests to begin. Difference analysis: Humans actively drove the process from numerical inspection to visualization and then to method summary; artificial intelligence tended to solve individual problems step by step, lacking a global perspective and initiative.
Cause Analysis of an53 Node Failure Role Method Human Humans correctly suspected that AI operations might have caused the failure of an53, requested timeline evidence, and mentioned peer reports about the 11:22 failure time. Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence systematically analyzed Slurm logs (sacct), tracked all srun commands (04:55-05:07, all completed successfully), identified a NODE_FAIL issue at 8:30 (3 hours after AI activity), and diagnosed it as possibly due to resource overload. Difference analysis: Humans used indirect evidence and peer reports to form hypotheses. Artificial intelligence used system logs and timestamps to build a clear timeline, proving that the operation ended 3 hours before the failure.
Design Decisions for Benchmarking Role Method Human Humans explicitly specified testing only the PCA+UNI2+STAIG_FUSION combination, with target chapters being 151673 and 151676. First, they created separate scripts to verify feasibility, then integrated them into subsequent processes. They repeatedly rejected ExitPlanMode, expecting to execute directly when the direction was clear. Artificial Intelligence The initial design covered multiple fusion methods and more chapters; after creating each plan, it tried using ExitPlanMode for approval; due to repeated rejections, it failed to understand the user’s execution style preferences. Difference analysis: Humans made strategic decisions (scope control, priority sorting), while artificial intelligence tended to cover everything comprehensively; humans preferred direct execution modes, and there was a tension between the artificial intelligence’s conservative approval process and the user’s expected execution efficiency.
Batch Processing Strategy for Historical Data Role Method Human Humans knew they needed to generate historical reports for 13 days, manually called the tool to process each date, and continued processing the next date when issues occurred. Artificial Intelligence As a daily report analyst, artificial intelligence passively responded to each individual date request, reprocessing dialogues, extracting information, generating reports each time, without realizing that this was a batch task, nor did it actively suggest workflow optimization. Difference analysis: Humans had a global intention for batch processing and time planning capabilities, while artificial intelligence could only see individual calls; artificial intelligence lacked the ability to actively identify repetitive patterns and suggest automation improvements.
Screen Saver User Experience in Video-Free Situations Role Method Human Users expect the screen saver to remain active without video loading, displaying a black screen and a clock (consistent with the system’s screen saver). Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence initially only saw the screen saver without video as an intentional design; it did not question the user experience assumption until it was explicitly requested. Difference analysis: Humans applied a psychological model from the macOS system screen saver; artificial intelligence followed literal code constraints, without inferring broader user experience expectations.
Judgment of Granularity in Merging Multiple Dialogues | Role | Method | |——|—-| | Human | Users explicitly request to analyze multiple dialogue segments on the same day separately (possibly from different devices or projects), and then merge these segments into a complete daily report to maintain logical clarity. | | AI | AI strictly follows the instructions, analyzing each segment separately before merging them. However, this leads to redundancy in the merged output (repeating similar issues rather than truly summarizing). Humans need to emphasize “merge and refine, not copy verbatim” in their prompts. |
Difference analysis: Humans understand that “merging” is about information aggregation and deduplication, while AI tends to retain all details, resulting in an output that is lengthy. AI needs explicit instructions to achieve the quality of summary that humans expect.
Artificial Intelligence Limitations Key Limitations During multiple evaluations, failed to actively detect configuration errors in the pi0.5 base model (the difference between libero and the base model), until the user explicitly raised this issue. Lack of initiative and global perspective: ① Did not proactively propose a visualization solution, which required explicit user requests; ② Did not proactively suggest writing method papers, which also required user requests; ③ After multiple ExitPlanMode failures, still failed to understand the user’s execution style preferences (direct implementation rather than waiting for approval); ④ After discovering zero embedding issues, failed to actively diagnose the root cause (why the embeddings saved in the second phase would crash). Error in the design of the compute_embedding_similarity function: It incorrectly called compute Embedding_similarity(embeddings_ref, niche_mask_query=None) in run_cross_section_benchmark, with incorrect parameters and confusing logic; it was only fixed after recreating the compute_cross_embedding_similarity function. This indicates a lack of deep understanding of cross-section similarity calculations. Multiple failures in Bash heredoc and path handling: ① Bash heredoc != escaped with != (syntax error); ② Multiple misses of DLPFC file names with partial prefixes; ③ Mistakenly thought that spatial/ was located directly in the sub-directory, not in Dataset/DLPFC/{section}/spatial; ④ Misuse of load_dlpfc_data() resulting in a lack of uns[‘spatial’]. This indicates a lack of understanding of the DLPFC dataset file organization and scanpy API. Error in the CUDA compatibility detection strategy: torch.cuda.is_available() only checks drivers and libraries, not GPU computing power compatibility; try/except cannot catch CUDA asynchronous failures; in the P100 asynchronous failure scenario, multi-layer CPU backtracking was ineffective. This indicates a lack of understanding of PyTorch CUDA mechanisms and asynchronous error propagation. As a daily report analyst, repeatedly called to handle similar tasks (analyzing historical dialogues → generating structured reports), but failed to recognize it as a batch processing scenario, nor did it actively suggest “whether batch processing mode is needed” or “automated scripts” to improve efficiency. The API token expired multiple times during the sub-agent exploration task, hindering investigations of checkpoints and zhaoganlong folders. Manual intervention/log-in was required. Before attempting to resolve the BC-RNN numpy dtype error, multiple debugging iterations were conducted (trying to track backtraces, ssh retries, checkpoint checks), and it was ultimately determined that to_robosuite_obs was the focus for filtering scalar types. When seeing that the SR in the coffee evaluation was 0%, failed to actively check the training logs; before investigating the upstream training failure, a clear issue statement was needed. General Limitations### Codex 模型 输入 输出 缓存创建量 缓存读取量 成本 占比 claude-opus-4-6 27,751 34,899 522,679 6,916,902 $7.7364 73.6% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 36,614 63,117 1,096,922 10,517,205 $2.7751 26.4% Codex 概述 指标 数值 总令牌数 12,713,756 输入令牌数 12,648,899 输出令牌数 64,857 推理令牌数 30,268 缓存读取量 12,095,872 总成本（美元） $3.9926 模型细分 模型 输入 输出 缓存创建量 缓存读取量 成本 占比 claude-opus-4-6 27,751 34,899 522,679 6,916,902 $7.7364 73.6% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 36,614 63,117 1,096,922 10,517,205 $2.7751 26.4% ——- —— —— ——— ———— —— ————- gpt-5.3-codex 12,648,899 64,857 30,268 12,095,872 $3.9926 100.0%
summary: On the DCC platform, the implementation of the MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark test was completed (including design, 781 lines of script code, error fixing, visualization processing, and 459 lines of documentation). Additionally, data from 13 days of historical AI conversation logs was processed using a MacBook, resulting in structured daily reports. The BC-RNN/Pi0.5 evaluation process was also built, and some key model configuration issues were identified on the Tianhe platform. Finally, the interface was optimized, and relevant documentation for the website was prepared.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-03

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-02 Daily Overview Completed Work: Research and development across multiple devices, involving three main projects: MIHD spatial transcriptomics clustering benchmark test (scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiment, prepared 36 visualization charts for the weekly report), critical bug fixes in the Desktop Video macOS application (security-grade bookmark system, screen saver UI redesign), and Error Recovery Benchmark VLA training (monitored 10 concurrent Pi0.5 LoRA tasks, created a list of external dependencies, identified GPU utilization bottlenecks). Implementation Methods: MIHD: System-level pipeline debugging through cached metadata repairs and parallel fusion evaluations. Desktop Video: Analyzing logs (4438 lines) to identify root causes, permission configurations, and impacts on SwiftUI. VLA: Using Slurm cluster management commands (squeue, srun with –jobid to bypass PAM restrictions), performing system-level parsing of dependency files, and mathematical analysis of action segmentation inference patterns. Impacts: MIHD: Identified a fundamental architectural issue where STAIG ignored gene encoder embeddings, affecting all published results; 36 visualization charts were presented during the Monday presentation. Desktop Video: Eliminated 100% of video/image recovery failures after application restart (code error 257). VLA: Provided accurate training progress reports (an49 task completed 38%, remaining about 37 hours; an53 task completed 27%, remaining about 46 hours), created a list of 10 categories with data covering over 65GB, identified the cause of 10% GPU utilization, laying the foundation for future optimization. DCC Completed Work: MIHD scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiment (11 parts × 5 fusion methods = 55 evaluations), prepared a weekly report containing 3 statistical charts and 33 partial clustering visualizations. Implementation Methods: Using pipeline configuration yaml files and phase2_evaluate.py for parallel experiments; generating high-quality charts with matplotlib/seaborn; creating spatial plots for each encoder using visualize_from_cache.py; switching from mclust to kmeans algorithm when high-dimensional embeddings caused program hangs. Impacts: Key scientific discovery: STAIG two-stage fusion used the original HVG matrix instead of gene encoder output → scGPT+UNI2+STAIG = PCA+UNI2+STAIG (ARI 0.546). QFormer achieved optimal scGPT fusion results (ARI 0.370, compared to 0.170 for just genes, a 118% increase). Complete evidence is ready for presentation at the Monday lab meeting. MacBook Completed Work: Desktop Video application: Fixed critical security-grade bookmark vulnerabilities (4 bugs: lack of permissions, errorLog concurrency issues, log spam, double window opening) and implemented time effects for liquid glass screen savers. Implementation Methods: Analyzing logs (879 calls to updateClockLabels, 671 timing logs) revealed that empty permission files caused bookmark creation failures → code error 257 occurred during restoration. Solution: Added com.apple.security.files.bookmarks.app-scope permissions and provided a non-security-grade fallback parsing method, along with lifecycle tracking for activeSecurityScopedURLs. UI aspects: Studied SwiftUI’s .glassEffect() and .mask(), as well as .ultraThinMaterial for glass text effects. Impacts: Eliminated 100% of video/image recovery failures after application restart (affecting all users since version 1.0). Reduced spam by over 1550 entries per session. Implemented liquid glass screen savers that matched macOS’s native design style, although users requested a fully transparent version and reported issues with video switching freezing. tianhe Completed Work: Monitored 10 concurrent Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning tasks on GPU nodes an49 (6 tasks, progress 38.3%) and an53 (4 tasks, progress 26.8%), created a list of 14 categories with data covering over 65GB, and identified GPU utilization bottlenecks in the VLA evaluation process. Implementation Methods: Using Slurm commands (squeue, srun –jobid, nvidia-smi) to bypass SSH PAM restrictions, analyzing training logs and checkpoint directories, performing system-level parsing of task_registry.yaml and benchmark configurations, creating a structured markdown list with path/destination/reference structures, and conducting mathematical analysis of action segmentation inference patterns (50-step segmentation means only 8 GPU calls in 400-step experiments). Impacts: Provided accurate training progress reports (an49 task remaining about 37 hours, an53 task remaining about 46 hours), created a reusable external_dependencies_inventory.md file covering 4 conda environments, 10 datasets, 9 BC-RNN checkpoints, 4 VLA checkpoints, configuration directories, and external tools. Identified the cause of 10% GPU utilization (action segmentation reduced inference to 8 calls per experiment, with GPU work time being 20 seconds/200 seconds), laying the foundation for future batch evaluation optimizations. Fixed critical bugs in MIHD scGPT benchmark test, identified STAIG’s architectural limitations; resolved Desktop Video macOS sandbox permission issues using security-grade bookmarks; monitored large-scale Pi0.5 VLA training process, created a comprehensive list of external dependencies, and identified GPU utilization bottlenecks in the evaluation process.
Tasks Architecture and Strategy - ✅ Fixed desktop video security-grade bookmark system issues — Root cause was empty permission files → bookmark creation failed, leading to code 257 error. By adding bookmark.app-scope permissions and providing a non-security-grade fallback parsing method in restoreFromBookmark/ensureFileAccess, along with lifecycle tracking via activeSecurityScopedURLs dictionary, the problem was solved. ✅ Created an external dependencies list for error_recovery_benchmark — Built a structured markdown list (docs/external_dependencies_inventory.md), listing 14 categories of external dependencies: configuration directories, AI assistant logs, external binary files, environment variables, Python packages, Google Drive rclone paths, external Git repositories, Ollama models, 4 conda environments, 10 HDF5 datasets (about 18.4GB), 9 BC-RNN checkpoints, 4 VLA checkpoints (about 47GB), startup programs, and troubleshooting guides. Updated the project overview.md and added references. ✅ Ran scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiment (5 methods × 11 parts) — Evaluated concatenation, mean, attention, llava_mlp, qformer fusion strategies. QFormer performed best (average ARI 0.370, experimental results were 151676). Confirmed STAIG fusion ignored gene encoders. ✅ Monitored progress of Pi0.5 LoRA training on GPU clusters — Used Slurm commands to check the status of 10 simultaneous training tasks on 49 and 53 nodes, analyzed training logs and GPU utilization, and estimated completion times. Reports showed 38.3% work completed on 49 nodes (remaining about 37 hours), 26.8% work completed on 53 nodes (remaining about 46 hours). 🔄 Diagnosed GPU usage bottlenecks in VLA evaluation process — Identified approximately 10% of GPU utilization issues: Pi0.5 action segmentation reduced inference calls to 8 per 400-step experiment (8 × 2.5 seconds = 20 seconds GPU work/total 200 seconds = 10% utilization). Users requested optimization plans, including research on Pi0/Pi0.5 inference techniques and implementation of batch evaluations, but the task was interrupted before completion. ✅ Fixed scGPT cached metadata and re-evaluated benchmarks — Added cache_version and metadata_json fields to 11 scGPT .npz files, ran phase2_evaluate.py again for gene-only clustering. Result: ARI increased from 0.114 (problematic) to 0.170 (+49%). 🔄 Debugged desktop video transparent bookmarks and video switching freezing issues — Users reported: 1) desire for fully transparent bookmarks (rather than frosted glass), 2) video switching causing freezing. Investigation began through log analysis and SingleScreenView.swift. The session was interrupted before the problem was resolved. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Generated MIHD weekly report visualizations — Created 3 summary charts (method ranking bar charts, partial heat maps, PCA vs scGPT comparison) and 33 partial clustering diagrams (PCA/scGPT/UNI2 × 11 parts). These charts were inserted into the docs/experiments/weekly_report_20260301.md file. ✅ Fixed Desktop Video errorLog() concurrency and log spam issues — Rewrote errorLog() to route it through FileFile.shared.write() (eliminating simultaneous FileHandle writes). Deleted 879 calls to updateClockLabels and 671 calls to startScreensaverTimer’s dlog. Fixed double openMainWindow issue at startup. ✅ Studied and implemented liquid glass screen saver clock — Studied SwiftUI’s .glassEffect() (macOS 26+) and backward-compatible .mask() methods, suitable for macOS 12.4+ versions. Used .mask(), .ultraThinMaterial, and mirror gradient effects to create glass text effects. Font time changed to .thin. Completely removed background cards. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Desktop Video: 100% failure rate of video/image recovery after application restart, resulting in code 257 “Operation Not Allowed” error Solution: Four-step fix: 1) Added com.apple.security.files.bookmarks.app-scope to the permission list (originally an empty dictionary). 2) Added a fallback option in restoreFromBookmark: If withSecurityScope fails, try using options[] to recover old non-security-grade bookmarks, then save as security-grade bookmarks again. 3) Track active URLs via activeSecurityScopedURLs dictionary and release them in clear(). 4) EnsureFileAccess() automatically resolves bookmark issues before file I/O. Key Insight: macOS sandbox bookmark system requires compile-time permission declarations and runtime url.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() lifecycle management. An empty permission list caused bookmark creation failures, leading to a return to non-security-grade bookmarks, ultimately resulting in withSecurityScope recovery failure and code 257 error. Due to concurrent FileHandle race conditions, the 879-line errorLog() never recorded errors, masking the root cause for months.
2. MIHD: STAIG two-stage fusion ignored gene encoder embeddings, making scGPT+UNI2+STAIG equivalent to PCA+UNI2+STAIG Solution: Checked models/Fusion.py and pipeline/runner.py: staig_fusion used staig_gene_feat (from cached raw HVG matrix), not the gene_emb parameter. This was an architectural issue, not a bug. This key discovery was recorded in the weekly report, affecting the interpretation of all STAIG results. Key Insight: Gene encoder (PCA/scGPT) output was only used by non-STAIG fusion methods. For graph-based fusion, like STAIG, pre-trained spatial embeddings did not enter the GCN node features. This explains why scGPT’s performance was poor: it was optimized for cross-organizational generalization, not for maximizing internal dataset variability like PCA.
3. VLA Evaluation: Although GPU utilization reached 100% during training, it was lower during Pi0.5 evaluation (about 10%), resulting in slower evaluation Solution: Identified the root cause: action segmentation architecture reduced inference calls to 8 per 400-step experiment (8 × 2.5 seconds = 20 seconds GPU work/total 200 seconds = 10% utilization). Although the solution plan was interrupted, optimization strategies were requested through network research and batch evaluation implementation. Key Insight: The bottleneck in VLA evaluation wasn’t GPU computing power, but rather stream serialization: action segmentation reduced GPU calls, while each inference cycle included CPU preprocessing, WebSocket I/O, and simulation steps, which accounted for the total time. Using timer-based request collection could improve throughput.
4. Due to PAM slurm_adopt rejecting connections, SSH access was denied on GPU nodes an49/an53, with the error message “No active tasks on this node” Solution: Bypass SSH restrictions using Slurm’s srun command, executing commands directly in the assigned task context: srun --jobid=45628 bash -c 'command' Key Insight: Slurm’s srun with –jobid provided a way to execute commands on allocated nodes, even if PAM blocked direct SSH connections. This was very useful for cluster environments with strict access controls.
General Issues 5. When running background tasks using srun on heavily loaded GPU nodes (an49), timeout occurred, with command response times exceeding 60 seconds Solution: Added –overlap flag and reduced the timeout expectation value. When nvitop was unavailable or too slow, switched to the simpler nvidia-smi. Accepted the fact that commands could be delayed on heavily loaded nodes. Key Insight: When GPU nodes ran workloads with 100% utilization, management commands executed through srun might experience significant delays; designed monitoring scripts should handle timeout issues gracefully and use lightweight fallback commands.
6. MIHD: During visualization, mclust clustering hung indefinitely on scGPT (512d) and UNI2 (1536d) embeddings Solution: In visualize_from_cache.py, replaced the default mclust with kmeans using the –cluster_method flag. High-dimensional data made mclust’s EM algorithm computationally difficult to handle.
Key Insight: Gaussian mixture model clustering (mclust) performed poorly when there were more than about 100 dimensions. For base model embeddings (512d-1536d), kmeans or spectral methods needed to be used for practical operation.
7. Desktop Video log files contained over 1550 spam entries per session (updateClockLabels × 879, startScreensaverTimer × 671) Solution: Removed dlog from updateClockLabels (called once per screen saver period). Moved startScreensaverTimer’s dlog after all early exit protection mechanisms (only logging actual scheduling timer). Also removed dlog in idle timer callbacks.
Key Insight: High-frequency dlog calls in timer callbacks generated huge log files, masking real errors. Logging should be based on state transitions rather than periodic ticks.
Human-AI Approaches Strategic Level MIHD: Recognized that scGPT+UNI2+STAIG should equal PCA+UNI2+STAIG Role Method Human User (Yi Zhang) noticed from pilot results that scGPT spatial embeddings were “similar to PCA” and suspected gene encoders might not have been used in STAIG fusion. AI The assistant initially planned to run all scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments to obtain STAIG ARI, assuming the gene encoder would be utilized. Difference Analysis: Human made assumptions based on domain knowledge (viewing scGPT as a dimensionality reduction tool) before code verification. The assistant simply followed the task instructions (obtaining ARI values), without questioning the fusion architecture. The human-led investigation revealed the actual implementation details.
VLA: Method for analyzing GPU utilization root causes Role Method Human Provided detailed mathematical analysis: Pi0.5 output 50-step actions, 400-step experiments required only about 8 inference calls, with each call taking 2.5 seconds = 20 seconds GPU work/total 200 seconds = 10% utilization, further breaking down CPU and GPU work within each 2.5-second inference cycle. AI The AI might first use analysis tools or log analysis to identify bottlenecks, then derive mathematical models. Difference Analysis: Users derived expected utilization rates by performing mathematical calculations from the architecture perspective, rather than relying on measurement-driven debugging. This shows the user’s deep understanding of VLA inference process structure.
Desktop Video: Diagnosed bookmark errors directly through log archaeology or directly checked code| Role | Method | |——|——| | Human | The user provides symptom descriptions and the location of log files, enabling evidence-based diagnosis. | | AI | The assistant read 4438 lines of log files and identified a “saved non-scoped bookmark” pattern (never a “saved safe-scoped”). It traced back to an empty rights file through code search and then verified the hypothesis. |
Difference Analysis: Log-based debugging revealed runtime behaviors where bookmark creation was always non-scoped, which couldn’t be detected with static code analysis alone. The root cause (empty rights) was invisible in the logs but became clear after identifying the non-scoped pattern.
VLA: External Dependency Documentation Format Role Method Human The user wants a clear and structured list of files, specifying the exact path, purpose, and reference locations within the code base for each external dependency. AI The AI initially created EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES.md, which contains more narrative and documentation about concepts and usage patterns. Difference Analysis: The user realized the need for operational references (which files exist) as well as explanatory documentation (how to use them). The user provided a clear plan with tabular specifications including path/purpose/reference. This indicates that the user has a clear understanding of the different uses of various documents.
Implementation Level Desktop Video: Using Existing visualize_from_cache.py Instead of Writing New Scripts Role Method Human The user interrupted the assistant after it wrote the new visualize_weekly_report.py and said, “Don’t rewrite the script. Use the existing visualize.” AI The assistant created a custom visualization script (visualize_weekly_report.py) using matplotlib/seaborn to generate 3 summary charts. It didn’t check whether the existing script could adapt to this new requirement. Difference Analysis: Humans prefer to reuse proven code (visualize_from_cache.py) rather than creating new scripts. The assistant focuses more on matching with specific specifications rather than consistency within the code base. In both cases, success was achieved: the new script generated summary charts (method rankings, heat maps, comparisons), while the existing script generated charts for each section.
AI Limitations Key Limitations MIHD scGPT experiments failed to challenge task assumptions. When asked to “get scGPT+UNI2+STAIG ARI,” the assistant should have first verified whether STAIG fusion actually used gene encoder embeddings before running the time-consuming 25-minute experiment. Human intuition, such as observing that it’s similar to PCA, is key to discovering architectural insights. Desktop Video debugging: The assistant rewrote errorLog() to fix concurrency issues but didn’t explain why no error logs appeared in the file at line 4438. Concurrency conflicts caused complete log loss, not just corruption. This should have been noted as “Key issue: errorLog is silently failing, all historical errors are missing.” General Limitations Multiple tool calls failed when trying to connect to GPU nodes via SSH directly (an49/an53). The PAM slurm_adopt limitation requiring access mode based on srun wasn’t immediately recognized. Access control mode should have been identified after the first failure, rather than retrying SSH multiple times. The initial EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES.md was more narrative and explanatory rather than a structured list format required by the user. A clear plan with tabular specifications was needed. Clarification questions about document purposes (operational references or conceptual guides) should have been asked before writing. MIHD visualization: The assistant created a new script (visualize_weekly_report.py) but didn’t explore the existing visualization infrastructure first. The Agent tool should have been used to investigate all visualize_*.py scripts before deciding to write new code. The user had to interrupt and restart. Lessons Learned Key Lessons - Basic Model Embeddings (scGPT 512d, UNI2 1536d) Can’t Replace Dimensionality Reduction in Clustering Tasks (PCA 50d). ScGPT Focuses on Cross-Dataset Transfer, Not Internal Differences within Datasets. Architecture Choice (Gene-only or Multimodal Fusion) Is More Important Than Embedding Quality: QFormer Achieves Higher ARI Improvements Than Using scGPT Alone by Learning Gene-Visual Alignment Methods. STAIG’s Graph-Based Fusion Completely Ignores Gene Encoder Output, Instead Directly Using the Original HVG Matrix. The action chunking behavior in VLA models fundamentally changed GPU utilization: Pi0.5 generated 50 steps of action sequences, so an evaluation experiment with 400 steps only required about 8 inference calls (about 20 seconds of GPU time), giving a total of 200 seconds, meaning GPU utilization was around 10%. The bottleneck shifted from GPU computation to pipeline serialization (CPU preprocessing, WebSocket I/O, simulation steps). Using timer-based request collection could improve throughput. macOS Sandbox Security-Level Bookmarks require compile-time permission declarations (in.entitlements file) and runtime lifecycle management (startAccessingSecurityScopedResource/stop). Empty permission declarations silently revert to non-security-level bookmarks, leading to 100% recovery failures. When permissions are missing, the bookmark system has no visible error channels. Log archeology (reading complete log files) can reveal hidden patterns in code: In Desktop Video, “saved non-security-level bookmarks” appeared 20 times across multiple sessions, revealing missing permission information. Concurrent write errors (errorLog FileHandle concurrency) silently corrupted diagnostic information. High-frequency timer logs (879 updateClockLabels calls) masked the real error—therefore, state-based logging was needed instead of counting-based logging. Slurm’s srun with –jobid flag can bypass SSH PAM restrictions by executing commands directly in the allocated task environment (e.g., slurm_adopt), which is important for cluster environments with strict node access controls. When GPU nodes are running workloads with 100% utilization, management commands executed through srun may encounter significant delays (over 60 seconds)—therefore, robust monitoring scripts should be able to handle timeout issues gracefully and use lightweight alternative commands (e.g., nvidia-smi instead of nvitop). External dependency documentation should serve operational purposes (structured lists: path, size, purpose, reference), which are separate from conceptual documentation (explanatory guides). A clear list with exact paths can achieve reproducibility and dependency auditing. For VLA benchmark projects, a comprehensive list includes 14 categories: configuration directories, logs, binary files, environment variables, Python packages, rclone paths, Git repositories, Ollama models, conda environments, datasets, checkpoints, launch programs, and troubleshooting guides. Practical Learnings SwiftUI’s .mask() + .ultraThinMaterial feature can achieve “sandglass text” effects on macOS 12.4 and later versions by using a blur effect for the masked material. This material automatically samples its content below it in the window view hierarchy (video player → material → masked text). A two-layer method (material mask + gradient overlay) simulates the physical glass effect with refraction and mirror highlights. Gaussian Mixture Model clustering performs poorly when more than 100 dimensions are involved. For basic model embeddings (512d-1536d), kmeans or spectral methods are needed for actual operation. mclust’s EM algorithm becomes computationally infeasible in high-dimensional data. Conversation Summary MIHD Space Transcriptomics ✅ scGPT+UNI2 fusion experiments, weekly report preparation, and visualization generation 04:12:09.065 | claude_code The PI requested a report on results involving scGPT+UNI2+STAIG to be presented on Monday. The investigation revealed that STAIG’s two-stage fusion ignored the use of gene encoder embeddings (instead using the original HVG matrix). The scGPT cache metadata was fixed, and the baseline results with only genes were re-evaluated (ARI improved from 0.114 to 0.170). Five fusion methods were tested on 11 parts (connection/mean/concentration/llava_mlp/QFormer). QFormer achieved the best scGPT fusion results (ARI of 0.370). The visualize_weekly_report.py script was created to generate 3 summary charts: method ranking bar charts, part heat maps, and comparison charts between PCA and scGPT. Users were directed to use the existing visualize_from_cache.py to generate charts for each part. A total of 36 visualization results were generated (3 summary charts and 33 charts for PCA/scGPT/UNI2 encoders), with key architectural findings recorded in the weekly report.
Desktop Video Wallpaper 🔄 Fixing security-level bookmark system, redesigning liquid glass screen protectors, and ongoing debugging work 22:35:54.328 | claude_code Users reported an “operation not allowed” error when loading video files after the application restarted. Log analysis at line 4438 revealed: 1) Empty rights files caused bookmarks to always be non-security-level; 2) Concurrent writing to errorLog lost all error logs; 3) There were over 1550 spam records in each session; 4) Opening two windows simultaneously at startup. These issues were resolved by adding bookmark.app-scope permissions, implementing non-security-level fallback parsing, rewriting errorLog to use sharedLogFile, deleting high-frequency log files, and fixing the startup sequence. The screen protector’s clock was changed from a card-based design to a liquid glass effect, using .mask() + .ultraThinMaterial + mirror gradient effects (the text itself is sandglass). Users then requested completely transparent labels (non-sandglass material) and reported freezing issues when switching videos. Investigation started with log analysis and SingleScreenView.swift, but the conversation was interrupted before problems were resolved.
Error Recovery Benchmarking 🔄 Pi0.5 VLA training monitoring, external dependency list, and GPU utilization optimization plan 06:44:17.740 | claude_code Users requested information about the entire GPU cluster’s training status. Data was successfully collected using Slurm commands (using srun –jobid to bypass PAM restrictions), training logs were analyzed, and a detailed report was provided: 6 Pi0.5 LoRA tasks were completed on an49 with progress of 38.3%, remaining time about 37 hours, 7/8 GPUs at 100% utilization; 4 tasks were completed on an53 with progress of 26.8%, remaining time about 46 hours, 7/8 GPUs at 100% utilization. Evaluation status information was also provided (Stack tasks had a success rate of 95-100%, Coffee tasks had a success rate of 5-25%). A comprehensive external dependency list was created (docs/external_dependencies_inventory.md), covering 14 categories, totaling over 65GB: configuration directories, AI assistant logs, external binary files, environment variables, Python packages, Google Drive rclone paths, external Git repositories, Ollama models, 4 conda environments, 10 HDF5 datasets (about 18.4GB), 9 BC-RNN checkpoints, 4 VLA checkpoints (about 47GB), launch programs, and troubleshooting guides. The project panoramic summary.md was updated with reference information. Users described the bottleneck in GPU utilization: due to action chunking, GPU utilization was about 10% (50 steps meant only 8 inference calls were needed for a 400-step experiment, each call taking 2.5 seconds, or 20 seconds of GPU time/200 seconds total time). Users requested an optimization plan, including research on Pi0/Pi0.5 inference techniques and methods for batch evaluation, but the conversation was interrupted before a plan could be delivered.
