summary: Fixed critical bugs in MIHD scGPT benchmark test, identified STAIG’s architectural limitations; resolved Desktop Video macOS sandbox permission issues using security-grade bookmarks; monitored large-scale Pi0.
title: Bug Journal 2026-03-02

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W09 (2026-02-23 ~ 2026-03-01) This week, we continued to work on two main tasks: space transcriptomics (MIHD) and the Error Recovery Benchmark on both DCC and Tianhe clusters. On DCC, we fixed a critical weight loading bug in scGPT, which improved ARI by 44.4%. We also extended the MIHD benchmark to the Visium HD dataset and completed a large-scale code refactoring involving approximately 250K lines of code. On Tianhe, we established the BC-RNN Phoenix baseline training pipeline with 9 tasks running in parallel. We identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in the Pi0.5 evaluation task, and obtained key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation (the learning strategy had an SR of approximately 0% under error scenarios, proving the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning). Additionally, we successfully launched the parallel fine-tuning of Pi0.5 LoRA on 6×A800 with stable performance.
Week Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-02-23 ~ 2026-03-01 Active Days 5 / 7 Total Conversations 19 Projects Involved 5 Tasks Completed 35 Ongoing Tasks 6 Total Tokens 177,973,151 Total Cost $105.52 Average Cost per Day $21.10 Project Progress Error Recovery Benchmark (Error Recovery Benchmark) (Active for 5 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
M14 three-way evaluation (Pi0/Pi0.5/BC-RNN) completed: BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random was the highest, indicating that the learning strategy failed to generalize well under error injection scenarios. This directly proved the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation: The training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task. Complete implementation of BC-RNN Phoenix baseline: Image mode configuration rewritten to match the MimicGen paper. 9 tasks ran in parallel smoothly, and the Stack D0 achieved an SR of 64% after 22 epochs. Fixed defects in Coffee BC-RNN configuration: Added a missing 57-dimensional object observation key, fixing the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR. Completed the BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline: Fixed a bug in the 4-layer continuous image format, generated 11 tip_over scenarios and MP4 visualizations. Successful launch of Pi0.5 LoRA with 9 tasks running in parallel (6×A800, GPU utilization 100%, 77.7GB per card). The evaluation framework was extended to include 5 MimicGen tasks, with a new task registry and unified management of the –task parameter. Evaluation of the Pi0.5 Phoenix 9-task base model: Total SR=4.2% (Stack D0 24%, others ≤2%). Discovery of a database containing 649 scenarios: ~519 compatible scenarios + ~130 natural_* incompatible scenarios. The actual effective evaluation target was ~519. Blocked Items:
⚠️ Initialization issues in the Coffee environment caused objects to be distorted or stuck (simulator bug). This issue was found after visualization of the BC-RNN rollout and remains unfixed. ⚠️ The effect of Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning (compared to the 4.2% baseline) needs verification after training is completed. MIHD (Multimodal Space Transcriptomics Fusion) (Active for 3 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Fixed the critical weight loading bug in scGPT: The omission of self.use_fast_transformer in TransformerModel.init caused key remapping to never execute in load_pretrained(). After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and NMI improved by 33.3%. STAIG fusion RM-IDEAL evaluation led the competition (average r=0.396 for 151673, peak r=0.644 for Layer_3). This proves that multimodal fusion is much more effective than single-modal baseline in capturing spatial niches (avg r≈0.06-0.12). Full support for Visium HD crop10large: 8 files were modified, and 4 path adaptation bugs were fixed. Silhouette=0.343 was achieved end-to-end (vs PCA 0.086). Discovery of VisiumHD barcode mapping (r_big//4 pattern), and comparative visualizations of pathologist annotation, STHD cell type, and STAIG fusion clustering. Comparison experiment between Vision Refine: It was found that scan_cluster refine is beneficial for weak fusion but harmful for strong fusion. qformer+no-refine was the best option (ARI=0.4832). Large-scale refactoring of the repository in 6 phases: 41 files/~250K code and 5.3MB images were removed. 6 functional modules were extracted, and 440 experiments passed after dry-run verification. The bug causing logic reversal in BasicContrastive drop_feature was fixed. Three CPU acceleration implementations for STAIG fusion (vectorization of cdist + GPU-based random number generation + batch forward. Expectation is a 20-50x improvement). Blocked Items:
⚠️ There were issues with objects being distorted or stuck during the initialization of the Coffee environment (simulator bug). This issue was found after visualization of the BC-RNN rollout and remains unfixed. ⚠️ The effect of Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning (compared to the 4.2% baseline) needs verification after training is completed. Key Tasks ✅ Fixed the scGPT use_fast_transformer weight loading bug and re-extracted all embeddings (2026-02-28) — The omission of self.use_fast_transformer in TransformerModel.init caused key remapping to never execute in load_pretrained(). All 17.7% attention weight parameters were randomly initialized. After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and all 186/186 parameters matched. ✅ Identified the root cause of OOM in Pi0.5 full fine-tuning and switched to LoRA architecture (2026-02-23) — It was confirmed that JAX FSDP only divides parameter storage but does not reduce activation memory. The complete training state of Pi0.5 requires ~62GB/GPU on A800 or higher. Switching to gemma_2b_lora+gemma_300m_lora reduced memory to ~22.5GB. Job 46553 ran smoothly at 2.0s/step. ✅ Completed the three-way evaluation of M14 and confirmed key conclusions (2026-02-27) — BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random (0.0199) was actually higher than all learning strategies. This proves that the learning strategy failed to generalize well under error injection scenarios, directly supporting the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. ✅ Cleaned and refactored the MIHD repository in 6 phases (2026-03-01) — In Phase1, 41 dead files (~250K code + 5.3MB images) were removed. In Phases 2-4, the run_benchmark.py shared functions were moved to 6 modules and the bug causing logic reversal in BasicContrastive drop_feature was fixed (>= replaced with <). 440 experiments passed after dry-run verification. ✅ Full support for Visium HD crop10large (8 files modified) (2026-02-28) — The data_loader/clustering/run_benchmark/pipeline modules were modified. Automatic clustering without annotations for HD was added, and HD-specific visualization logic and hd_global configuration sections were added. The coordinate space alignment and double preprocessing issues were fixed. Silhouette=0.343 was achieved end-to-end. ✅ Discovered and fixed compatibility issues with M14 scenarios (2026-02-23) — All three-way evaluations crashed on scene 122. AI analysis using EnvironmentMismatchError found that the 649 scenario database contained two types of environments (~519 compatible + ~130 natural_* incompatible). The collector.py added a try-except for scene-level to fix this issue. There were approximately 519 actual effective evaluation scenarios. 🔄 Launched the parallel fine-tuning pipeline for Pi0.5 LoRA with 9 tasks (2026-03-01) — The train_pi05_benchmark.py was created (5 subcommands). 18 configurations were registered to openpi/config.py. Four launch bugs were fixed (JAX_PLATFORMS, norm_stats path, boolean parameters, W&B proxy). It successfully launched on 6×A800 with 100% GPU utilization and 77.7GB per card. 🔄 Completed the script implementation of BC-RNN Phoenix baseline and launched 9 tasks in parallel (2026-02-28) — The train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py was implemented based on the hyperparameters from the MimicGen paper. Three library-level bugs were fixed (mujoco_py import, MimicGen environment registration, get_bounding_box_half_size inheritance chain). 9 tasks ran in parallel on a single GPU (~2.2GB VRAM per task), with 50 rollouts evaluated every 20 epochs. ✅ Located and fixed the missing object observation keys in Coffee BC-RNN (2026-03-01) — By comparing the actual obs key structure of the HDF5 dataset, it was found that Coffee lacked a missing 57-dimensional object state (Pod/Machine/Holder position + relative pose + hinge angle). Extra_low_dim=[‘object’] was added for all 9 tasks. This fixed the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR. ✅ Identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation (2026-02-26) — It was confirmed that the training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task. Humans could directly identify this obvious issue through a single question, while AI analysis failed to detect it immediately. ✅ Rewritten BC-RNN image mode configuration (5 tasks, consistent with MimicGen paper) (2026-02-26) — The bc_rnn_configs were rewritten from all low_dim to image mode: ResNet18+SpatialSoftmax encoder, CropRandomizer 76×76, RNN hidden_dim 1000, batch_size 16, epoch_every_n_steps 500. This was consistent with the main results in the MimicGen paper. ✅ Achieved three CPU acceleration implementations for MIHD fusion training (2026-02-23) — scipy cdist vectorization replaced O(n²) nested loops (expected a 100-500x improvement). Random number generation was moved to GPU to eliminate the transmission overhead per epoch. QFormerFusion added a batch forward interface (expectation is a 20-50x improvement). ✅ Developed the BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline (2026-03-01) — The bug in the 4-layer continuous image observation access of robomimic BC-RNN was fixed. 11 tip_over error scenarios and MP4 visualizations were generated. ✅ Completed the full evaluation of Section 151673 RM-IDEAL (27 methods × 7 layers) (2026-02-28) — The RM-IDEAL evaluation with 27 different embedding methods was run. 189 three-dimensional panel visualizations were generated. staig_fusion had an average r of 0.396, ranking first (peak r of Layer_3 was 0.644). This quantified the advantage of multimodal fusion. ✅ Extended the evaluation framework to include 5 MimicGen tasks (2026-02-26) — 5 new task YAML configs and task_registry.yaml were created. The –task parameter was added to the 1/3/4 scripts. The observation dimension error caused by _D0 env suffix stripping was corrected from 65 to 37. run_full_eval.sh was rewritten to support multi-task loops. ✅ Completed the full benchmark test of STAIG fusion (11 DLPFC slices) (2026-02-23) — pca+uni2+staig_fusion and none+uni2+staig_fusion were run on 11 DLPFC slices. The UNI2 patch_size compatibility bug was fixed (256×256 → 224×224). 10/11 slices were successful, with an average ARI of 0.546. Section 151676 had known collapses. ✅ Completed the comparison experiment between MIHD Vision Refine and baseline (2026-02-23) — On slice 151673, a full comparison was run for 8 fusion methods with/without scan_cluster refine. qformer+no-refine was the best option (ARI=0.4832). It was found that refine was beneficial for weak fusion (attention +0.086) and harmful for strong fusion (-0.054). ✅ Fixed key bugs in evaluate_mimicgen.py and collector.py (2026-02-23) — The bug causing in-place array modification of quat2axisangle was fixed (using np.clip+copy instead). The collector.py’s scene-level EnvironmentMismatchError catching was fixed. The reproducibility of env.seed() was ensured, and the state dimension 8D was verified by assertion. Problems and Solutions 1. Pi0.5 full fine-tuning requires ~62GB/GPU. JAX FSDP=4 cannot effectively reduce memory. Repeated OOM in [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Switch to the gemma_2b_lora+gemma_300m_lora architecture, which reduces training parameters by ~90% and reduces memory to ~22.5GB/GPU. Job 46553 ran smoothly at 2.0s/step.
2. All three-way evaluations in M14 crashed on scene 122/649: ~130 natural_* scenario xml_hash incompatibilities with the current mimicgen environment caused EnvironmentMismatchError, leading to a complete evaluation process crashing [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Add a try-except for EnvironmentMismatchError in collect_on_scenes() of collector.py. Skip incompatible scenarios and record warnings. Resume the process with –resume. It was confirmed that there were approximately 519 actual effective evaluation scenarios.
3. Training logs had serious distortions due to stdout buffering: Pi0.5 training showed only ~580 steps, while actually reaching step 3000. This led to misjudgment of training termination and unnecessary restarts [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Standardize the addition of PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1+stdbuf -oL in the training script and sbatch submission scripts. For long-term training, use sbatch to submit independent batch jobs and decouple them from the interactive session lifecycle.
4. The success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation was 0%, which was misjudged as a model quality issue [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-26) Solution: It was confirmed that the root cause was that the training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task (Coffee/Stack/Threading/Assembly).
5. Observation dimension mismatch in BC-RNN evaluation (Expected 65, got 37) [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-26) Solution: Add import mimicgen to create env.registry during create_env(). Do not strip the _D0 suffix anymore. Keep all MimicGen observables (the _D0 environment provides additional object state observables).
6. In scGPT, TransformerModel.init omitted self.use_fast_transformer, causing key remapping in load_pretrained() to never execute. This led to random initialization of 17.7% attention weights. When strict=False, this was silently ignored [MIHD] (2026-02-28) Solution: Add a line of self.use_fast_transformer = use_fast_transformer to model.py. After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and all 11 DLPFC slices’ embeddings were re-extracted.
7. WebSocket client always encountered ConnectionRefusedError. Attempting different address formats (localhost/127.0.0.1/0.0.0.0) failed [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-28)Solution: It was found that the http_proxy environment variable was set to 127.0.0.1:10087. The websockets library failed to connect through this proxy. After unsetting all proxy variables at the beginning of run_eval.sh, the connection succeeded. 8. The CoffeeMachineBodyObject lacks the get_bounding_box_half_size() method. An AttributeError was reported during the rollout initialization of the MimicGen Coffee task due to an interface gap between MimicGen and the current robosuite version. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-28) Solution: The entire call chain (coffee_machine.py → CoffeeMachineBodyObject → CompositeBodyObject → MujocoXMLObject) was traced back. The get_bounding_box_half_size() method was implemented in each of the three different levels of base classes.
9. In HD data, the uns[‘spatial’] images appear to be full-sized but their coordinates are in the fullres pixel space after cropping. This mismatch leads to visual misalignment. Additionally, since X has been logged transformed, the pipeline will again perform normalize_total + log1p, resulting in a double transformation. [MIHD] (2026-02-28) Solution: In load_hd_data(), replace uns[‘spatial’] images with cropped_fullres.tif and recalculate the scalefactor. Add a skip_log parameter to preprocess_data(). For HD data with skip_log=True, only perform HVG filtering.
10. The Coffee BC-RNN loss converged normally (-7.66→-15.4), but the SR remained at 0% for a long time. Tasks configured with the same parameters achieved 64% after 20 epochs. This performance is highly similar to that of models with insufficient capacity. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: By comparing the actual obs key structure in the HDF5 dataset, it was found that Coffee requires a 57-dimensional object state (required for multi-stage precise operations). The configuration template lacked this field. Add extra_low_dim=[‘object’] to all 9 tasks and regenerate the configuration.
11. There were four consecutive bugs when accessing image observations with robomimic BC-RNN: The logic for enabling the camera, the checkpoint config in JSON string format, issues with obtaining image resolution, and problems with converting from HWC to CHW formats. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: ① Check the rgb modality from the checkpoint config JSON string (which requires json.loads). ② Read the actual 84×84 size from shape_metadata. ③ Manually perform HWC→CHW transposition and conversion from uint8 to float32/255 normalization (this is automatically handled by ObsUtils.process_obs in standard rollouts).
12. There were four consecutive bugs when starting Pi0.5 LoRA training: ① Setting JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu alone was ineffective. ② The norm_stats path was incorrect. ③ The boolean parameter –overwrite=False was ineffective. ④ Access to W&B network interfaces on HPC nodes was blocked. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: ① Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously. ② The actual path for norm_stats is assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json. ③ Use –no-overwrite instead of –flag=False. ④ Preset WANDB_MODE to disabled.
13. Adapting the pipeline path for VisiumHD HD data: Failure occurred when finding the image path for the vision encoder and loading spatial coordinates during the fusion stage, as it assumed the DLPFC directory structure. [MIHD] (2026-03-01) Solution: Add a cropped_fullres.tif pattern recognition to find_spatial_image(). Pass crop_dir.parent as data_root when calling the vision encoder. In the fusion stage, directly load coordinates from data.obsm[‘spatial’] without looking up the path.
14. When calculating norm_stats, the GPU util value was 0, but the memory was fully occupied. The task did not progress for a long time. The root cause was that PaligemmaTokenizer eagerly loaded all visible GPUs when calling get_config().model. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously to force the CPU mode (norm_stats does not require model inference at all). Add CPU parallelism and sub-sampling (max-frames 10000) to reduce the calculation time from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes.
Learning Outcomes Architecture JAX FSDP only divides parameter storage, which does not affect the loading of the complete model during initialization or forward activation memory. The full training state for Pi0.5 requires ~62GB/GPU, and a single A800 cannot handle full fine-tuning with 80GB. LoRA (gemma_2b_lora) is the only feasible solution for the A800 cluster, reducing the memory requirement to ~22.5GB. Switch to LoRA when encountering the warning “Can’t reduce memory use below 62.46GiB”. The observation keys configured for BC-RNN must match the task complexity: Tasks with multi-stage precise operations like Coffee require a 57-dimensional object state. A single 84×84 image cannot provide sufficient spatial precision. The performance of configuration bugs (normal loss but SR=0) is highly similar to insufficient model capacity. It is necessary to distinguish between them by comparing dataset structures. Vectorization and batching significantly accelerate the process: O(n²) nested loops can be reduced by about 100-500x through cdist matrix operations. Python’s loop iteration can also be accelerated by about 20-50x through batch GPU forward operations. Norm_stats calculations do not require model inference at all. Forcing the CPU mode, sub-sampling, and parallelism can reduce the calculation time from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes. Methodology for restructuring large repositories: Systematically grep to verify all imported dependencies, then execute in the order of “creating new modules → updating references → deleting old files”, and perform a dry-run verification. Before merging functions with the same name but different variants, carefully compare the boundary conditions (>vs>=vs< can lead to completely opposite results). Debugging PyTorch model.load_state_dict(strict=False) will silently ignore non-matching keys. Keyed weights may run for a long time with random values without causing any errors. Production code should actively print missing_keys/unexpected_keys and verify the statistical values of parameters. There may also be similar bugs in the official open-source repository. It is necessary to audit the weight loading path actively rather than blindly trusting it. Key details of robomimic BC-RNN inference: The checkpoint config is stored as a JSON string (which requires json.loads, not direct dict access). When bypassing the standard rollout process, manual HWC→CHW transposition and uint8→float32/255 normalization are required (this is automatically handled by ObsUtils.process obs in standard rollouts). Domain Knowledge Key conclusions from M14: BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random (0.0199) was actually higher than all learning strategies. This directly proves the necessity of LoRA fine-tuning in M15. The 649 scenario database consists of two types of environments: ~519 impulse/augmented compatible scenarios (xml_hash matching) and ~130 natural_* natural capture scenarios (xml_hash different). There were approximately 519 effective evaluation scenarios in M14. All document and target episode numbers need to be updated accordingly. STAIG fusion is overall leading in the RM-IDEAL evaluation (average r=0.396 for 151673, peak r=0.644 for Layer_3). Multimodal fusion has a much better ability to capture spatial niche structures than single-modal baselines (gene/vision average r≈0.06-0.12). The effect of Vision Refine is bipolar: It is beneficial for strong fusion (attention +0.086 ARI), harmful for weak fusion (-0.054 for qformer). Strong fusion already has the ability to handle high-dimensional inputs, and reducing dimensions actually results in a loss of information. Cross-task evaluation is a common pitfall in robot learning: If the training set task does not overlap with the evaluation task, it directly leads to 0% SR. It is necessary to ensure consistency between train/eval tasks during the experimental design phase. The MimicGen _D0 environment provides additional object observables. Removing the _D0 suffix from observations reduces the dimensionality from 65 to 37, disrupting the input consistency during reasoning. VisiumHD coordinate mapping: 2um bin of r_big//4 results in an 8um grid row (similar for col). The visualization size parameters of scanpy spatial are multiples of spot_diameter_fullres. For HD with 8μm diameter (diameter≈7.3), the size should be approximately 4.0. For DLPFC with a diameter of approximately 144, use size=1.0. The Leiden community found that resolution=1.0 is the default clustering strategy for unlabeled HD data (crop10large k=17, Silhouette=0.302). Tools Best practices for Slurm cluster training projects: For long-term training, sbatch should be used instead of srun to avoid session timeout due to SIGTERM. All training scripts should be standardized with PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 + stdbuf -oL. Before starting the job, use fuser /dev/nvidiaX to clean up zombie GPU processes (monitoring tools like nvitop also hold CUDA contexts). MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID corresponds to the physical GPU number, not the logical CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES number. Key configurations for JAX/Pi0.5 training: ① Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously. ② XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION=0.95 is effective for A800 with 80GB (from 61 to 77.7GB). ③ HPC nodes must have WANDB_MODE set to disabled. ④ The argparse boolean flag –no-overwrite cannot be used instead of –flag=False. ⑤ The openpi norm_stats path is assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json. The HTTP/HTTPS proxy environment variable (http_proxy) will be transparently proxied by the Python websockets library to establish WebSocket connections. When debugging local services on HPC clusters, it is necessary to unset the proxy variables. The MimicGen environment registers with robosuite through the side effects of importing mimicgen. It is necessary to explicitly perform this import before calling external tools. It is necessary to trace back the entire inheritance chain and implement methods that are missing in third-party libraries in all base classes. Only fixing the point where it is called most recently will cause other subclasses to still report errors. The pam_slurm_adopt cluster’s GPU nodes do not support direct SSH access. srun –overlap can attach a new step to an existing interactive job to reuse node resources. Robomimic does not have a TTY environment, and will exit with EOFError if it encounters an existing checkpoint directory. It is necessary to completely clean the directory before starting each time. The checkpoint directory is the most reliable evidence of training completion. A SLURM job crashing does not mean that the training has failed. Notes on AI Usage Effective Modes:
✓ Systematic code auditing to identify root causes: By comparing the key values in checkpoints and model state_dicts, it was possible to accurately locate the bug in ScGPT weight loading (just one line of omission). By comparing the actual obs key structure in the HDF5 dataset, a configuration defect in Coffee was discovered independently (without any human intervention). ✓ Complete call chain tracing: Fixed the issue where CoffeeMachineBodyObject lacked a method by tracing the three-level inheritance chain (coffee_machine → CoffeeMachineBodyObject → CompositeBodyObject → MujocoXMLObject). Implement it in all base classes instead of just fixing the point where it is called most recently. ✓ Progressive debugging: Resolved Pi0.5 training OOM issues by analyzing step by step (downgrading dependencies → patching source code → switching architectures → submitting sbatch jobs). ✓ SubAgent parallel monitoring: Monitored multiple long-time experiments through TaskOutput in the background and advanced the different projects across DCC/Tianhe clusters in parallel. Limitations:
✗ Misjudged the memory usage principle of JAX FSDP (thought that FSDP=4 could be reduced to ~16GB). Only switched to LoRA after several OOMs. It was necessary to take immediate action when encountering the warning “Can’t reduce memory use below 62.46GiB”. ✗ Failed to identify the most obvious reason for the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 during diagnosis. Listed secondary factors such as OOM/action clipping first, and was directly questioned by users, hitting the key point. ✗ Lack of active long-term task health checks: Abnormalities such as GPU idling (util=0 but full memory) and processes getting stuck relied on users to discover them actively, rather than AI auditing them. ✗ lacked preventive verification before integrating external tools: Did not check the availability of mclust/rpy2, image format conventions (HWC vs CHW), outbound network restrictions (W&B), and zombie GPU processes before multiple failures were fixed. ✗ Attempted to automatically call ExitPlanMode to proceed after writing the plan several times (was rejected by users 2-3 times). AI failed to understand that a plan is a decision-making checkpoint for users, not an automatic progress signal. ✗ Lacked active verification of outdated dialogue cache information (such as Pi0.5 step 5000 crash records). It was necessary to rely on the actual checkpoint file status rather than dialogue records. Next Week’s Plans Next week’s core tasks: ① Wait and analyze the results of the LoRA 9 task fine-tuning for Pi0.5 (expected to be completed in 53 hours). Compare it with the 4.2% base model baseline to verify the general improvement effect of LoRA on MimicGen tasks. ② Progress the training of BC-RNN 9 tasks to 600 epochs, focusing on the convergence of complex tasks such as Coffee/ThreePieceAssembly D1. ③ Fix the simulator bug in the Coffee environment initialization (object misalignment/crashing), and complete the closed-loop evaluation of Coffee BC-RNN. ④ Start the M15 experiment design: Based on the conclusions from M14 (learning strategy error scenario SR≈0%), plan the data collection strategy for LoRA fine-tuning in error scenarios, and determine the sampling ratio and data volume of error types. ⑤ Expand MIHD: Run multimodal benchmark tests on more VisiumHD slices to verify the statistical stability of STAIG fusion. Also conduct a special investigation into the training collapse of DLPFC 151676 slices (adjust tau/dropout rate/figure construction parameters).

summary: This week, we have been working concurrently on two projects within the DCC and Tianhe clusters: Space Transcriptomics (MIHD) and the Error Recovery Benchmark. On the DCC side, we fixed a critical weight loading bug in scGPT, resulting in an average improvement of 44.4% for ARI tasks. We also extended the MIHD benchmark to the Visium HD dataset and completed a major refactoring of the code involving approximately 250,000 lines of code. On Tianhe, we established a new BC-RNN Phoenix training pipeline with 9 parallel tasks. We identified and fixed the root cause of the task distribution mismatch that led to zero success rates in the Pi0.5 evaluation. This led to key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation: the learning strategy achieved SR≈0% in error scenarios, proving the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Additionally, we successfully stabilized the parallel fine-tuning of Pi0.5 LoRA on 6×A800 hardware.
title: Weekly Summary 2026-W09

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-01 Daily Overview Completed Work: Parallel execution in three computing environments: using HPC for space transcriptomics visualization and code library modernization (DCC); training robotic learning models on supercomputers (Tianhe) and managing cluster infrastructure; enhancing development tools and writing retrospective documents on local workstations (MacBook). Implementation Methods: DCC utilized STHD/STAIG processes to fix dataset compatibility issues and systematically refactored the code through a multi-stage plan; Tianhe used Slurm job scheduling for iterative training debugging and GPU resource optimization; MacBook automated log integration using gadgets/summarize tools and performed planned-driven multi-file collaborative editing. Impact: Successfully unified multiple annotation sources for VisiumHD analysis with 100% match rate; transformed MIHD from a monolithic structure of 136K to a modular architecture; recorded the complete development history for February through 28 automated reports; achieved a production-ready robotic learning baseline (BC-RNN reached 98% on Stack, Pi0.5 evaluation was 4.2%), with 6 GPUs running in parallel and memory utilization reaching 95%; released Desktop Video 4.1 version, fixing critical playback issues, and adopting macOS Tahoe native design. DCC Completed Work: Completed VisiumHD crop10large multi-source annotation visualization (100% match rate when pathologist matching was at 8um, STHD was 96.3%, STAIG fusion with Silhouette achieved 0.343); refactored the MIHD code library in 6 stages (removed 250,000 lines of useless code, extracted shared functions, verified integrity), generated job logs for February 28th with over 60 visualization links; switched scGPT from mclust (over 40 minutes per session) to KMeans (only 2 minutes per session). Implementation Methods: Resolved HD dataset path incompatibility issues through 5 rounds of debugging, implemented r_big//4 coordinate mapping for Zenodo annotations, optimized STHD 2um→8um intervals using majority voting, performed step-by-step dependency refactoring (migrating dependencies before removing source files), and verified with pipeline dry-run (440 experiments) and grep checks for remaining references. Impact: Ability to directly compare pathologist expertise with clinical tissue computational predictions; MIHD evolved from a chaotic monolithic structure to a clear modular architecture (model library, tool library, process library); shortened scGPT workflow from 6 hours to less than 3 minutes (360 times faster); established STAIG performance baseline on HD data. MacBook Completed Work: Main devices performed three parallel workflows: batch historical report generation (28 days from February to March), merged workflow optimization (synchronized processing daily), and enhancement of the Desktop Video application (fixed 3 critical playback issues and completely redesigned Liquid Glass user interface). Implementation Methods: Automated multi-device log integration through dailySummary.py merging operations (export→merge→deploy process); modified cmd_merge() function to delegate –sync when no date is specified to _dmmerge_sync_all(); performed planned-driven collaborative editing of Desktop Video (fixed 7 file playback issues, optimized user interfaces for 6 files), and completed comprehensive localization and build verification. Impact: Successfully integrated 25 days of data into structured reports (3 reports incomplete due to output limitations); simplified future batch operations by reducing over 50 manual commands; released Desktop Video 4.1 preview version 0301 with complete PowerSave/PowerSave+/automatic mode functions, and Liquid Glass was upgraded to macOS Tahoe native design (.regular glass, Capsule container, localized format). Tianhe Completed Work: Completed BC-RNN 9 task training (stack accuracy reached 98%, Coffee’s accuracy was 0% due to lack of object observation data); Phoenix Pi0.5 evaluation (overall accuracy was 4.2%, with 450 cases); diagnosed training failure issues, cleaned up 468GB of idle GPU memory, fixed Pi0.5 LoRA training process through 5 rounds of debugging, and archived the v4.17 project structure (mimgen_workspace old code, external dependency index). Implementation Methods: Used Slurm overlapping mode for GPU resource management; monitored training processes through file system polling and TensorBoard; conducted iterative diagnostics on startup scripts (JAX CPU configuration, path mismatch, CLI flags, W&B disable issues); shut down idle VLA servers (Phoenix/Pi0/Pi0.5); increased GPU allocation from 4 to 6, setting XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION=0.95; file archiving based on bash scripts. Impact: Established BC-RNN baseline with stack tasks showing success rates of 44%-98% (verified training process), identified the root cause of Coffee’s 0% accuracy (lack of 57-dimensional object observation keys); obtained complete Phoenix results for 9 tasks; released key GPU resources; achieved 6 GPUs running in parallel with memory utilization reaching 100%, 77.7GB per GPU allocated (95% memory used); systematically organized code library and recorded external dependencies. TzJsDesktop Completed Work: Made minor improvements to the gadget tool: added conversation_summaries field to daily report structure. Implementation Methods: Enhanced structural functionality through code editing. Impact: Enriched historical documents with project-specific dialogue analysis. Cross-device work was extensive: completed integration of MIHD and multi-source annotations, carried out large-scale code library refactoring (cleaned 250,000 lines of code), optimized batch-generated 28 daily report processes, fixed BC-RNN/Pi0.5 training processes, achieved 6 GPUs running in parallel, and released Desktop Video 4.1 version with playback fixes and Liquid Glass interface design.
Tasks Architecture and Strategy - ✅ STAIG Fusion HD Dataset Support — Fixed 6 technical issues (path processing, image patterns, coordinate loading, clustering backtracking, stdout buffering), enabling use of PCA × UNI2 × STAIG fusion process on HD crop10large; generated cluster visualization results with Silhouette=0.343, k=20, and 300 training cycles ✅ MIHD 6-stage Code Library Refactoring — Stage 1: removed 41 useless files (approx. 250K code + 5.3MB images). Stage 2: extracted shared functions from run_benchmark.py into models/staig_utils.py, models/vision_extractors.py, models/Fusion.py, and utils/clustering.py. Stage 3: removed run_benchmark.py and 6 related models. Stage 4: extracted STAIG’s common code, fixed BasicContrastive drop_feature errors. Stage 5: updated 5 documentation files, removed benchmark_config.yaml file. Stage 6: verified process import, model import, performed dry-run (440 experiments), used grep to check remaining references ✅ BC-RNN Configuration Fixes: Adding Object Observation Keys — Modified train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py, added extra_low_dim=[‘object’] for all 9 tasks, resolved Coffee’s 0% SR root cause (lack of 57-dimensional object states, including container/holder positions). Regenerated all configuration files, verified changes, and updated templates and documentation to v4.16 version ✅ Fixing Pi0.5 LoRA Training Process Issues (5 Debugging Cycles) — Resolved startup script problems through iterative debugging: (1) added JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu for norm_stats using only CPU; (2) changed path of norm_stats from checkpoints/ to assets/; (3) changed –overwrite=False to –no-overwrite syntax; (4) set WANDB_MODE=disabled for high-performance nodes; (5) increased GPU array from 4 to 6, adjusted XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION to 0.95 to achieve 77.7GB/GPU allocation. Achieved 6-GPU parallel training at 100% utilization ✅ VisiumHD Pathologist Annotation Visualization and Mapping — Mapped Zenodo pathologist CSV data to 8um intervals using r_big//4 coordinate transformation, achieving 100% match rate (17,502 intervals), generated H&E and annotation dual-panel visualizations, identified 4 spatial regions: tumor (8498), connective tissue (5166), non-tumor epithelial tissue (3826), smooth muscle (12) ✅ Desktop Video Playback Mode Errors Fixed (3 Interrelated Issues) — Fixed issues such as: (1) PowerSave/PowerSave+ not delegating to global evaluation when creating new windows; (2) lack of low-power mode detection in automatic mode; (3) UI mode changes not triggering state re-evaluation. Coordinated editing of 7 files: AppState.swift (playbackMode.didSet), SharedWallpaperWindowManager Swift (delegated to global), AppDelegate Swift (NSProcessInfoPowerStateDidChange observer + isLowPowerModeEnabled), removed unnecessary onChange handlers, updated localization for 5 languages, added ChangeLog entry for v4.1 preview 0301 ✅ BC-RNN 9 Task Training and Evaluation — Monitored the 20th training cycle on coffee_d0/d1, stack_d0/d1, stack_three_d0/d1, threading_d0, three_piece_assembly_d0/d1; first rolling evaluation results showed: stack_d0 at 64%, stack_d1 at 44%, coffee series at 0% (root cause: lack of object observation keys); final result: Stack D0/D1 SR rate was 98%, Coffee D0/D1 SR rate was 0% ✅ Phoenix Pi0.5 Evaluation (9 Tasks × 50 Experiments) — Completed the full MimicGen benchmark test for Pi0.5 checkpoints, 99999th step; overall success rate was 4.2% (19/450), stack tasks showed meaningful learning effects (D0 at 24%, D1 at 12%), Coffee/StackThree/Threading/Assembly accuracy approached 0% ✅ scGPT Clustering Performance Optimization — Switched from mclust to KMeans, reduced processing time for all DLPFC parts to approximately 2 minutes, average ARI=0.1695, NMI=0.2772; speed increased by 360 times while maintaining quality ✅ Implementing Gadget Mergence - Daily Processing — Modified cmd_merge() function in daily_summary.py to automatically process logs daily when using –sync flag and without specifying –date. Checked if args.date was None, then delegated to _dmmerge_sync_all() function, which groups files by date, generates sub-processes for each day, skips existing reports, and finally batches deployment ✅ Desktop Video UI Optimization: Liquid Glass and Duplicate Content Removal — Removed duplicate PlaybackMode selectors and idlePauseSensitivity sliders from GeneralSettingsView; redesigned CombinedGlassClock to match macOS Tahoe interface style (.regular glass, using Capsule/RoundedRectangle, 28pt date/96pt time font, 20pt spacing, removed shadows, removed GlassCompat); unified clock position factor to constant 0.85; implemented localized date format through EEEMMMMd template ✅ GPU VRAM Cleanup (Released 468GB of Space) — Shut down 3 idle VLA servers: Phoenix serve_policy.py (PID 2413737, approx. 407GB, distributed across 7 GPUs, utilization 0%), Pi0 server (PID 2138978, approx. 61GB), Pi0.5 server (PID 2046779, approx. 61GB). Released GPUs 0-6, only GPU 7 is still used for BC-RNN training ✅ Error Recovery Benchmark Project Cleanup (v4.17) — Stages 1-4: archived top miscellaneous files (19MB PDF), outdated documentation (README_V4), old mimicgen_workspace code (v1-v3 framework, over 13 files), temporary logs and pycache; merged VLM tutorial; created README.md, .gitignore, docs/EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES.md; updated CLAUDE.md and project summary to v4.17 version ✅ Finalization Mechanism for Gadget Mergence Phase — Tracked the finalization status of source logs in cmd_merge(); set report._finalized=True when target_date < today and all source logs were finalized; _dmmerge_sync_all() skipped finalized reports; added –force parameter to override checks and pass to sub-process Implementation and Fixes ✅ Batch Generation of February Historical Daily Reports (28+ Dates) — Generated structured JSON/Markdown reports for February 1st, 3rd, 5th, 8th, 9th, 10th, 12th, 13th (×3), 14th (×3), 15th (×2), 16th, 17th (×3), 18th, 19th (×3), 20th, 22nd (×2), 23rd, 26th (×2), 28th, and March 1st. Each merged multi-device log covered MIHD/error-recovery-benchmark/CalendarPro/gadget projects. Generation output tokens for 3 complex dates were limited to 32K ✅ Generating MIHD Work Logs and Visualization Links for February 28th — Created docs/experiments/daily_summary_20260228.md file, containing metrics for 3 main directions (Visium HD support, visual optimization process, performance optimization), measurement tables for 7 experimental groups (A-G), and over 60 PNG visualizations using relative paths for GitHub/Hugo rendering ✅ Improvements to STHD/MIHD CLAUDE.md — Enhanced STHD CLAUDE.md: added missing CLI commands (hdpp, sthdviz), detailed descriptions of the frontline/qcmask/roi/sim modules’ architectures, pdata format specifications, Numba JIT annotations. Enhanced MIHD CLAUDE.md: added examples of visual enhancement models (STEGO/BYOL/SCAN/SpatialGAT), HD dataset command examples, test scripts, cache directory descriptions Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. VisiumHD Dataset Path Compatibility with DLPFC-Directed Processes: Image file mismatches, spatial coordinate loading failures, mclust unable to handle HD interval counting (17,502 intervals vs 4,992 positions on DLPFC) Solution: 5 rounds of debugging: fixed path parsing logic, refactored coordinate loading mechanism, implemented mclust-to-KMeans backtracking for large datasets, added tqdm and PYTHONUNBUFFERED for better visibility. HD crop10large can now complete the entire STAIG process quickly, with speed increased by 360 times (2 minutes vs over 40 minutes/partial processes) Key Insight: DLPFC and VisiumHD differ in scale by more than 3fold; mclust’s EM algorithm cannot handle large datasets (17,502 intervals requires 40 minutes), KMeans provides acceptable quality trade-offs when handling exploratory space transcriptomics (ARI is about 0.17 vs 0.20-0.25)
2. Coffee BC-RNN failed to achieve any success rate across all 600 cycles, while Stack achieved 98% Solution: Users identified the root cause: all 9 task configurations from the same template contained only robot-centered observation data (eef_pos, eef_quat, gripper_qpos), missing 57-dimensional object state information (container/holder positions). Coffee’s multi-stage precise operations required object information; simple tasks could rely on visual processing. This issue was fixed by adding extra_low_dim=[‘object’] to all tasks and regenerating the configuration
Key Insight: Observation space defects have a significant impact on task complexity. Simple tasks can compensate with visual processing, while complex tasks cannot complete them at all. Configurations based on templates need to be customized for each task’s observation data
3. MIHD Refactoring Phase Dependency Order: Pipeline/Modules Imported from run_benchmark.py in Large Numbers, Preventing Direct DeletionSolution: First, perform the second stage (moving shared functions to models/staig_utils.py, models/vision_extractors.py, models/Fusion.py, utils/clustering.py, and updating the import statements for all pipelines). Then, carry out the third stage (deleting run_benchmark.py). Verify the remaining references and pipeline operations using grep. Key Insight: Large-scale refactoring must follow the principle of gradually decoupling dependencies: first migrate dependent functions, then delete source files to avoid intermediate import failures. Topological sorting is very important.
4. The Desktop Video PowerSave/PowerSave+ mode uses a per-screen updatePlayState(for:) logic, resulting in the creation of new windows only affecting the current screen, while other screens continue playing. Solution: Modify SharedWallpaperWindowManager.updatePlayState(for:) to delegate handling for .powerSave and .powerSavePlus cases to AppDelegate.shared?.updatePlaybackStateForAllScreens(), ensuring a global re-evaluation during window events.
Key Insight: When mode semantics require system-level decisions, per-screen state methods must be explicitly delegated to the global evaluator. The dichotomy between local and global needs careful routing.
5. When CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" is set to only CPU, JAX crashes due to “No GPU/TPU found”. Solution: Added the JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu environment variable, while setting CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" to force JAX CPU backend initialization.
Key Insight: JAX needs to explicitly choose the backend through JAX_PLATFORMS, not just mask the CUDA devices. Using only CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES will lead to backend initialization errors.
6. In VisiumHD crop10large comparison, pathologist annotation coordinates (micrometer-level on full slices) need to be mapped to a 8um bin grid. Solution: Through experimentation, it was found that the mapping rule of r_big//4 achieved 100% barcode matching (17,502/17,502 intervals), and this was verified through spatial overlay checks.
Key Insight: VisiumHD’s multi-resolution grid (2um intervals, 4×4 aggregated to 8um) requires integer division coordinate conversion. Zenodo metadata lacks a clear mapping document.
7. The Desktop Video Auto mode has no battery state awareness function, and always uses the PowerSave logic even when macOS is in low-power mode. Solution: Added an observer for NSProcessInfoPowerStateDidChange to applicationDidFinishLaunching; implemented a powerStateDidChange handler; updated shouldPauseAllVideos() to check ProcessInfo.processInfo.isLowPowerModeEnabled: normal power → allOverlaysCompletelyCovered(), low power → anyOverlayCompletelyCovered().
Key Insight: macOS low-power mode can be queried through ProcessInfo.isLowPowerModeEnabled, and observed through NSProcessInfoPowerStateDidChange. Adaptive behavior needs to be handled by combining queries and observations.
8. GPU VRAM depletion - All 8 GPUs were nearly full, resulting in the inability to execute new training tasks. Solution: It was found that Phoenix serve_policy.py (PID 2413737) consumed approximately 407GB of memory on 7 GPUs, but the utilization was only 0% (idle server). The script and two other idle VLA servers (PID 2046779, 2138978, each consuming about 61GB of memory) were shut down. GPUs numbered 0-6 were released.
Key Insight: If not monitored, idle model service processes may monopolize GPU memory for days. Regular use of nvidia-smi for checks and process auditing can prevent resource exhaustion. Multi-GPU models waste resources especially when idle.
9. Training tasks are indefinitely stuck in the wandb.init() proxy error (no proxy configured on the computing node). Solution: Added WANDB_MODE=disabled to the training command environment.
Key Insight: HPC computing nodes usually lack internet access; for batch jobs, disable cloud logging services like W&B, MLflow, Comet, or configure an offline mode.
10. Gadget merging –sync downloaded all logs and processed them in a single batch, with only one warning regarding multiple dates that needed manual iteration to fill in missing data. Solution: Modified the cmd_merge() function to check if args.date was None when args.sync was True, then delegate it to _cmd_merge_sync_all(). This function groups files by date (in YYYY-MM-DD_device.json format), processes each date in a different sub-process, skips dates already reported, and finally deploys them in batches.
Key Insight: Unifying the –sync behavior with –sync-all eliminates confusion and makes batch filling more efficient (eliminating over 50 manual commands for historical data in February).
11. Both Pi0.5 training tasks 46551 and 46553 failed/canceled. Solution: Analyzed the training logs: both tasks were manually canceled using the scancel command. Task 46551 only reached about 579 steps, while the target was 100k steps. The root cause was user intervention, not a system or code error.
Key Insight: Manually canceling tasks and training failures are different - when diagnosing “failed” runs, it’s necessary to distinguish between system errors and manual stops.
12. norm_stats path does not match: The script checks checkpoints//norm_stats/, but compute_norm_stats.py writes data to assets//benchmark/mimicgen_/norm_stats.json. Solution: Updated the skipped checks (step 1) and prerequisite checks (step 2) to use the correct asset/path structure.
Key Insight: The output path of tools doesn’t always match the intuitive checkpoint directory structure; verify the actual output location through dry runs.
13. Bash tool completely failed: All commands (echo hello, true) returned exit code 1 with no output. Solution: The user insisted on a retry (“try again”); the tool eventually recovered on its own. The root cause was unknown, possibly a temporary issue with the sandbox binary or the NFS file system.
Key Insight: Tool-level failures sometimes only require simple retries rather than deep diagnostics. During Bash failures, read/write/Glob tools can still work properly and can serve as a backup for partial tasks.
14. Complex multi-project dates (February 15th, 16th, 17th, and 28th) exceeded the Claude API’s 32K output token limit during merging operations. Solution: No immediate fix needed; accept this limitation. Since the daily workload distribution among devices is natural, most reports remain within the limit.
Key Insight: Daily reports with high activity across multiple devices may exceed the API output limit. Future mitigations include chunk merging, processing by device, or using a hierarchical summary approach (day→week→month).
Common Issues 15. Training scripts had parsing errors for “–overwrite/–no-overwrite: ignored explicit argument False”. Solution: Change –overwrite=False to –no-overwrite (boolean flag syntax instead of key=value format).
Key Insight: Python CLI boolean flags usually use –flag/–no-flag mode, not –flag=value syntax.
Human and Artificial Intelligence Approaches Strategic Level Responsibility for Problem Diagnosis Role Method Human Coffee BC-RNN provided a 0% SR complete diagnostic report: root cause analysis (missing object keys), why Stack was unaffected (simple task visualization could solve it), comparison of dataset object dimensions Role Method AI Claude was responsible for implementing fixes: modifying code to add configuration fields, generating files in batches, verifying the fix results, updating documentation. Did not participate in initial problem diagnosis Difference Analysis: Humans are responsible for advanced problem diagnosis and root cause analysis; AI handles code modifications and batch processing tasks. This division reflects their respective strengths: human’s ability to integrate domain knowledge and AI’s ability to perform batch operations.
Order of MIHD Refactoring Phase Role Method Human The user explicitly stated in the plan that “the second stage must be carried out before the third stage,” and realized that pipelines/modules were heavily dependent on run_benchmark.py, requiring function migration before deleting the source code Role Method AI AI initially created 6 parallel tasks but did not realize the strong dependencies between the second and third stages. Only implemented them in sequence during implementation Difference Analysis: Humans have a global architecture perspective and can foresee execution order limitations driven by dependencies; AI tends to treat tasks as independent units for parallel processing, lacking an understanding of dependencies between modules.
Desktop Video Liquid Glass Aesthetics Goals Role Method Human Requested “closer to macOS native design,” but without specifying. Before深入分析norm_stats calculations that do not require GPU reasoning, a user’s explicit prompt was needed." Difference Analysis: No differences found.
Deterministic Mechanism Design Decisions Role Method Human The user explicitly requested to merge the finalization logic “with the export”: mark only when the date is before today and ensure no additional content is added to maintain idempotence Role Method AI AI implemented a more refined logic: merge _finalized=True requires the target date < today and all source logs have been finalized, reflecting two-stage propagation semantics Difference Analysis: Humans provide advanced requirements (“with the export”); AI refines the implementation with stricter conditions (all sources have been finalized), with reasonable explanations that do not deviate from the topic.
Merging Workflow Behavior Expectations Role Method Human The user expected that the –sync flag could automatically process logs without –date when it was used alone, once a day (consistent with –sync-all behavior), and hoped that a single command could efficiently handle the entire backlog task Role Method AI AI initially implemented –sync to download all logs and process them in one batch, only warning about multiple dates without splitting them." Difference Analysis: Humans prioritize automation and parallel batch processing (one sub-process per day); AI’s original implementation focuses on simplicity (single batch), but at the cost of manual iteration. The user’s method is more suitable for scenarios with a backlog of 10 days or more.
Pi0.5 Fine-tuning Task Execution Order Role Method Human The user explicitly specified to start “from coffee_d0,” demonstrating a step-by-step verification strategy (verifying the feasibility of a single task before expanding to 9 tasks). Role Method AI AI tends to submit all 9 tasks in parallel or plan comprehensively, lacking the experimental discipline to spontaneously initiate from small-scale starts and expansions." Difference Analysis: The user demonstrates engineering pragmatism (reducing risk through step-by-step verification); AI defaults to comprehensive parallelism without considering containment of failure modes.
Tool Failure Response Strategy Role Method Human When the Bash tool failed, the user adopted a simple direct strategy: “Please try again,” “Try again.” Without investigating the root cause, they believed retrying would solve the problem." Role Method AI Claude tried multiple diagnostic methods: tested different commands (echo/true/), attempted dangerlyDisableSandbox, tried different timeout settings, used Read/Write as a backup solution. Preferred understanding the root cause rather than blindly retrying." Difference Analysis: The contrast between human pragmatism and AI’s analytical completeness. In a black-box failure scenario, simple retrying may be more effective than deep diagnosis. However, AI’s backup solutions (using Write to create directories) also have their value.
Artificial Intelligence Limitations Key Limitations - Before explicitly using grep for verification, it was not possible to detect dependency issues in run_benchmark.py - Initially thought that these codes could be deleted after functional comparison. It took 5 separate debugging iterations to fix the Pi0.5 training launcher issue, rather than conducting a comprehensive preliminary analysis of potential failure modes (JAX backend, paths, CLI syntax, network dependencies). During multiple attempts to generate comprehensive daily reports, due to the output token limit reaching 32,000, the API crashed, and no smooth downgrade or automatic chunking was implemented. Initially unaware that JAX needed to set JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu when only CPU mode was supported, not just masking CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES; it was only through repeated experiments that this was discovered. Before the training prerequisites check failed, the script’s expected checkpoint location did not match the tool’s actual output (assets/) path mismatch issue was not detected. No proactive identification of GPU resource usage anomalies. Before深入分析norm_stats calculations that do not require GPU reasoning, a user’s explicit prompt was needed to state “8 GPUs are occupied but the utilization is only 0%.” There was no active multi-metric cross-validation method. When formulating the implementation plan, AI called ExitPlanMode multiple times without user explicit approval, resulting in multiple rejections; lacked patience waiting for an “execute” signal. When users started generating multiple consecutive historical reports, they did not proactively suggest daily batch processing but implemented this function only after a user explicitly requested it. An anticipative approach could identify the filling pattern after 2-3 manual merge commands. Although this is a common cluster configuration mode, it was not foreseeable that W&B network dependencies would cause problems on HPC computing nodes. Over-reliance on Agent exploration. In the project cleanup task, 3 parallel Agents were generated for deep exploration (current file, code merging opportunities, document status), but this was immediately interrupted by the user. The user might have expected direct execution rather than re-planning. Although infrastructure was shared, independent repair plans were initially proposed for 3 Desktop Video playback vulnerabilities, showing the limitations of system-level architecture awareness before explicit exploration. General Limitations When extracting HD patches, it was not possible to see the buffered Python output. The user had to ask “Where are you stuck?” and then add tqdm and PYTHONUNBUFFERED for viewing. When reaching the 32K output token limit during report generation, no alternative strategies (device segmentation, reducing details, chunked output) were provided; instead, the failure outcome was accepted. During monitoring and evaluating progress, a task output polling mechanism with a long timeout (600 seconds) was used, although this process clearly took a long time (50 events × 9 tasks). A more effective approach would be to perform non-blocking checks regularly and use an exponential backoff strategy. Learning Outcomes Key Learnings - mclust probability clustering performance significantly decreased after over 5000 observations (at least 40 minutes for 17,502 intervals); KMeans provided a 360-fold acceleration (2 minutes compared to over 6 hours for 11 parts), while the quality loss was acceptable (ARI was about 0.17 compared to 0.20-0.25) suitable for exploratory spatial transcriptomics analysis.- It is impossible to safely delete the “old” code without a comprehensive dependency analysis using grep. During the transition period, the new modular process usually still imports tools from the old monolithic files. If the new code is imported from these “old” files, they may still be critical infrastructure. Best practices for large-scale refactoring: Analyze dependencies (use grep to check all import statements) → Migrate in topological order (migrate dependencies first, then the items that depend on them) → Delete the source file → Global verification (check remaining references and run tests), ensuring that every intermediate state is runnable. The design of the observation space has a non-linear impact on task performance: Missing object keys can result in a 0% SR for Coffee, but Stack’s simple tasks can still achieve 98%. Different tasks have varying dependencies on observation information; configuration templates cannot be generalized. BC-RNN performs significantly better than Pi0.5 on normal MimicGen tasks (Stack D0: 98% vs 24%), but both perform poorly in terms of error recovery (success rate is approximately 0%). This indicates that cloning behavior from demonstrations works well for tasks within the distribution, but it cannot be generalized to error scenarios without a clear training recovery trajectory. Configuration errors can be a silent killer – Coffee BC-RNN still has no successful cases after 600 cycles of training because the configuration omitted the “object” observation key (57 dimensions of the task’s critical state). Although the loss decreases, the strategy does not learn any useful information. Always verify whether the observation keys match the environment capabilities. VisiumHD’s multi-resolution interval system (native 2 microns, reaching 8 microns through a 4×4 aggregation) requires integer division for coordinate conversion (r_big//4) to ensure consistency with pathologist annotations; the coordinate mapping is empirically determined and not recorded in Zenodo metadata. The HD/VisiumHD dataset requires consideration of dataset path handling throughout the process (visual extraction, gene extraction, fusion, clustering) – it cannot be assumed that the directory structure is similar to that of DLPFC. Different datasets require different path structures; HD uses cropped areas, and non-standard naming requires special handling. The duality between each screen and the global state requires clear routing: When semantics are system-wide, local methods must be delegated to the global evaluator. Multi-file coordinated editing benefits from extraction, centralized orchestration (AppState.didSet → global re-evaluation), and comprehensive positioning. JAX only CPU execution requires specifying CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" and JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously; using the former alone will cause an error stating “GPU/TPU not found”. Long-running operations running on HPC must have visibility (tqtd + PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1) – buffering output can lead to confusion and wasted time checking process status. The final marker has a two-stage propagation semantics: The export stage sets it as_finalized when the date < today and there are no new conversations → The merge stage reads all source logs that are finalized → Merging occurs only when all sources have been completed and the date < today, with the merge report set to true to ensure integrity checks. Multi-device development environments require a systematic documentation workflow; the two-stage export then merge architecture allows for asynchronous log collection from DCC/MacBook/tianhe and centralized integration, which is crucial for HPC cluster settings with limited network access. macOS’s adaptive behavior (response to low-power mode) can be achieved through ProcessInfo queries and NotificationCenter observation. NSProcessInfoPowerStateDidChange can be queried and observed through ProcessInfo.processInfo.isLowPowerModeEnabled. BC-RNN shows task-dependent convergence rates during training on MimicGen data: At 20 cycles, the success rate for stacked tasks reached 44-64%, while Coffee tasks remained at 0% (the increase in difficulty was confirmed); this verifies the completeness of the training process and prepares for VLA comparison experiments. GPU memory leaks on idle model servers are a major operational risk in shared clusters. Phoenix servers consumed approximately 407GB of memory on 7 GPUs, but the utilization rate was only 0%. Automatic monitoring/cleanup or strict resource quotas are required. GPU resource monitoring requires cross-validation with multi-dimensional metrics: High memory usage does not equal high GPU utilization. The Norm_stats data preprocessing steps may consume GPU memory due to model eager loading, but the actual calculations are performed on the CPU. XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION controls JAX’s GPU memory preallocation: The default value is 0.75, which can be safely increased to 0.95 for dedicated training workloads to maximize available memory (from 61GB per GPU to 77.7GB). HPC computing nodes usually do not have direct internet access; cloud log services (W&B, MLflow, Comet) are always disabled via WANDB_MODE=disabled, or batch training jobs are configured for offline mode. Code library cleanup requires a systematic approach: v4.17 successfully cleaned up error_recovery_benchmark by (1) archiving old code (v1-v3 mimicgen_workspace), (2) creating external dependency indexes (checkpoints/datasets/repos), (3) merging duplicate documents (VLM tutorial), and (4) establishing.gitignore rules. The cleaned-up structure is easier to maintain. The completeness of experimental result documentation requires not only code modifications (what/how) but also experimental metric tables (ARI/NMI/Silhouette) and visualization image links (more than 60 PNGs), providing a complete reference for future research. Batch historical report generation benefits from isolated daily processes rather than single batch processing: Independent sub-processes can prevent API errors on a single date from blocking the entire backlog, allowing parallel execution on future multi-core systems and enabling progressive tracking of progress. SwiftUI Liquid Glass follows conventions on macOS Tahoe: Using .regular glass for better readability, using geometric containers (Capsule/RoundedRectangle), reducing shadows, and implementing localization settings through DateFormatter templates (EEEEMMMMd), maintaining consistent spacing. The shared HPC conda environment may point to software installed by any user (openpi05→lipeida/openpi), while configuration files are stored elsewhere (zhaoganlong repository); explicit verification is required before assuming that code configurations are consistent. The Slurm srun –overlap flag allows for executing multiple commands on the same job assignment, without having to start a new job, allowing for step-by-step debugging on reserved nodes. For failures of black-box tools, adopt a pragmatic approach: When Bash tools fail completely and cannot be diagnosed, a simple retry strategy (user’s “try again”) ultimately solves the problem. In a production environment, quick recovery may be more important than conducting root cause analysis. Project documentation maintenance requires strict version tracking: When adding features, it is necessary to update the version number, statistical data (LOC count, file count), list of files, and version history simultaneously. title: Bug Journal 2026-02-28

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-27 Key Points of the Day Completed Work: A comprehensive inventory of storage space in the HDD_POOL directory on the Tianhe cluster was completed. The process for using Slurm GPU nodes was optimized, and the results of the M14 baseline evaluation were reviewed. The success rate for BC-RNN was 0.28%, while the success rates for Pi0/Pi0.5 were both zero. Implementation: The slow scanning speed in the Lustre file system was solved through parallel du scans. Resource checks were performed using sinfo/sacctmgr, and existing JSON results were directly obtained from the evaluation_logs directory. Implications: The results of M14 clearly show that all current strategies failed in error recovery scenarios. This provides a good reason to implement strategy M15 (manual data recovery + LoRA fine-tuning for Pi0.5). A comprehensive inventory of storage space was completed on the Tianhe cluster, and guidelines for using Slurm were established. The results of the M14 baseline evaluation showed that BC-RNN had a success rate of 0.28% (5/1800 cases), while Pi0 and Pi0.5 had zero success rates. Interestingly, the recovery progress of the random baseline was actually higher than all strategies, at 0.0199. All 649 PickPlace error scenarios failed, with significant distribution deviations.
Tasks for Today Architecture and Strategy ✅ Review of M14 Baseline Results — The results of the M14 evaluation were reviewed in the error_recovery_benchmark/outputs/evaluation_logs directory: BC-RNN had a success rate of 0.28% (5/1800 cases), while Pi0 and Pi0.5 had zero success rates. The recovery progress of the random baseline was actually higher than all strategies, at 0.0199. All 649 PickPlace error scenarios failed, with significant distribution deviations. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Guidelines for Using Slurm on the Cluster — Commands available on the tianhexy-a cluster (srun/salloc/sbatch), partition status (ai/xy-a800 has 9 idle nodes), account permissions (QOS: normal/backup/emergency), and key limitations caused by pam_slurm_adopt were identified. These limitations prevent direct SSH access to compute nodes. A complete guide for applying for GPU nodes and connecting to them was created (tmux+salloc is recommended). ❌ Code Modification for ‘Evaluation Training Set’ — The user requested changes to the evaluation from “evaluation on the training set” to “evaluation on the training set,” but such modifications were not found in pi05_phoenix/, scripts/, or configs/. The conversation ended with further clarification by the user, indicating that the modification may have been completed on other devices/sessions. ✅ Comprehensive Inventory of HDD_POOL Storage Space — All user directories under sysu_gbli2xy_1 were scanned. The total size of the entire file system was 7.5PB, with 81% (5.7PB) used and 1.5PB remaining. chenjunye had approximately 5.1TB of space, while tangzijia (user) only had 28GB. Five directories including miniconda3/chenshiyu/cpx1 failed due to excessive file sizes; the total number of files estimated to exceed 2TB. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. All learning strategies (BC-RNN/Pi0/Pi0.5) had almost zero success rates in error recovery scenarios. Solution: This is a expected result, not something that needs fixing. The recovery progress of the random baseline was 0.0199, indicating that these strategies were even worse than random operations in error scenarios.
Key Insight: Error injection pushed the environment beyond the training distribution, causing the strategy to get stuck in local optima. This confirms the need for specialized error recovery training data (goal of M15).
General Problems 2. The scanning speed in the Lustre distributed file system was extremely slow, with multiple user directories failing within 5-10 minutes. Solution: Switch to parallel bash background tasks (each directory runs independently with a timeout and runs in the background), allowing for partial results to be obtained within 120 seconds. For超大目录(VLA_data), use –max-depth=1 to limit the depth of traversal.
Key Insight: Metadata operations in Lustre FS are costly. Combining parallel scans with step-by-step depth limits is the only real-time estimation method.
3. The pam_slurm_adopt mechanism prevents direct SSH access to compute nodes (an53 test returned “access denied”). Solution: First, use salloc to request resources and obtain a JOBID, then use srun –pty bash to enter the node. Only after the task is allocated can SSH access be used.
Key Insight: This is a cluster security policy, not a failure. It is recommended that srun be used as the standard interface for interacting with nodes.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Existence and Interpretation of M14 Evaluation Results Role Method Human The user directly asked “how the evaluation progress is?” This shows that humans have prior knowledge about the evaluation process and only need specific numerical results. AI AI needs to search through the output directory, read JSON files, and then aggregate numbers—it lacks human’s overall project memory capacity. Difference Analysis: Humans maintain a mental model of project progress during conversations. AI can only reconstruct context from the file system. In long-term projects, human knowledge far exceeds that of AI.
Location of Code Modifications for “Evaluation Training Set” Role Method Human The user was sure they requested changes to evaluation to “evaluation on the training set,” and had a clear memory of usage scenarios (pi0.5 and BC-RNN evaluations). AI AI conducted extensive searches but couldn’t find any such modifications. It could only further clarify the issue for the user. Difference Analysis: AI cannot access conversations from other sessions, resulting in situations where users remember changes but AI can’t find them. This exposes the fundamental flaw of AI’s cross-session memory function in multi-device/multi-session workflows.
AI Limitations Important Limitations AI cannot access cross-session conversation history. The user explicitly stated that they had previously requested modifications to evaluations, but AI couldn’t find any such changes in the code. It could only further clarify the issue for the user. General Limitations When dealing with slow scanning speed in Lustre file systems, AI tried more than 5 serial methods, all of which failed. More time was spent trying parallel methods—these should have been judged as ineffective earlier. When GRES is empty, AI didn’t verify whether the GPU really existed. By using nvidia-smi to check the current node, it could only confirm that there was no GPU on the current node; it couldn’t determine the GPU configuration of the compute node. Summary for Today Key Conclusions - BC-RNN and Pi0/Pi0.5 had almost zero success rates in error recovery scenarios (649 PickPlace errors). The recovery progress of the random baseline was higher than all strategies, indicating that specialized error recovery training data is needed for M15. This provides a solid foundation for advancing M15. Practical Takeaways When using pam_slurm_adopt on the tianhexy-a cluster, you need to first obtain a JOBID through salloc before entering the node. tmux+sloc+srun is a standard workflow for stable GPU sessions. The cost of Du scanning in the Lustre distributed file system is very high. Combining parallel background tasks with step-by-step depth limits allows for real-time estimation of the size of large directories. Meeting Summary ✅ Review of M14 Baseline Results: BC-RNN/Pi0/Pi0.5 had no success rate in error recovery scenarios.
05:57:24.382 | claude_code
The user asked about the previous evaluation results for Pi0 and BC-RNN. AI found the complete M14 results in outputs/evaluation_logs/. In 649 PickPlace error scenarios, BC-RNN had a success rate of 0.28% (5/1800 cases), while Pi0 and Pi0.5 had zero success rates. The recovery progress of the random baseline was actually higher than all strategies, at 0.0199. AI believes this proves the need for M15 (LoRA fine-tuning + human data recovery).
✅ Exploration of the Slurm environment on the Tianhe cluster: GPU node applications, connection methods, and stable connection solutions
05:18:36.275 | claude_code
The user wanted to know how to use Slurm on the tianhexy-a cluster and how to apply for GPU nodes. AI found all standard Slurm commands available. The cluster had 9 idle nodes (such as an16/21/23), equipped with ai and xy-a800 GPUs. Currently, there were no available GPUs on the ln207 node. The main finding was that pam_slurm_adopt can prevent direct SSH access; you need to first request resources through salloc, then use srun to enter the node. AI provided a complete guide for applying for GPU nodes and recommended tmux+sloc as a stable connection method.
❌ Attempted code modification to switch Pi0.5/BC-RNN evaluations to training set—failed
07:57:48.382 | claude_code
The user requested changes to “evaluation on the training set” (for pi0.5 and BC-RNN). However, AI couldn’t find such modifications in pi05_phoenix/, scripts/, or configs/. It is suspected that the modification may have been done on other devices or sessions. The user was asked to describe specific intentions (such as seed reuse, training/testing split, etc.). The conversation didn’t lead to any conclusions.
🔄 Comprehensive analysis of HDD_POOL storage space and user directory sizes
04:28:09.784 | claude_code
The user requested an analysis of the usage of all user directories in HDD_POOL. AI confirmed that the overall file system utilization was 81% (total capacity 7.5PB, remaining 1.5PB). It also scanned 23 user directories under sysu_gbli2xy_1. Most user data was obtained: chenjunye had approximately 5.1TB of data, while tangzijia only had 28GB. Five超大目录 (miniconda3/chenshiyu/cpx1/liangzhijia/mozihao) couldn’t be scanned within 10 minutes due to excessive file sizes; the total number of files estimated to exceed 2TB.
