title: Bug Journal 2026-02-27

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-26 Key Points of the Day Completed Work: Investigated two core issues: Pi0.5 had a success rate of only 0% in evaluation tasks, and there was a mismatch in BC-RNN dimensionality. The evaluation framework was extended to include 5 MimicGen tasks. Parallel training of BC-RNN image patterns began, and the infrastructure for evaluating Pi0.5 phoenix_comparison checkpoints was explored. A two-stage evaluation process was planned. Implementation Methods: The root causes were identified through log analysis and code review (task mismatch, _D0 environment registration issues). The BC-RNN image pattern configuration was rewritten according to the official MimicGen guidelines. Using srun tool, 5 training tasks were started concurrently on an49 nodes with 8×A800 resources. Parallel exploration of two codebases was conducted to locate checkpoints, and the communication architecture between VLA servers and evaluation clients was understood. Achievements: The reason for Pi0.5’s 0% success rate was identified, and a task-related evaluation framework was established. BC-RNN training was completed, and the results can be directly compared with the findings in the MimicGen paper. A two-stage evaluation process for Pi0.5 (VLA server + evaluate_mimicgen.py) has been defined, and the technical obstacles in the first stage of evaluation have been resolved. On Tianhe Supercomputer, we systematically solved the fundamental mismatch between Pi0.5/BC-RNN training and evaluation processes. The evaluation framework was extended to include 5 MimicGen tasks, and parallel training of BC-RNN image patterns began. We also explored the infrastructure for evaluating Pi0.5 checkpoints at intervals of 10,000 - 100,000 steps. The LaTeX resume format on a MacBook was optimized.
Tasks of the Day Architecture and Strategy ✅ Analyze and fix the task mismatch issue in Pi0.5 training evaluation — Investigated the reason for Pi0.5’s 0% success rate: Training was done on 5 MimicGen tasks, but the evaluation used PickPlace (not part of the training set). This indicates a complete mismatch between tasks. The Coffee_D0 and other MimicGen environments were created by adding import mimicgen to solve the BC-RNN dimensionality mismatch issue. Stop removing the _D0 suffix, and correct the LSTM input dimension error from the expected 65 to the actual 37. 🔄 Rewrite BC-RNN training configuration to image pattern and start 5 parallel tasks — Rewrote the BC-RNN JSON configuration into 5 tasks suitable for image patterns: Enable agentview_image + robot0_eye_in_hand_image, ResNet18Conv + SpatialSoftmax(32kp) encoder, 76×76 CropRandomizer, RNN hidden layer dimension = 1000, batch size = 16, hdf5_cache_mode = low_dim. Use srun –jobid=45628 to start 5 training tasks concurrently on an49 nodes for a total of 600 cycles. At the end of each session, the task progress was as follows: coffee (initialization 84%), stack (26th cycle), stack_three (188th cycle), threading (198th cycle), three_piece_assembly (190th cycle). 🔄 Explore and plan Pi0.5 MimicGen evaluation process — Systematically explored the location of phoenix_comparison checkpoints at intervals of 10,000 - 100,000 steps, with an interval of about 5000 steps. We clarified the relationship between two codebases: evaluate_mimicgen.py/start_vla_server.py by tangzijia and serve_policy.py by zhaoganlong. Confirmed that the evaluation result directory was empty (first evaluation). A two-stage evaluation process was planned: Start VLA server on GPU nodes → Execute evaluate_mimicgen.py at the evaluation end. Since there were no available GPU resources on the login node, actual evaluation had not been carried out yet. ✅ Expand the evaluation framework to support 5 MimicGen tasks — Created 5 new task YAML configurations (coffee/stack/stack_three/threading/three_piece_assembly) and task_registry.yaml. Added –task parameter in 1_generate_scenes.py/3_collect_data.py/4_analyze_results.py, and rewritten run_full_eval.sh to support multi-task processing. Implementation and Fixes ✅ LaTeX resume format optimization — Generated a professional LaTeX resume for user Tang Zijia based on Jake’s resume template. After multiple iterations, the publication section was optimized: changed from flushright to resumeProjectHeading inline, and finally adopted a two-line format where the paper title appeared on the first line, with the author role and conference name on both sides of the second line. Satisfied users can directly paste the resume into Overleaf for compilation. ✅ Multi-project connection test — Sent multiple short sessions containing only “hello” in project directories such as mozihao-VLA, chenxingping-X-VLA, and HOME. Most sessions were interrupted by users, indicating that these might be connection tests or accidental starts. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Pi0.5 evaluation had a success rate of 0%. Initially, it was suspected to be a model training problem or action clipping issue. Solution: The root cause was a complete task mismatch: Pi0.5 was trained on 5 MimicGen tasks, but the evaluation used PickPlace (not part of the training set). This problem was solved by changing the evaluation method to use the training tasks.
Key Insight: Ensure that the training dataset named local/mimicgen_phoenix_9tasks actually contains 5 MimicGen tasks. PickPlace represents a completely different task system, and a 0% success rate is inevitable. Aligning the evaluation framework with the training tasks is crucial for successful experiments.
2. RuntimeError in BC-RNN inference: input.size(-1) must equal input_size. The expected value was 65, but it was actually 37. Solution: In create_env(), Coffee_D0 was removed, causing additional observation data to be lost in the MimicGen environment. This problem was solved by registering the _D0 environment with import mimicgen, retaining all environmental details.
Key Insight: The _D0 environment provides additional object states through the robosuite registration mechanism. Removing the suffix causes the observation dimension to differ from that during training.
3. Challenges in Pi0.5 evaluation infrastructure: No available GPUs on the login node, unclear relationship between two codebases (tangzijia vs zhaoganlong), and an empty evaluation result directory. Solution: Use squeue to confirm that the user has a reusable interactive GPU node (an49, task number 45628). Clarified the relationship between two codebases: prioritize using tangzijia’s evaluate_mimicgen.py (adapted for 9 MimicGen tasks, using encapsulated TCP protocol), while zhaoganlong’s serve_policy.py uses WebSocket protocol and requires compatibility checks. An empty evaluation result directory indicates this is the first evaluation. Key Insight: The two codebases use different communication protocols (TCP vs WebSocket), which needs to be confirmed before evaluation. Having an existing SLURM interactive job can avoid reapplying for GPU resources.
4. BC-RNN training configuration was initially set to low_dim mode, which did not match the settings in the MimicGen paper. Solution: Reviewed the official MimicGen configuration generation script and confirmed that the paper used image mode (84×84, ResNet18 encoder). The configurations of all 5 tasks were modified.
Key Insight: The main results in the MimicGen paper are based on image observation data. low_dim is not a benchmark comparison metric. The main parameters include: hidden_dim=1000, actor_layer_dims=[], batch_size=16, CropRandomizer 76×76.
General Issues 5. SLURM sbatch submission failed: The user’s group was not allowed to use this partition (xy-a800). Solution: An existing interactive job (45628, an49) was found running. Using srun –jobid=45628 –overlap allowed running 5 training processes on the existing allocated resources, thus bypassing the partition permission restriction.
Key Insight: srun can be attached to an existing SLURM interactive job allocation. When sbatch permissions are unavailable, this is a viable alternative.
6. LaTeX resume publication section: Long titles were not formatted correctly using line breaks. Solution: Changed the single-line inline format to a two-line format using resumeSubheading: The first line was the title, and the second line was the author role and impact factor.
Key Insight: In LaTeX, long text should be used in two lines to avoid overflow. The appropriate command should be used based on the text length.
Human Thinking vs Artificial Intelligence Thinking Strategic Level Judgment of Pi0.5 training data range Role Idea Human The user directly asked, “Are you mixing 9 tasks together for model training?” This directly pointed out the fundamental issue of task coverage. Artificial Intelligence Initially focused on technical details such as action clipping and OOM issues. Local logs distracted the AI’s attention, preventing it from questioning the basic consistency between training/evaluation tasks. Difference Analysis: Compared to humans, artificial intelligence focuses more on the overall logic of projects, while humans can better understand the overall structure of a project. The user’s single question directly pointed out the root cause, while artificial intelligence requires guidance from the user to correct its approach.
Choice of BC-RNN observation patterns and evaluation progress Role Idea Human The user explicitly requested using the image pattern consistent with the paper results. This requirement was raised again during the evaluation process, indicating a desire to move faster towards execution rather than staying in the planning stage. Artificial Intelligence Initially created a low_dim configuration without explicitly choosing an observation pattern. After completing the evaluation exploration, it remained in the “design scheme” stage and did not actively take action to execute." Difference Analysis: The user had a clear goal of aligning with paper results and wanted to proceed quickly. Artificial intelligence tends to provide technical solutions or complete explorations before taking action, resulting in a gap between the user’s expectations and its approach.
Implementation Level LaTeX format adjustment Role Idea Human The user requested placing the author role on the second line and precisely expressing visual aesthetics requirements through multiple iterations. Artificial Intelligence Initially proposed technical solutions in single-line format. After multiple iterations, the user’s layout requirements were met." Difference Analysis: The user’s visual needs required repeated adjustments, while artificial intelligence tends to prioritize technical feasibility over optimal visuals.
Limitations of Artificial Intelligence Main Constraints When analyzing Pi0.5’s 0% success rate, technical issues (OOM, action clipping, server failures) were prioritized, without checking the basic consistency between training/evaluation tasks. This led to rework due to a user’s issue. Before implementation, key parameters were not carefully selected: BC-RNN configuration was created using low_dim template, without considering observation patterns. When submitting SLURM job, no partition permissions were checked, resulting in rework for both situations. During Pi0.5 evaluation phase, although multiple parallel sub-proxies were used to gather information, the process remained in the design stage and did not progress to actual execution (e.g., starting a server on GPU node via SSH), which did not match the user’s expected fast execution speed. General Constraints LaTeX resume format required multiple adjustments (3 times) to meet the user’s layout requirements. The final requirement was not anticipated in advance. Today’s Summary Key Conclusions Ensuring the evaluation framework is consistent with training tasks is crucial for successful experiments: When designing the evaluation process, it is necessary to confirm the specific tasks covered by the training set. Any abnormal success rate should prioritize checking the consistency of task coverage issues. MimicGen does not provide pre-trained checkpoints but only offers HDF5 demonstration datasets. The benchmark algorithm BC-RNN needs to be trained independently. The main results in the paper use image observation data (ResNet18 + SpatialSoftmax 84×84), rather than low_dim. _D0 environment must be registered with import mimicgen to robosuite; it is not allowed to remove the suffix, otherwise the observation dimension will differ, leading to BC-RNN inference failure. The main parameters include: hidden_dim=1000, actor_layer_dims=[], batch_size=16, CropRandomizer 76×76, hdf5_cache_mode=low_dim. Pi0.5 evaluation adopted a two-stage architecture: ① Start the WebSocket policy server on GPU nodes using the openpi05 conda environment; ② Execute evaluate_mimicgen.py at the evaluation end to connect to the server and repeat the execution of 9 MimicGen tasks, with each task iterating 20 times. The phoenix_comparison checkpoints contain complete checkpoints from step 10,000 to 100,000, allowing for multi-step comparisons during evaluation. Two existing codebases (tangzijia’s error_recovery_benchmark and zhaoganlong’s motion self-reflection framework) use different communication protocols: the former uses TCP sockets, while the latter uses WebSocket. Compatibility needs to be confirmed before use; prioritize using tangzijia’s version (adapted for handling 9 MimicGen tasks). Practical Summary srun –jobid= –overlap can be attached to an existing SLURM interactive job to run additional processes, making it a viable alternative when sbatch partition permissions are unavailable. Conference Abstract ErrorRecoveryBenchmark**🔄 Investigation of Pi0.5/BC-RNN training evaluation issues, expanded to 5-task evaluation framework, started BC-RNN image pattern training, planned Pi0.5 evaluation process** 01:10:41.534 | claude_code The user pointed out the 0% success rate of Pi0.5 and the mismatch in BC-RNN dimensionality. AI initially focused on technical details but was distracted by local logs, leading to a misunderstanding of the fundamental issue: Training was done on 5 MimicGen tasks, while evaluation used PickPlace. Subsequently, a multi-task evaluation framework (5 task YAML configurations + script parameters) was implemented, and the BC-RNN configuration was changed from low_dim to image mode. Using srun –jobid=45628 on an49 nodes with 8×A800 resources resolved the _D0 environment registration issue. Parallel training of 5 BC-RNN tasks began. Meanwhile, at steps 10,000 - 100,000, phoenix_comparison checkpoints were explored, and the relationship between two codebases was clarified. A two-stage evaluation process (VLA server + evaluate_mimicgen.py) was defined. Since there were no available GPUs on the login node, actual evaluation had not been carried out yet.
ResumeBuilder ✅ LaTeX resume format optimization: Multiple iterations improved the publication section 04:33:57.481 | claude_code The user provided an academic resume based on Markdown for Tang Zijia (Duke University Computer Science), including 4 papers. AI generated the initial version according to Jake’s resume template. After multiple iterations, the publication section was optimized: changed from flushright to resumeProjectHeading inline, and finally adopted a two-line format where the title appeared on the first line, with the author role and conference name on both sides of the second line. Satisfied users can paste it directly into Overleaf for compilation.
Multi-Project Connection Tests (mozihao-VLA/chenxingping-X-VLA/HOME) ❌ Multi-project directory connection tests 11:18:53.200 | claude_code The user sent short sessions containing only “hello” in project directories such as mozihao-VLA, chenxingping-X-VLA, and HOME/sysu_gbli2xy_1. Most sessions were interrupted by the user, indicating that these tests were either connection checks or accidental starts.

summary: On the Tianjin supercomputer, we systematically studied the fundamental differences between the training and evaluation processes of Pi0.5/BC-RNN, and addressed these issues. Our evaluation framework has been extended to include 5 MimicGen tasks, and parallel training of BC-RNN image patterns has also begun. We also explored the infrastructure requirements for evaluating checkpoints of Pi0.5 between 10,000 and 100,000 steps. The LaTeX resume format was optimized for processing on MacBook computers.

title: Bug Journal 2026-02-23

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W08 (2026-02-16 ~ 2026-02-22) This week was focused on the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project. We completed a systematic investigation of the H&E Image-Only clustering method (established a literature baseline for ARI between 0.11–0.16), implemented three self-supervised enhancement schemes (SCAN improved ARI from 0.251 to 0.303, +20.6%), and established a two-stage fusion framework for Vision Refinement. Meanwhile, we parallelly carried out the Error Recovery Benchmark on the tianhe cluster (M14 evaluation of infrastructure verification, full-scenario evaluation started at 649 scenarios), and built up the data pipeline for Phoenix pi0.5 (18.4GB MimicGen dataset stored, training configuration ready). Throughout the week, we resolved several engineering issues such as STEGO NaN, double normalization bug, lerobot version conflict, and HuggingFace proxy issues. The OOM issues of Pi0.5 and video verification for visualize_scene.py were postponed to next week.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-02-16 ~ 2026-02-22 Active Days 3 / 7 Total Conversations 9 Projects Involved 4 Completed Tasks 11 Ongoing Tasks 5 Total Tokens 28,509,501 Total Cost $14.13 Average Cost per Day $4.71 Project Progress MIHD (Spatial Transcriptomics Clustering) (3 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Completed four core technical documents: RM-IDEAL bilingual instructions, guidelines for using visual encoders, literature review of pathological PFM, evaluation analysis of UNI/UNI2 original papers Conducted a systematic investigation of the H&E Image-Only method, established a literature baseline for DLPFC ARI (SpaConTDS=0.16, stLearn=0.11), and analyzed five root causes of Foundation Model failure Implemented and verified three self-supervised enhancement schemes: STEGO/BYOL+GAT/SCAN. SCAN achieved the best result with ARI=0.303 (baseline 0.251, +20.6%). We also verified the complementary nature of its embedding with gene features (mean fusion +0.065 ARI) Identified and fixed a double normalization bug in eval_scan_fusion.py for STAIG. Decided to use the correct path in run_benchmark.py instead of fixing the independent script Integrated CacheManager embedding caching in run_benchmark.py (loaded in seconds, supports custom cache names for scan_uni2) Implemented a two-stage fusion framework for Vision Refinement (~60 lines of minimal intrusion). Started batch experiments with 7 fusion strategies in the background Re-generated all 11 sections visualizations in batches (using .npz caching, no need for re-inference), and added an H&E original image panel Blocked Items:
⚠️ The coordinate dimension bug in eval_scan_fusion.py caused incomplete debugging of the SCAN fusion evaluation script ⚠️ The first experiment with Vision Refinement (scan_cluster + concat, ARI=0.313) was lower than direct concat (0.387). The issue of diversity of self-supervised compression loss features needs further analysis ⚠️ The implementation of ImageEncoder for the big goal 7 of ENHANCEMENT_PLAN_CN.md has not yet started Error Recovery Benchmark & Phoenix pi0.5 Replica (1 active day) — 🔄 active Completed:
Developed a complete Phase II execution plan covering 7 steps, covering G1–G7 big goals and M12–M15 milestones. The critical path is about 16 days M14 baseline evaluation of infrastructure verification (10 scenarios passed sanity check), full-scenario CPU evaluation started at 649 scenarios (surpassing expectations by 454, +43%) Completed the full data pipeline for Phoenix pi0.5: convert_mimicgen_to_lerobot.py, evaluate_mimicgen.py, OpenPI training configuration (100K steps, 4-GPUs), and downloaded 18.4GB MimicGen dataset with format conversion completed in tasks 7-8/9 Diagnosed the OOM bottleneck of Pi0.5, standardized GPU access from SSH to srun –overlap Resolved compatibility issues between lerobot 0.1.0 and datasets>=4.0 (downgraded to 3.6.0) Established hf-mirror.com as the standard data acquisition solution for the cluster’s HuggingFace Blocked Items:
⚠️ The OOM issue of Pi0.5 remains unresolved (GPU memory insufficient by 150MB), and baseline evaluation is still blocked ⚠️ The video verification of visualize_scene.py is blocked due to SLURM node permissions issues ⚠️ The Pi0 VLA Server port conflict (port 5555 occupied) caused session interruptions Key Tasks ✅ Systematic investigation of H&E Image-Only clustering method (2026-02-19) — Collected information on methods such as MILWRM/F-SEG/Deep Contrastive Clustering. Verified the value of DLPFC ARI in image-only experiments (SpaConTDS=0.16, stLearn=0.11). Conducted in-depth research on the application of BYOL/STEGO/SCAN in pathology. Reviewed frameworks for addressing domain gaps in the CV community ✅ Established MIHD technical documentation system (2026-02-19) — Created four core technical documents: RM-IDEAL bilingual structure document, guidelines for using visual encoders (12 chapters), literature review of pathological PFM, evaluation analysis of UNI/UNI2 original papers (34 clinical tasks) ✅ Analyzed root causes of Foundation Model failure in spatial domain recognition (2026-02-19) — Conducted a systematic analysis from five dimensions: training data domain mismatch, pre-training task mismatch, minimal morphological differences between brain tissue layers, feature redundancy, and lack of spatial context. Provided case studies using the UNI2 brown repetitive patch phenomenon ✅ Implemented Image-Only clustering enhancement schemes (STEGO/BYOL+GAT/SCAN) (2026-02-19) — Created four model files: STEGOHead/BYOLAdapter/SpatialGATRefiner/SCANHead. Conducted comparative tests on 151673 sections, with SCAN achieving the best result with ARI=0.303 (+20.6%) ✅ Investigated double normalization bug in STAIG fusion and made architectural decisions (2026-02-20) — Confirmed that not passing staig_alignment_config in eval_scan_fusion.py caused StandardScaler to be used again within STAIGTrainer. Decided to use run_benchmark.py instead of fixing the independent script ✅ Integrated embedding caching mechanism in run_benchmark.py (2026-02-20) — Introduced CacheManager, loading cache before gene/vision encoding and skipping encoder instantiation if hit. Supported custom cache names for scan_uni2, with vision caches available in standard/freq/staig_strict variants 🔄 M14 baseline evaluation of infrastructure verification and full-scenario evaluation started (2026-02-22) — sanity check (60 episodes, about 7 minutes per episode, SR=0% as expected). Subsequently, started full CPU evaluation with 649 scenarios × 2 strategies × 3 seeds (about 3894 episodes), running in the background 🔄 Established the complete data pipeline for Phoenix pi0.5 (2026-02-22) — Written convert_mimicgen_to_lerobot.py and evaluate_mimicgen.py, configured pi05_base_mimicgen_phoenix training parameters. Downloaded 18.4GB MimicGen dataset through hf-mirror.com (9 tasks with 9000 demos), completed format conversion in tasks 7-8/9 🔄 Joint evaluation of SCAN embedding and multi-modal fusion (2026-02-19) — Written eval_scan_fusion.py to compare the optimized 256-dimensional visual embedding with PCA gene features across all fusion methods. Mean fusion achieved ARI +0.065, partially fixed the coordinates dimension bug, script debugging in progress 🔄 Implemented two-stage fusion framework for MIHD Vision Refinement and batch experiments (2026-02-22) — Added –vision_refine parameter (scan_cluster/stego_refine/byol_spatial), with minimal intrusion of about 60 lines of code. The first experiment achieved ARI=0.313, which was lower than baseline concat 0.387. Batch experiments with 7 fusion strategies are running in the background ✅ Formulated complete execution plan for Error Recovery Benchmark Phase II (2026-02-22) — Analyzed dependencies between G1–G7 big goals and M12–M15 milestones, developed an execution plan covering 7 steps. Clarified GPU allocation strategy (srun –overlap with at least 50% free memory), with a critical path of about 16 days ✅ Diagnosed Error Recovery Benchmark baseline issues and updated GPU access specifications (2026-02-22) — Confirmed that Pi0.5 OOM (150MB insufficient GPU memory) and BC-RNN obs key issues have been resolved, and 649 scenarios are ready. Standardized GPU access methods in CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md to srun –overlap 🚫 Force parameter expansion for visualize_scene.py (2026-02-22) — Completed adding force_override/duration_override/settle_steps parameters and Phase 3 neutral action logic. Video verification is still blocked due to SLURM node permissions issues ✅ Special comparison experiment with STAIG fusion (151673) (2026-02-20) — Tested staig_fusion × {UNI2_raw, SCAN(UNI2)} separately. ARI values were 0.3929/0.3880 respectively, almost equal. Verified that StandardScaler + PCA within STAIG offset the optimization effect of SCAN ✅ Added H&E panel to UNI2 visualization and updated in batches (2026-02-19) — Modified to a 1×3 layout (H&E + GT + prediction). Re-generated all 11 sections visualizations using .npz caching, with 151510 successfully repaired through hires→lowres symlinks Issues and Solutions 1. STEGO training loss was NaN throughout the process. A dense similarity matrix of 3639×3639 caused overflow when temperature=0.07 [MIHD] (2026-02-19) Solution: Two-step fix: Normalize the input image_emb; replace InfoNCE with a numerically stable version (reduce maximum value before taking logsumexp, increase temperature to 0.1)
2. MILWRM was incorrectly classified as an Image-Only method. The first AI summary mixed multi-modal methods with image-only results. The conclusions of the first research need significant revision [MIHD] (2026-02-19) Solution: Use WebFetch to read the full PMC text to confirm that MILWRM actually relies on gene expression. Specifically extract image-only data points from ablation experiments in articles like SpaConTDS
3. There was a double normalization bug in staig_fusion in eval_scan_fusion.py (StandardScaler used again within STAIGTrainer) [MIHD] (2026-02-20) Solution: Abandon fixing the independent script and directly use run_benchmark.py, which correctly passes staig_alignment_config. Reuse the verified path
4. In run_benchmark.py, the encoder was re-instantiated to extract embeddings every time, lacking pipeline-level caching support [MIHD] (2026-02-20) Solution: Integrate CacheManager from pipeline/cache_manager.py. Load cache before encoding and write it back after extraction. Skip encoder instantiation if hit
5. Lerobot 0.1.0 was incompatible with datasets>=4.0 (torch.stack reported TypeError: Column object replaced list) [Error Recovery Benchmark / Phoenix pi0.5] (2026-02-22) Solution: Downgrade datasets from 4.4.1 to 3.6.0; for datasets 4.0, change the return value of dataset[‘column’] from list to Column object. Lerobot expects a list/tuple of tensors
6. HuggingFace official download failed due to proxy (Squid 503), and Python download script couldn’t connect [Error Recovery Benchmark / Phoenix pi0.5] (2026-02-22) Solution: Use hf-mirror.com + wget instead. URL format: https://hf-mirror.com/datasets/{repo_id}/resolve/main/{file_path}，通过集群 HTTP proxy available at 40–200MB/s
7. Unstable access to Slurm GPU nodes: srun without –overlap caused commands to hang, direct SSH connection was unreliable, multi-partition submissions were rejected [Error Recovery Benchmark / Phoenix pi0.5] (2026-02-22) Solution: Standardize the workflow: source set-XY-I.sh → squeue → srun –jobid= –overlap; prohibit direct SSH to GPU nodes
8. In eval_scan_fusion.py, there was an anomaly in spatial coordinate dimensions (became (1,2)), causing errors for multiple fusion methods [MIHD] (2026-02-19) Solution: Abandon calling load_spatial_coordinates() (barcode matching failed). Instead, directly read coordinates from adata.obsm[‘spatial’]. Fix the error in unpacking return values of load_dlpfc_data
Learnings Domain Knowledge### Architecture SCAN is the most effective method for image-only spatial transcriptomics tasks, with an ARI of 0.303 and a gain of +20.6%. Its core advantage lies in offline feature k-NN mining, which decouples embedding learning from clustering. The 256-dimensional optimized embeddings, when fused with gene features, show strong complementarity (mean fusion +0.065 ARI). Two-stage fusion is not necessarily superior to one-stage fusion: After compressing the visual embedding from 1536d to 256d using scan_cluster, the ARI of multi-modal fusion (0.313) is lower than that of direct concatenation (0.387). This indicates that self-supervised compression may lose the original feature diversity required for fusion tasks. The goals of self-supervised clustering optimization and downstream fusion tasks are inconsistent. STAIG fusion uses StandardScaler+PCA preprocessing to absorb the effects of external embedding optimization (the difference in ARI between SCAN and UNI2_raw is only 0.005). Embeddings should be saved as raw embeddings before normalization, and post-encoder normalization should be performed after loading to ensure consistent results across different call paths. VLA baseline evaluations must clearly state the source of checkpoints: Models fine-tuned on non-target datasets (pi0_libero, pi05_base) are evaluated for zero-shot cross-domain recovery capabilities. The paper must explicitly state this experimental setup, and additional comparative experiments after fine-tuning are needed to fully demonstrate the usefulness of the dataset. In complex experimental systems, it is advisable to reuse existing, validated tool paths (such as run_benchmark.py). Independent scripts can easily introduce hidden bugs due to inconsistent preprocessing. Before calling internal functions within the project, the source code should be read in real-time to verify function signatures, rather than relying on memory. Debugging When calculating large-scale dense similarity matrices (n>3000) using InfoNCE with comparative loss, it is necessary to use numerically stable log-sum-exp (minus the row maximum). Otherwise, exponential operations with temperature=0.07 under float32 precision may overflow and cause NaN values. This is a key engineering constraint for implementing large-scale comparative learning. Tools Lerobot 0.1.0 has strict constraints on dataset versions; it should be pinned to <4.0 (recommended 3.x). Dataset 4.0 changes the return value of dataset[‘column’] to a Column object, causing a TypeError in torch.stack. MimicGen is fully compatible with LIBERO obs/action format (84×84 images, 8D states, 7D actions), and can directly reuse OpenPI’s LeRobotLiberoDataConfig. The standard way to access HuggingFace from Chinese mainland HPC clusters is via hf-mirror.com. The URL format is: https://hf-mirror.com/datasets/{repo_id}/resolve/main/{file_path}，通过 wget + HTTP proxy with a speed of 40–200MB/s. This should be the default approach. OpenPI’s JAX training natively supports multi-GPU data parallelism. Just specify the list of GPUs using CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, and JAX will automatically build a 2D mesh parallel without modification to TrainConfig. Slurm –overlap is a key parameter for running new commands on existing interactive jobs, and it is a core technique for calling cluster GPU nodes in the Claude Code environment. Notes on AI Usage Effective Patterns: ✓ Parallel sub-tasks are used to explore project structures and develop cleanup plans (MIHD organizes tasks), reducing the time spent on serial exploration. ✓ Three methods (STEGO/BYOL/SCAN) are executed in parallel on GPUs to compare and verify, significantly reducing the total experiment time. ✓ Using .npz caches allows for batch re-generation of visualizations, completely decoupling inference from visualization. This worked well with 11/11 sections. ✓ Independent temporary scripts (_test_staig_scan.py) isolate tests for individual fusion methods, reducing the task duration from 2 hours to 30 seconds. ✓ Independent exploration of JAX’s multi-GPU mechanism (no need to modify config) and alternative download solutions via hf-mirror.com are independent tasks that do not require user intervention. ✓ The minimum invasive code modification (about 60 lines) is made in run_benchmark.py by inserting the Vision Refinement stage, maintaining the stability of the architecture. Limitations: ✗ Inaccurate classification of literature: MILWRM was incorrectly classified as an image-only method. Initial research results were mixed with multi-modal results, requiring two interventions from users to converge to the correct research boundary. ✗ Lack of awareness to actively expose key experimental assumptions: The paper did not explicitly state how VLA evaluation using LIBERO fine-tuned checkpoints affects the validity of the experiment. This topic was only discussed after user inquiries. ✗ API usage tends to rely on memory rather than real-time verification: eval_scan_fusion.py had multiple errors in function signatures/return values. The source code should be read in before calling. ✗ Slurm permission issues led to multiple attempts to access partitions (5+ times). The correct account/partition information was not quickly obtained from users. ✗ Insufficient status monitoring of background tasks: Data conversion was still ongoing when LeRobot dataset verification was triggered, resulting in false timestamp violation errors. Frequent sleep polling was interrupted multiple times. ✗ In the HPC environment, CPU is used for model validation by default, hiding real performance issues and causing additional interaction problems. Next Week’s Plans Next week will focus on three parallel tasks: ①MIHD: Fixing bugs in eval_scan_fusion.py to evaluate SCAN embeddings and full fusion strategies. Analyze batch experiment results of 7 fusion strategies and diagnose the root causes of Vision Refinement compression loss features. Consider adjusting refinement hidden_dim or switching between stego_refine/byol_spatial methods. ②Error Recovery Benchmark: Organize results of the full CPU evaluation for M14 649 scenarios to solve Pi0.5 OOM issues (request for larger memory GPUs or reduce batch size). Start training Phoenix pi0.5 with 100K steps on 4-GPUs. ③Engineering Block Elimination: Resolve SLURM node permission issues to complete video verification of visualize_scene.py. Address Pi0 VLA server port conflicts (detect using lsof -i:5555 + alternate port mechanism) and advance the implementation of ImageEncoder enhancement in ENHANCEMENT_PLAN_CN.md.

summary: This week, we focused on the MIHD spatial transcriptomics project. We completed a systematic investigation into H&E Image-Only clustering (establishing a literature baseline for ARI values ranging from 0.11 to 0.16), implemented three self-supervised enhancement methods (SCAN improved ARI from 0.251 to 0.303, a +20.6% increase), and established a two-stage framework for Vision Refinement. Meanwhile, we continued to work on Error Recovery Benchmarking on the tianhe cluster (M14 evaluation of infrastructure, initialization of full-scenario evaluations with 649 scenarios). We also prepared the Phoenix pi0.5 data pipeline (storage of 18.4GB MimicGen dataset, training configuration ready). Throughout the week, we resolved several engineering issues, including STEGO NaN, dual normalization bugs, version conflicts with lerobot, and problems with HuggingFace proxies. The remaining issues related to Pi0.5 OOM and visualizing scenes via video verification will be addressed next week.

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-19 Today’s Highlights Completed: Progress was made in the core experiments of two projects: MIHD and error recovery benchmark tests. A complete evaluation documentation system was established for MIHD; three image-based self-supervised clustering models were implemented, and literature on pure visual ST domain was organized. The second phase of error recovery design was completed, with complete M13/M14 code implemented, and project documentation was managed. Actions Taken: For MIHD, four technical documents were written using the codebase and extensive literature research (more than 10 WebSearch/WebFetch calls). Four self-supervised model modules were integrated into the run_benchmark.py process. The numerical instability issue in DLPFC 151673 was resolved. For the error recovery project, eight VLA papers were integrated through web search, the roadmap document was reorganized, M13 statistical analysis scripts using Cohen’s kappa/Kruskal-Wallis were implemented, a triple-tuple data collection mechanism was developed, and compatibility issues with Windows SSH ControlMaster were addressed. Benefits: MIHD established a complete evaluation documentation system, and visual feature baselines were strengthened. The optimal combination of SCAN and llava_mlp in multimodal fusion was determined (ARI was 0.409). MIHD filled the literature gap on ARIs in the pure visual ST domain. The error recovery project built a framework from theoretical basis to empirical verification. M14’s infrastructure code is ready for immediate use in complete evaluations, including 4 strategies × 454 scenarios. SSH access in the development environment has been restored. MIHD completed four core spatial transcriptomics documents, implemented three self-supervised clustering enhancements (SCAN with image-based ARI was 0.303, increased to 0.409 when combined with llava_mlp), and systematically studied the performance limits of image-based methods. Error recovery benchmark tests completed VLA literature research, reorganized the second-phase roadmap, implemented complete M13 classification code (passed 30 unit tests), established M14’s baseline evaluation infrastructure, and simplified project documentation.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics Core Documentation System (RM-IDEAL/Visual Encoder/Literature Research/UNI2 Analysis) — Four technical documents were created: (1) docs/RM_IDEAL_SCORE.md—Chinese-English documentation for RM-IDEAL scoring, including mathematical formulas for WWL core images/Wasserstein+Hamming cost matrices; (2) docs/VISION_ENCODER_GUIDE.md—a 12-chapter end-to-end Chinese guide comparing UNI2/UNI/HIPT/ResNet50 encoders; (3) docs/VISION_ENCODER_LITERATURE_SURVEY.md—a survey of PFM use compared to spEMO, STAIG, HEST-1k, etc.; (4) in-depth analysis of UNI/UNI2 original papers (34+8 benchmark tasks), highlighting the lack of literature on ARIs in the pure visual ST domain. ✅ Implement and verify image-based self-supervised clustering in DLPFC (STEGO/BYOL+GAT/SCAN) — Four new model modules (STEGOHead/BYOLAdapter/SpatialGATRefiner/SCANHead) were created and integrated into the run_benchmark.py process. SCAN was tested in DLPFC 151673: SCAN performed best (ARI was 0.303, a 20.6% improvement over the baseline of 0.251), BYOL ranked second (0.237), and STEGO was below the baseline level (0.209). The NaN loss issue in STEGO dense similarity matrices was resolved. ✅ Evaluation of SCAN embeddings versus multimodal fusion methods — eval_scan_fusion.py was created, replacing the original UNI2 features with optimized visual embeddings and comparing them with PCA gene features. ARI for llava_mlp+SCAN reached 0.409 (best), mean+SCAN had the greatest improvement (+6.5%), while adaln_attention performance declined after using SCAN. ✅ Literature research on H&E image clustering, basic model failure analysis, and BYOL domain adaptability — Web search was conducted for 8 error recovery VLA models (SC-VLA/CycleVLA/VLA-SCT/FailSafe/FPC-VLA/RoboFAC/VLAC/Self-Improving VLA) and 9 mainstream basic models (π0/π0-FAST/π0.5/OpenVLA/Octo/GR00T N1/HPT, etc.). docs/VLA_Foundation_Models_Survey.md was created and synchronized to section 14 of the project summary document. ✅ Complete implementation of M13 classification system code (passed 30 unit tests) — Four new documents were created: docs/taxonomy_justification.md (FMEA mapping + 6×3 integrity matrix + literature comparison), scripts/7_classifier_reliability.py (Cohen’s/Fleiss’ kappa + non-redundant verification + 24×24 heatmap), scripts/8_error_type_discriminability.py (Kruskal-Wallis + post-hoc Dunn’s + rank reversal detection), tests/unit/test_classifier_reliability.py (passed 30 unit tests within 0.32 seconds); Makefile was updated with analyze-reliability/analyze-discriminability targets. ✅ Rebuild the second-phase roadmap for error recovery benchmark tests (G4-G7/M12-M15) — The project roadmap was extended from phase one to phase two (for validation), adding G4-G7 main objectives, detailed milestone plans for M12-M15 (M12: dataset expansion, M13: classification verification, M14: baseline evaluation, M15: fine-tuning verification), and a 16-day execution plan along with risk analysis for phase two. Project overview.md was upgraded to version v4.9. ✅ Basic code implementation for M12 large dataset — scripts/6_dataset_statistics.py (including gap analysis report), configs/mimicgen_augment_200.json were created; database.py was modified (added create_stratified_split), configs/benchmark_v4.yaml (reduced detector minimum step threshold: instability from 100 to 50, proximity from 10 to 5, pre-capture from 50 to 20), 1_generate_scenes.py (added –dataset_path parameter), Makefile (added M12 targets). Current dataset status was confirmed: 454 scenarios/empty transport stage. ✅ Implement M14 baseline evaluation infrastructure code — collector.py added Resume support (triple-tuple unique identifier + dictionary reconstruction of all episodes); 3_collect_data.py added –resume/–scene_offset/–scene_limit parameters; 4_analyze_results.py was rewritten to support multi-run merging, decomposition by error type (including heatmap matrix), and summary.json output; Makefile added m14-cpu/pi0/pi05/analyze/sanity complete targets. All Python files passed syntax checks. ✅ Create documentation on VLA basic model research — Integrated 8 error recovery VLA models (SC-VLA/CycleVLA/VLA-SCT/FailSafe/FPC-VLA/RoboFAC/VLAC/Self-Improving VLA) and 9 mainstream basic models (π0/π0-FAST/π0.5/OpenVLA/Octo/GR00T N1/HPT, etc.), creating docs/VLA_Foundation_Models_Survey.md and synchronizing it to section 14 of the project summary document. Implementation and Fixes - ✅ Optimization and refinement of project overview.md — Content in project overview.md was streamlined from 1554 lines to 763 lines (a 51% reduction), duplicate content in section 11 was removed, VLMs tutorial was moved to docs/vlm_tutorial.md, all statistical data was updated (file count: 31→53/code lines: 7565→14300), while all 87 checklists were retained; CLAUDE.md was compressed from 205 lines to 163 lines, the number of error types corrected (17→24), verification list filled in (4 items), and instructions on test fixtures and data formats added. ✅ Enhancement of UNI2 clustering visualization features — H&E reference panels + batch generation for all 11 parts were modified. The create_clustering_visualization() function in run_benchmark.py was updated to display H&E, GT, and predicted results using a 1×3 layout, and the alpha=0 parameter was used in sc.pl.spatial() to show unobstructed H&E images. Cached.npz embedded files were used to batch generate visualizations for all 11 DLPFC parts, and the issue of missing lowres_image.png image for sample 151510 was resolved. ✅ Fixing Windows VS Code Remote-SSH connection issues — SSH connection failures for tianhe/an49 were resolved (due to file descriptor/mux_client_request_session errors). The root cause was that ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist in SSH configuration did not support Unix domain sockets on Windows systems. These three configurations were commented out, and residual files in the ~/.ssh/sockets directory were removed after cleaning. ✅ Abnormal issues with robot benchmark results — an47 refused ExitPlanMode due to pam_slurm_adopt cluster strategy, which had nothing to do with SSH configuration. Key Issues 1. Regarding PFM embedding techniques for unsupervised clustering methods in ST domain ARIs, there is a lack of direct literature; obtaining accurate data for imaging methods that use only images is very difficult, and no multimodal experiments using only images have ever been conducted. Solution: The only clear report on PFM using only image data was found in the SpConTDS paper. The results of stMMC ablation were cited (ARI improvement rate was 4.6%), to quantify the contribution of images, and HEST-1k was used to provide relative rankings for PFM. MILWRM was incorrectly classified as a method using only images, but this was corrected after full-text verification (“gene expression-based”).
Key Insight: There is almost no literature on configurations using only images in the ST domain. This scarcity represents both limitations and opportunities for MIHD to develop baselines using only images. Accurately classifying based on summaries carries systematic risks.
2. STEGO’s training loss was always NaN, and when dividing a dense similarity matrix of 3639×3639 by temperature 0.07, the float32 logsumexp calculation caused overflow issues. Solution: A numerically stable implementation was adopted: the sim matrix was truncated to be between (-10, 10), manual logsumexp operations were replaced with F.log_softmax, and L2 normalization was used to standardize input features first.
Key Insight: Large batch data (n_spots approximately 3600) and low temperature values (0.07) may lead to numerical overflow in dense comparison losses. A stable numerical implementation should be chosen during development.
3. The transport/placement stage in 454 scenarios is completely blank. Current demonstration-based generation only triggers the detector at the pre-emptive/reach/upgrade stage. Solution: A two-pronged approach was adopted: (1) lowering the detector’s minimum step threshold; (2) using BC-RNN to capture natural errors in later stages—VLA had a success rate of only 0%, so it could not enter the transport/placement stage.
Key Insight: Since the current VLA strategy has a success rate of only 0%, BC-RNN is needed to capture natural errors in later stages. Lowering thresholds and using BC-RNN is the fastest way to fill the gap without waiting for VLA fine-tuning.
4. The second-phase core experiment requires restoring G4 training data, but the success rate of BC-RNN on error scenarios may be close to 0%, so it cannot restore trajectories through automated evaluation. Solution: Manual remote operations were used to collect and restore trajectories (at least 200 targets). First, hierarchical processing was performed by error type to independently test representative scenarios, then the entire process was completed.
Key Insight: Although Oracle’s recovery process is automated, its quality is questionable. Remote operation data can provide higher quality and capture natural errors, thus meeting the requirements of the paper.
Today’s Achievements Core Achievements #### Actual Results Principles for optimizing large technical documents: “Operable assets” (checklists, command descriptions, API references) should not be compressed to reduce size. “Descriptive content” (repeated architectural descriptions, version change records) is the main target of compression. Windows SSH’s ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist cannot be used on Windows systems; these settings must be removed from the configuration. Today’s Key Points Core Key Points Performance limits and literature gaps for pure visual ST domain ARIs: DLPFC images had only ARIs of 0.11-0.16 (compared to 0.45-0.64 for multimodal). This was because the basic model was trained with cancer tissue data, and there were subtle morphological differences between brain cortex layers, while fragment-level independent encoding lacked spatial context. There is almost no literature on PFM embedding → unsupervised KMeans → domain ARIs in ST. MIHD is filling this practical literature gap. SCAN self-supervised clustering performed best in DLPFC and multimodal transfer: Feature k-NN mining + clustering consistency loss achieved the best results in image-only clustering (ARI was 0.303, a 20.6% improvement over the baseline of 0.251), better than STEGO and BYOL. SCAN embedding + llava_mlp multimodal fusion further increased ARI to 0.409. Self-supervised refined visual features had transfer advantages in downstream fusion. The average fusion effect using SCAN was the best (+6.5%), while adaln_attention performance declined after using SCAN. BYOL’s unique advantage in H&E domain adaptation: No negative samples required (to avoid ignoring similar layers), insensitive to batch size, STAIG verification showed that BYOL pre-training could improve feature quality and enhance multimodal clustering ARIs—it is the most practical image-only enhancement option for MIHD. A four-step progressive framework for verifying the effectiveness of robot benchmark tests: (1) dataset size and coverage; (2) meaningful classification systems (theoretical basis + empirical consistency + actual discrimination ability); (3) establishing baseline performance; (4) data that can enhance basic models—missing any step will result in failure to convince reviewers. Dependency and performance patterns of PFM tasks: spEMO confirmed that different tasks require different PFMs (spatial recognition最适合使用GPFM/CHIEF, scene retrieval最适合使用UNI2); HEST-1k revealed a logarithmic relationship between PFM performance and model size (Pearson R=0.81); pre-training for pathology was about 8% better in gene expression prediction than ImageNet pre-training. Priority of unsupervised domain adaptation: Across FGVC/Medicine/Radiology/Microscopy domains; within the same domain, unsupervised pre-training is better than direct transfer from general training; feature refinement layers (STEGO/SCAN) are better than direct K-means clustering; spatial graph structure modeling is a key supplement for fine-grained domain recognition. Solutions to the problem of empty transport/placement stages: Lowering the detector’s minimum step threshold (instability reduced from 100 to 50, etc.) and using BC-RNN rolling algorithms (can achieve 60-70% SR in later stages) are the fastest ways to fill the gap. VLA has a success rate of only 0%, meaning that capturing natural errors in later stages cannot rely on oracle recovery. FailSafe and VLA-SCT are plug-ins that do not require training and can be used immediately for M15 comparison experiments after M14 baseline is completed. Dense contrast loss (InfoNCE) is prone to numerical overflow issues when dealing with a large number of samples (n_spots approximately 3600) and low temperatures (0.07). Clamp(-10,10) + log_softmax stability treatment is required, and input features must first be normalized using L2 normalization. Principles for optimizing large technical documents: “Operable assets” (such as checklists, command descriptions, API references) should not be compressed, but “descriptive content” (such as repeated architectural descriptions, version change records) should be prioritized for reduction. Windows SSH’s ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist cannot be used on Windows systems; these settings must be removed from the configuration. Today’s Summary Core Key Points- The performance limits and lack of literature in ARIs for the pure visual domain: DLPFC images only yield ARIs with values between 0.11-0.16 (compared to 0.45-0.64 for multimodal cases). This is because the base model was trained using cancer tissue data, which results in subtle morphological differences between different layers of the cerebral cortex. Additionally, patch-level independent encoding lacks spatial context. There is almost no literature on PFM embeddings → unsupervised KMeans → ARIs for the pure visual domain. MIHD is filling this gap. SCAN self-supervised clustering achieves optimal performance in DLPFC and multimodal transfer: Feature k-NN mining + clustering consistency loss yielded the best results in image-level clustering (ARI was 0.303, a 20.6% improvement over the baseline value of 0.251), outperforming STEGO and BYOL. SCAN embeddings + llava_mlp multimodal fusion further improved ARIs to 0.409. Self-supervised refined visual features have transfer advantages in downstream fusion. Using SCAN yields the best average fusion results, with a increase of 6.5%, while adaln_attention shows a decline in performance after using SCAN.
BYOL has unique advantages in adapting to H&E domain: It does not require negative samples (to avoid ignoring similar layers) and is insensitive to batch size. STAIG verification indicates that BYOL pre-training can improve feature quality and enhance multimodal clustering ARIs - it is the most practical image-only enhancement option in MIHD.
A four-step progressive framework for verifying the effectiveness of robot benchmark tests: (1) dataset size and coverage; (2) meaningful classification systems (theoretical basis + empirical consistency + practical discrimination); (3) establishing baseline performance; (4) data that can enhance the base model - missing any of these steps will not convince reviewers.
Dependencies and performance patterns of PFM tasks: spEMO confirms that different tasks require different PFMs (spatial domain recognition is best done with GPFM/CHIEF, while scene retrieval is best done with UNI2); HEST-1k reveals a logarithmic relationship between PFM performance and model size (Pearson R = 0.81); pre-training for pathological features performs approximately 8% better in gene expression prediction than ImageNet pre-training.
Priority of unsupervised domain adaptation: Applications across FGVC/medicine/radiology/microscopy domains; within the same domain, unsupervised pre-training is superior to direct transfer from general training; feature refinement layers (such as STEGO/SCAN) are better than direct K-means clustering; spatial graph structure modeling is an important supplement for fine-grained domain recognition.
Solutions to empty transportation/location stage problems: Reducing the minimum step size threshold of the detector (instability decreases from 100 to 50, etc.) and using BC-RNN unfolding technology (which can achieve 60-70% SR in later stages) are the fastest ways to fill in the gaps. The success rate of VLA is 0%, which means that capturing natural errors in later stages cannot rely on oracle recovery. FailSafe and VLA-SCT are plug-ins that do not require training and can be used for M15 comparison experiments immediately after the completion of the M14 baseline.
Dense contrast loss (InfoNCE) tends to have numerical overflow issues when dealing with a large number of samples (about 3600 spots) and low temperatures (0.07). A stabilization method using clamp(-10,10) + log_softmax is required, and input features must be normalized using L2 before processing.
Practical Achievements Principles for optimizing large technical documents: Do not compress “operable assets” such as checklists, command descriptions, API references. Instead, focus on reducing “descriptive content” such as repeated architectural descriptions and version change records. ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist of Windows SSH cannot be used on Windows systems, so these settings must be removed from the configuration.

A four-step progressive framework for verifying the effectiveness of robot benchmark tests: (1) dataset size and coverage; (2) meaningful classification systems (theoretical basis + empirical consistency + practical discrimination); (3) establishing baseline performance; (4) data that can enhance the base model - missing any of these steps will result in inability to convince reviewers.
Dependencies and performance patterns of PFM tasks: spEMO confirms that different tasks require different PFMs (spatial domain recognition is best done with GPFM/CHIEF, while scene retrieval is best done with UNI2); HEST-1k reveals a logarithmic relationship between PFM performance and model size (Pearson R = 0.81); pre-training for pathology performs approximately 8% better in gene expression prediction than ImageNet pre-training.
Priority of unsupervised domain adaptation: Adaptation across FGVC/medicine/radiology/microscopy domains; within the same domain, unsupervised pre-training is superior to direct transfer from general training; feature refinement layers (such as STEGO/SCAN) are better than direct K-means clustering; spatial graph structure modeling is a key supplement for fine-grained domain recognition.
Solutions to empty transportation/location stage problems: Reducing the minimum step size threshold of the detector (instability decreases from 100 to 50, etc.) and using BC-RNN rolling algorithm (which can achieve 60-70% accuracy in later stages) are the fastest ways to fill in the gaps. The success rate of VLA is 0%, which means that capturing natural errors in later stages cannot rely on oracle recovery. FailSafe and VLA-SCT are plug-ins that do not require training and can be used for M15 comparison experiments immediately after the completion of the M14 baseline.
Dense contrast loss (InfoNCE) tends to have numerical overflow issues when dealing with a large number of samples (n_spots ~ 3600) and low temperatures (0.07). A stable implementation using clamp(-10,10) + log_softmax is required, and input features must be normalized using L2 before processing.
Practical Achievements Principles for optimizing large technical documents: Do not compress “operable assets” such as checklists, command descriptions, API references. Instead, focus on reducing “descriptive content” such as repeated architectural descriptions and version change records. ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist of Windows SSH cannot be used on Windows systems, so these options must be removed from the configuration.
Today’s Summary Key Points Performance limits and lack of literature in ARIs for the pure visual domain: DLPFC images only yield ARIs with accuracy values between 0.11-0.16 (compared to multimodal ARIs at 0.45-0.64). This is because the base model was trained using cancer tissue data, which results in subtle morphological differences between different layers of the cerebral cortex. Additionally, patch-level independent encoding lacks spatial context. There is almost no literature on PFM embeddings → unsupervised KMeans → pure visual domain ARIs. MIHD is filling this practical gap.
SCAN self-supervised clustering achieves optimal performance in DLPFC and multimodal transfer: Feature k-NN mining + clustering consistency loss yields the best results in image-level clustering (ARI was 0.303, a 20.6% improvement over the baseline value of 0.251), outperforming STEGO and BYOL. SCAN embeddings + llava_mlp multimodal fusion further improves ARIs to 0.409. Self-supervised refined visual features have transfer advantages in downstream fusion. Using SCAN yields the best average fusion results (+6.5%), while adaln_attention shows a decline in performance after using SCAN.
BYOL has unique advantages in adapting to H&E domain: It does not require negative samples (to avoid introducing similar layers) and is insensitive to batch size. STAIG verification indicates that BYOL pre-training can improve feature quality and enhance multimodal clustering ARIs - it is the most practical image-only enhancement option in MIHD.

Dependencies and performance patterns of PFM tasks: spEMO confirms that different tasks require different PFMs (spatial domain recognition is best done with GPFM/CHIEF, while scene retrieval is best done with UNI2); HEST-1k reveals a logarithmic relationship between PFM performance and model size (Pearson R = 0.81); pre-training for pathology performs approximately 8% better in gene expression prediction than ImageNet pre-training.
Priority of unsupervised domain adaptation: Adaptation across FGVC/medicine/radiology/microscopy domains; within the same domain, unsupervised pre-training is superior to direct transfer from general training; feature refinement layers (such as STEGO/SCAN) are better than direct K-means clustering; spatial graph structure modeling is a key supplement for fine-grained domain recognition.
Solutions to empty transportation/location stage problems: Reducing the minimum step size- SCAN自监督聚类在DLPFC和多模态迁移中的最佳性能：特征k-NN挖掘+聚类一致性损失在仅图像级的聚类中取得了最佳结果（ARI为0.303，比基线值0.251提高了20.6%），优于STEGO和BYOL。SCAN嵌入+llava_mlp多模态融合进一步将ARI提高至0.409。自监督精细化的视觉特征对下游融合具有迁移优势。使用SCAN的平均融合效果最佳（+6.5%），而adaln_attention在使用SCAN后性能下降。
BYOL在H&E领域适应中的独特优势：不需要负样本（以避免忽略相似层），对批量大小不敏感，且STAIG验证表明BYOL预训练可以提高特征质量并增强多模态聚类ARIs——这是MIHD中最实用的仅图像增强选项。
验证机器人基准测试有效性的四步渐进框架：（1）数据集规模和覆盖范围；（2）有意义的分类系统（理论基础+经验一致性+实际区分度）；（3）建立基线性能；（4）能够提升基础模型的数据——缺少任何一步都会导致无法说服评审员。
PFM任务的依赖性和性能模式：spEMO证实不同任务需要不同的PFM（空间域识别最适合使用GPFM/CHIEF，场景检索最适合使用UNI2）；HEST-1k揭示了PFM性能与模型大小之间的对数关系（Pearson R=0.81）；针对病理的预训练在基因表达预测方面比ImageNet预训练好约8%。
无监督领域适应的优先级：跨FGVC/医学/放射学/显微镜领域；在同一领域内，无监督预训练优于从通用训练中直接迁移；特征细化层（如STEGO/SCAN）优于直接的K-means聚类；空间图结构建模是精细领域识别的关键补充。
解决空运输/放置阶段问题的方案：降低检测器的最小步长阈值（不稳定性从100降低到50等），并使用BC-RNN展开技术（在后期阶段可实现60-70%的SR）是最快填补空白的方法。VLA的成功率为0%，这意味着后期阶段捕捉自然错误不能依赖预言恢复。FailSafe和VLA-SCT是无需训练的插件，可以在M14基线完成后立即用于M15比较实验。
密集对比损失（InfoNCE）在处理大量样本（约3600个位置）和低温度（0.07）时容易出现数值溢出问题。需要采用clamp(-10,10) + log_softmax稳定的实现方式，并且输入特征必须先进行L2归一化处理。
实际成果 优化大型技术文档的原则：不应压缩“可操作资产”（如检查清单、命令描述、API参考），而应重点压缩“描述性内容”（如重复的架构描述、版本变更记录）。Windows SSH的ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist在Windows系统上无法使用，因此必须从配置中移除这些设置。
今日总结 核心要点 纯视觉领域的ARIs的性能上限和文献空白：仅包含DLPFC图像领域的ARIs仅为0.11-0.16（而多模态为0.45-0.64）。原因是基础模型是用癌症组织数据训练的，大脑皮层层之间存在细微的形态差异，且patch级独立编码缺乏空间上下文。关于PFM嵌入→无监督KMeans→ST领域ARIs的文献几乎不存在。MIHD正在填补这一空白。
SCAN自监督聚类在DLPFC和多模态迁移中的最佳性能：特征k-NN挖掘+聚类一致性损失在仅图像层面的聚类中取得了最佳效果（ARI为0.303，比基线值0.251高出20.6%），优于STEGO和BYOL。SCAN嵌入+llava_mlp多模态融合进一步将ARIs的性能提高至0.409。自监督的精细视觉特征对下游融合具有迁移优势。使用SCAN的平均融合效果最佳（+6.5%），而adaln_attention在使用SCAN后性能下降。
BYOL在H&E领域适应中的独特优势：不需要负样本（以避免误识别类似层），对批量大小不敏感，且STAIG证实BYOL预训练可以提高特征质量并增强多模态聚类ARIs——这是MIHD中最实用的仅图像增强选项。
验证机器人基准测试有效性的四步渐进框架：（1）数据集规模和覆盖范围；（2）有意义的分类系统（理论基础+经验一致性+实际区分能力）；（3）建立基线性能；（4）能够提升基础模型的数据——缺少任何一步都会导致无法说服评审员。
PFM任务的依赖性和性能模式：spEMO证实不同任务需要不同的PFM（空间域识别最适合使用GPFM/CHIEF，场景检索最适合使用UNI2）；HEST-1k揭示了PFM性能和模型大小之间的对数关系（Pearson R=0.81）；针对病理的预训练在基因表达预测方面比ImageNet预训练好约8%。
无监督领域适应的优先级：跨FGVC/医学/放射学/显微镜领域；在同一领域内，无监督预训练优于从通用训练中直接迁移；特征细化层（如STEGO/SCAN）优于直接的K-means聚类；空间图结构建模是精细领域识别的关键补充。
解决运输/放置阶段空白场景的方案：降低检测器的最小步长阈值（不稳定性从100降低到50等），并使用BC-RNN展开技术（在后期阶段可实现60-70%的SR）是最快的填补方法。VLA的成功率为0%，这意味着后期阶段捕捉自然错误不能依赖预言恢复。FailSafe和VLA-SCT是无需训练的插件，可以在M14基线完成后立即用于M15比较实验。
密集对比损失（InfoNCE）在处理大批量数据（n-spots约3600）和低温度（0.07）时容易出现数值溢出问题。需要采用clamp(-10,10) + log_softmax稳定化方法实现，并且输入特征必须先进行L2归一化。
实际成果 优化大型技术文档的原则：不应压缩“可操作资产”（如检查清单、命令描述、API参考），而应减少“描述性内容”的篇幅。对于重复出现的架构描述、版本更新日志等，也是需要重点优化的部分。Windows SSH中的ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist在Windows系统上无法使用，因此必须从配置中移除这些设置。
今日要点 核心要点 仅视觉领域的ARIs在DLPFC图像-only ARIs上的性能上限和文献空白：DLPFC图像-only ARIs仅为0.11-0.16（而多模态为0.45-0.64）。原因是基础模型是用癌症组织数据训练的，大脑皮层各层之间存在细微的形态差异，且片段级独立编码缺乏空间上下文。关于PFM嵌入→无监督KMeans→仅视觉领域的ARIs的文献几乎不存在。MIHD正在填补这一实际的文献空白。
SCAN自监督聚类在DLPFC和多模态转移中的最佳性能：特征k-NN挖掘+聚类一致性损失在图像-only聚类中取得了最佳效果（ARI为0.303，比基线值0.251高出20.6%），优于STEGO和BYOL。SCAN嵌入+llava_mlp多模态融合进一步将ARIs的准确率提高到0.409。自监督精细化的视觉特征对下游融合具有迁移优势。使用SCAN的平均融合效果最佳（+6.5%），而adaln_attention在使用SCAN后性能下降。
BYOL在H&E领域适应中的独特优势：不需要负样本（以避免误判相似层次），对批量大小不敏感，且STAIG验证表明BYOL预训练可以提高特征质量并增强多模态聚类ARIs——它是MIHD中最实用的仅图像增强选项。
验证机器人基准测试有效性的四步渐进框架：（1）数据集规模和覆盖范围；（2）有意义的分类系统（理论基础+经验一致性+实际区分能力）；（3）建立基线性能；（4）能够提升基础模型的数据——缺少任何一步都会导致无法说服审稿人。
PFM任务的依赖性和性能模式：spEMO证实不同任务需要不同的PFM（空间域识别最适合使用GPFM/CHIEF，场景检索最适合使用UNI2）；HEST-1k揭示了PFM性能和模型大小之间的对数关系（Pearson R=0.81）；针对病理的预训练在基因表达预测方面比ImageNet预训练好约8%。
无监督领域适应的优先级：跨FGVC/医学/放射学/显微镜领域的应用；在同一领域内，无监督预训练优于从通用训练中直接转移；特征细化层（如STEGO/SCAN）优于直接的K-means聚类；空间图结构建模是精细领域识别的关键补充。
解决运输/放置阶段空白场景的方案：降低检测器的最小步长阈值（不稳定性从100降低到50等），并使用BC-RNN滚动算法（在后期阶段可实现60-70%的检索率）。VLA的成功率为0%，这意味着后期阶段捕捉自然错误不能依赖预测结果。FailSafe和VLA-SCT是无需训练的插件，可以在M14基线完成后立即用于M15比较实验。
密集对比损失（InfoNCE）在处理大批量数据（n-spots约3600）和低温度（0.07）时容易出现数值溢出问题。需要采用clamp(-10,10) + log_softmax稳定化处理，并且输入特征必须先进行L2归一化。
实际成果 优化大型技术文档的原则：不应压缩“可操作资产”（如检查清单、命令描述、API参考），而应减少“描述性内容”的篇幅。对于重复出现的架构描述、版本更新日志等，也是需要重点优化的部分。Windows SSH中的ControlMaster/ControlPath/ControlPersist在Windows系统上无法使用，因此必须从配置中移除这些设置。
今日要点 核心要点- The performance limits and literature gaps in ARIs in the pure visual domain: Only ARIs for DLPFC images achieved values of 0.11-0.16 (in contrast, multimodal ARIs ranged from 0.45-0.64). This is because the underlying model was trained with cancer tissue data, resulting in subtle morphological differences between layers of the cerebral cortex. Fragment-level independent encoding lacks spatial context. There is almost no literature on PFM embedding → unsupervised KMeans → ARIs in the pure visual domain. MIHD is filling this real literature gap. SCAN self-supervised clustering shows excellent performance in DLPFC and multimodal transfer: Feature k-NN mining + clustering consistency loss achieved the best results in image-only clustering (ARIs were 0.303, which was 20.6% higher than the baseline level of 0.251), outperforming STEGO and BYOL. SCAN embedding + llava_mlp multimodal fusion further improved ARIs to 0.409. Self-supervised refined visual features have a transfer advantage in downstream fusion. Using SCAN for average fusion yielded the greatest gain (+6.5%), while adaln_attention showed a decline in performance after using SCAN. BYOL’s unique advantages in adapting to H&E domain: No need for negative samples (to avoid pushing similar layers too far), insensitive to batch size. STAIG has demonstrated that BYOL pre-training can improve feature quality and enhance multimodal clustering ARIs – this is the most practical image-only enhancement option in MIHD. A four-step progressive framework for verifying the effectiveness of robot benchmarks: (1)
summary: Solution: The only clear report on PFM using only image data was found in the SpConTDS paper. The results of stMMC ablation were cited (ARI improvement rate was 4.6%), to quantify the contribution of images, and HEST-1k was used to provide relative rankings for PFM.
title: Bug Journal 2026-02-19

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-18 Today’s Summary Completed Work: Monitored the intermediate results of the MIHD space transcriptomics benchmark test (coordinate correction verification successful), and established checkpoint management and visualization infrastructure for error recovery benchmarks on Tiansheng. Completion Method: Monitored the progress of backend processes through file system polling, reviewed literature such as HEST-1k to confirm UNI/UNI2 patch standards, managed scattered checkpoints using symbolic links, and wrote new scripts to generate MP4 files under EGL off-screen rendering conditions. Achievements: The coordinate correction effect was quantitatively verified (ARI of uni2 is 86%, the concatenated effect reaches 0.2751, exceeding the PCA baseline); the error recovery benchmark test has complete checkpoint entries and video playback functions, and the feasibility path for PickPlace VLA fine-tuning was clarified (MimicGen→LeRobot→Pi0.5 LoRA). This work was carried out in parallel on two HPC servers: DCC and Tiansheng. The MIHD multimodal fusion benchmark test with corrected coordinates was monitored, and UNI/UNI2 literature standards were studied. On Tiansheng, the unified checkpoint management system for error recovery benchmarks was established, visualization scripts were developed, and the training path for PickPlace VLA was planned.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Verify MIHD coordinate correction effect — The ARI/NMI of completed fusion strategies were extracted from experiment_comparison.csv: The optimal ARI for the concatenated strategy is 0.2751 (exceeding the PCA baseline of only 0.2613), while the improvement of uni2 alone ranges from 0.065 to 0.121 (+86%). All fusion strategies showed significant improvements. 🔄 Research and planning for PickPlace VLA training data — It was confirmed that there are only 10 source demonstration data, and no PickPlace data exists in zhaoganlong; the plan is to expand the MimicGen pipeline from 10 to 500 demonstration data → LeRobot format conversion → Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning. The specific implementation remains pending. ✅ UNI/UNI2 literature research — Searching for UNI/UNI2 patch extraction standards: Input size is 224×224px, patch size = 14, ImageNet normalization; HEST-1k uses a region of 112µm×112µm with a resolution of 0.5µm/px = 224×224px, using non-overlapping patches. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Visualization policy deployment script — Created visualize_policy_rollout.py (about 200 lines of code), supporting four strategies: random/bc_rnn/vla_pi0/vla_pi05. Through EGL off-screen rendering and HUD overlay, MP4 files with success/failure markers were output; it was confirmed that the random strategy could generate suitable videos. ✅ VLA model and checkpoint inventory — The code supports four types of VLA (pi0/pi05/phoenix/flare). Actually available checkpoints include pi0_libero/pi0_base/pi0_fast_base/pi05_base (about 11-12GB each, using JAX OCDB format); phoenix/flare has no checkpoints; robomimic only has the Lift task (can/square/transport tasks are empty). ✅ Unified checkpoint management — Created a checkpoints/ directory for error recovery benchmarks, using symbolic links to manage the model weights of bc_rnn/vla/robomimic. In benchmark_v4.yaml, three absolute paths were updated to symbolic link paths. 🔄 MIHD benchmark test process monitoring — Monitored the core_multimodal_fast experiment (pca×uni2×8 fusion type×11 parts=88 experiments). Each part took about 1 hour in the basic_contrastive stage, and this process continued after the session ended. Key Issues 1. Pi0/LIBERO checkpoints completely failed in PickPlace tasks (zero success rate), VLA cannot be generalized across tasks. Solution: Plan to use MimicGen to expand the source demonstration data from 10 to 500, and then fine-tune the dedicated model using Pi0.5 LoRA. Key Insight: In the robosuite environment, VLA needs to be benchmarked against weights for specific tasks; generic pre-trained weights cannot be generalized across tasks.
2. The can/square/transport task directory in robomimic_checkpoints is empty, missing key benchmark checkpoints. Solution: Need to retrain or download the pre-trained weights for the corresponding task. Currently, only the Lift task can be used. Key Insight: Although the checkpoint directory structure was planned, actual model files were missing, affecting the comprehensive evaluation of multi-task benchmarks.
3. Basic contrastive training is very slow, each part takes about 1 hour, resulting in a total time of over 10 hours for 88 experiments. Solution: Wait for the background process to complete and use this time to handle other tasks on Tiansheng. Key Insight: The computational cost of conditional output buffering is much higher than that of simple fusion strategies (concat/mean), which becomes a bottleneck in benchmark test runtime.
4. Conditional output buffering mechanism prevents real-time monitoring of the process progress. Solution: Use file system polling (find -mmin) to monitor the modification time of intermediate files, indirectly determining the process progress. Key Insight: By observing the addition of new content in the output file, it is possible to bypass the buffer of the conda run. This actually monitors the completion status at the part level, rather than real-time progress.
Human Thinking vs. Artificial Intelligence Thinking Strategic Level Does zhaoganlong have a dedicated PickPlace checkpoint? Role Idea Human The human questioned AI about using pi0_libero and thought that there should be a dedicated checkpoint in the zhaoganlong folder. AI Based on previous investigations, AI concluded that the checkpoint had been deleted, so it provided weights for the LIBERO task as an alternative solution. Difference Analysis: Humans posed a more direct question to AI, while AI gave a clear answer but did not consider all possibilities with humility.
Solution to PickPlace VLA training Role Idea Human The human actively asked whether training could be carried out in this case and clarified the solution by asking about MimicGen data. AI AI pointed out that pi0_libero had a success rate of 0% in PickPlace, but did not propose a training solution. Difference Analysis: Humans were more goal-oriented, directly pointing out feasible solutions; while AI tended to show the current state rather than suggesting next steps.
Initiative in UNI/UNI2 literature research Role Idea Human The human actively searched for literature on how to use UNI/UNI2 and sought practices from community standards to verify correctness. AI AI performed the search and summarized the results, but this initiative was proposed by humans. Difference Analysis: Humans followed scientific standards to ensure consistency of methods; AI did not perform literature verification. Implementation Level Choice of exploration method: Exploring sub-agents or direct commands Role Thinking Method Human The human would interrupt AI’s operations to start exploring sub-agents, preferring a more direct and controllable approach. AI AI usually uses sub-agents for extensive searches, considering it to be more thorough. Difference Analysis: Humans focused on speed and controllability, unwilling to wait for sub-agents to run for a long time; while AI valued thoroughness, with different trade-offs in efficiency.
Limitations of Artificial Intelligence Main Limitations Over-emphasizing the certainty that “checkpoints have been deleted” without conducting a comprehensive search of the file system. This exposed an issue of overconfidence when dealing with uncertainty. In response to the question “What is the success rate of Pi0 in PickPlace being 0%?”, AI only presented the status, without providing solutions, requiring user guidance for data research. General Limitations Restrictions of web searching (some URLs return 303/ECONNREFUSED), making it impossible to access the HEST code library and full articles from “Nature Methods”. Additionally, detailed information on patch extraction codes was not obtained. Inefficient pipeline monitoring strategies: Multiple 30-minute waiting periods, only changing the strategy after timeout occurs, lacking the ability to dynamically adjust waiting intervals based on progress. Today’s Summary Main Conclusions Coordinate Correction Effect: Concatenated fusion achieved an ARI of 0.2751, exceeding the PCA baseline of 0.2613. This indicates that the corrected visual features effectively supplemented gene expression information. The improvement in uni2 alone increased from 0.065 to 0.121 (+86%), confirming that before correction, patch errors were the main performance bottleneck. UNI/UNI2 Patch Extraction Standards: Input size is 224×224 pixels, patch size = 14, ImageNet normalization; HEST-1k uses a region of 112µm×112µm with a resolution of 0.5µm/px = 224×224 pixels. These are standard practices in the field of space transcriptomics. Robosuite Environment: VLA failed to achieve generalization across tasks (pi0_libero had a success rate of 0% in PickPlace), requiring fine-tuning for specific tasks. The MimicGen pipeline was tested on zhaoganlong (10 source demonstrations → 500 generated demonstrations → LeRobot format → LoRA fine-tuning), which is a proven feasible path. Actual Summary STAIG-style contrastive learning is the computational bottleneck in multimodal fusion benchmarks, with each part taking about 1 hour. This should be considered when designing benchmarks; possibly requiring separate grouping or increasing computing resources. Best practices for managing checkpoints in error recovery benchmarks: Use symbolic link directories to manage model weights across multiple paths. The configuration file only references symbolic link paths, facilitating migration and maintenance. Meeting Summary MIHD Space Transcriptomics 🔄 Monitor the progress of multimodal fusion benchmarks and study UNI/UNI2 patch extraction standards 00:01:02.520 | claude_code The meeting continued while waiting for the core_multimodal_fast pipeline to run. Through file system polling, it was confirmed that 6/11 parts were completed in the basic_contrastive stage. Intermediate results were used to verify the coordinate correction effect: Concatenation achieved the optimal ARI of 0.2751, and uni2 alone improved from 0.065 to 0.121 (+86%). Users actively sought standards for UNI/UNI2, and AI searched and organized materials such as HuggingFace UNI2-h, HEST-1k, SPADE, etc., confirming that the input should be 224×224 pixels, ImageNet normalization, and a patch region of 112µm×112µm. The pipeline was still running in the background at the end of the meeting.
Error Recovery Benchmarks 🔄 Unified checkpoint management, visualization script development, Makefile updates, and planning for PickPlace training data 00:26:49.979 | claude_code Four infrastructure tasks were completed: symbolic link management of bc_rnn/vla/robomimic checkpoints, creation of visualize_policy_rollout.py (supporting random/BC-RNN/VLA, EGL rendering output MP4, verified), updates to the paths and Makefile visualization targets in benchmark_v4.yaml, and updating the project summary document. Subsequently, an investigation was conducted on PickPlace training data, finding that there were only 10 source demonstrations. No PickPlace data existed in zhaoganlong, and a pipeline of MimicGen expansion → LeRobot → Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning was planned. Users decided to pause until further instructions were given.
🔄 Comprehensive inventory of checkpoints and confirmation of VLA release conditions, discussion on PickPlace fine-tuning path 05:32:29.978 | claude_code The system listed all checkpoints: bc_rnn (epoch50-600, 12 files), robomimic (only Lift, can/square/transport empty), VLA (4 large models, 11-12GB). It was confirmed that VLA could be released, but two terminals were needed to start the server and client separately. AI pointed out that pi0_libero had a success rate of 0% in PickPlace, and users questioned whether zhaoganlong had a dedicated checkpoint. AI admitted that the investigation might not have been thorough enough, so users turned to the MimicGen training solution, confirming that there were 10 source demonstrations, and agreed to plan an expansion training with 500 demonstrations.
✅ Complete inventory of VLA models 04:52:08.850 | claude_code The Explore sub-agent was used to fully inventory project VLA resources: The code supported four models (pi0/pi05/phoenix/flare). Actually available checkpoints included pi0_libero/pi0_base/pi0_fast_base/pi05_base (about 11-12GB each, using JAX format), while phoenix and flare had no checkpoints. Currently, the direct combination used was Pi0 LIBERO (port 5555) and Pi0.5 Base (port 5557); bc_rnn had 12 epochs of files available.
❌ VLA model list interrupted 04:51:03.383 | claude_code Users requested a list of available VLA models, but the meeting was immediately interrupted without any substantial results.
